findings.case: "Bourgeois left-nationalism" critique in the Canadian Native-question movement
findings.evidence: Canada has been built from the beginning on the enslavement of the Native fatherland for the ends of the growing bourgeoisie.
findings.source: /media/user/data/old-hdd/old-hdd/prolewiki/Exports/Library/Library_Nation or Genocide_ The Struggle of the Native People Against Canadian and American Imperialism.txt
findings.implication: Distinguishes legitimate national-liberation analysis (oppressed-nation solidarity) from "bourgeois left-nationalism" (defending one's own state under left cover) — gives the PatSoc Pipeline a concrete marker: drift begins when defend-the-fatherland language displaces class/imperialism analysis.

findings.case: 'Group of German Communist Army Officers' (KPD-linked), 1923 Ruhr crisis -- Spenglerian national bolshevism adopted mid-crisis
findings.evidence: This group even defined the council system as "a Prussian notion based on concepts of an elite, solidarity and mutual responsibility".
findings.implication: A named, real KPD-linked faction adopted Spengler's 'Prussian socialism' wholesale during a nationalist crisis (the French Ruhr occupation) -- the canonical historical instance of the PatSoc Pipeline's trigger condition (national humiliation) and terminal content (class categories reread as elite/Prussian ones).

findings.case: 1981 Modern Times debate on the Hawaii national question (J.D. responding to John Reinecke)
findings.evidence: between a) those of all races who see Hawaii sharply distinguished from mainland USA and b) those who identify themselves as native Hawaiians and see nationhood in strictly ethnic terms
findings.implication: A real Left debate splits national-question sentiment into a civic/territorial axis ('Island nationalism', closer to anti-imperialist self-determination) versus an ethnic/blood axis ('Native nationalism'). The PatSoc drift pipeline may need this second axis: NATIONALISM read as civic-anti-imperialist is not yet the same vector as NATIONALISM read as ethnic, and the latter is what should feed toward Strasserism.

findings.case: ATM's critique of the October League's evasive position on Chicano self-determination (regional autonomy vs. reformist demands)
findings.evidence: an oppressed Chicano nation was forged in the Southwest, a nation with the right to self-determination
findings.implication: Shows the national-question axis is graded, not binary, in real line-struggles: orgs occupy positions from full self-determination/land-confiscation demands down to reformist 'drugs out of the barrio' — useful for making the CLASS_ANALYSIS/NATIONALISM tag a continuous rather than discrete value.

findings.case: African Blood Brotherhood Program's formal ally/enemy taxonomy, 1922
findings.evidence: The class-conscious white workers who have spoken out in favor of African liberation and have a willingness to back with action their expressed sentiments, must also be considered as actual allies
findings.implication: The ABB codified a graded alliance ladder (actual allies / potential allies whose class-consciousness must be 'awakened' / enemies) rather than a binary friend-enemy split -- a real precedent for a doctrine tag vector that computes faction relations continuously rather than as fixed camps.

findings.case: African Blood Brotherhood Program's warning against race-first economic nationalism (Black-owned enterprise) as a liberation substitute, 1922
findings.evidence: sudden financial collapse of such enterprises may break the whole morale of the Liberation Movement.
findings.implication: Even within a nationalism-inflected program, there is an explicit, contemporaneous internal critique flagging ethnic-capitalist substitution for class organizing as a morale-destroying trap -- useful as an early historical marker on the PatSoc drift gradient, distinct from the ABB's own class-conscious mainline.

findings.case: African Blood Brotherhood Program, 1922 -- dual open/secret organizational structure
findings.evidence: Such a movement could be carried on openly in the North, but would have to be built up secretly in the South in order to protect those members living in the South
findings.implication: A real historical organization that fused explicit class analysis (chattel-vs-wage-slavery framing, Third International alignment) with race-first liberation goals in one program, plus a base-area design (open Federation in the North, secret 'Protective organization' in the South) modeled explicitly on Sinn Fein -- concrete grounding for a doctrine node that carries both CLASS_ANALYSIS and NATIONALISM tags simultaneously, not as contradiction but as one real tag vector.

findings.case: African Blood Brotherhood vs. Garvey's UNIA defection wave, per C. Lorenzo, The Toiler, Dec. 1921
findings.evidence: several thousand members have left the UNIA, and that organization now faces a severe international crisis
findings.implication: A real, numbered cross-organizational defection flow from a race-only/bourgeois nationalist pole (Garvey's UNIA) toward a class-and-race synthesis pole (ABB's 'Workers' Republic' framing) — a concrete historical defection-fraction to calibrate how strongly a CLASS_ANALYSIS doctrine node should pull members away from a pure-NATIONALISM rival organization.
findings.parameter: 'Several thousand members' defected from the UNIA toward the ABB pole, c. 1921.

findings.case: Baku Congress of the Peoples of the East (1920) — negotiating CLASS_ANALYSIS vs national/religious solidarity
findings.evidence: the Highland poor, and even the ulemas, issued a call for a ghazavat, a holy war, against all the oppressors of the East
findings.implication: Unlike the Hamburg case, this is the Comintern itself sanctioning a fusion of religious/national mobilizing language ('holy war') with class-revolutionary aims in a colonial context, without being coded as a PatSoc drift. This is the calibration case: the tag-vector weighting of NATIONALISM should not auto-trigger the PatSoc pipeline in national-liberation contexts — the distinguishing factor is whether CLASS_ANALYSIS is retained as load-bearing alongside it (as here) versus displaced by it (as in Hamburg).

findings.case: Baku Congress of the Peoples of the East, September 1920 — Radek's appeal for a 'holy war' invoking historical conquerors
findings.evidence: We appeal, comrades, to the warlike feelings which once inspired the peoples of the East when these peoples, led by their great conquerors, advanced upon Europe
findings.implication: Even orthodox Bolshevik agitators reached for civilizational/martial-glory framing rather than pure class analysis when mobilizing a national audience — historical proof that the earliest steps of the PatSoc drift chain are indistinguishable from legitimate anti-imperialist solidarity rhetoric, which is exactly why the pipeline needs to be zero-cost and easy to enter unnoticed.

findings.case: Baku Congress of the Peoples of the East, September 1920 — delegate composition
findings.evidence: Communists, 1,071; Communist Party sympathisers, 334; Young Communists, 31; non-party, 467
findings.implication: At the very founding of the Comintern's colonial-world outreach, over half of ~2,050 delegates were non-Communist or unaffiliated nationalists brought in on a national-liberation basis rather than class basis — a quantified, real ratio for how CLASS_ANALYSIS vs NATIONALISM coexist/compete inside a single united-front body from day one.
findings.parameter: Baku 1920: of 1,926 classified delegates, 1,071 Communist (55.6%), 855 non-Communist/sympathiser/unaffiliated (44.4%)

findings.case: Burstein-Loren exchange on the national question's universal-vs-particular status, CPML 'New Voice' debate, 1980
findings.evidence: the struggle of oppressed nations for self-determination and against imperialism is at once a part of the universality of Marxism and also a question of U.S. particularities
findings.implication: Shows the CLASS_ANALYSIS-vs-NATIONALISM tension recurring even among nominal allies within one 'trend,' with no stable resolution offered — supports keeping the national-question tag perpetually contestable/load-bearing rather than resolvable by a single doctrine purchase.

findings.case: CCP's 1963 accusation that Soviet aid to Nehru's India served Soviet great-power interest under internationalist language
findings.evidence: they often take an attitude of great-power chauvinism and national egoism in matters concerning aid to newly independent countries
findings.implication: A concrete historical accusation-pattern for the PatSoc Pipeline: aid/solidarity framed in internationalist vocabulary while materially serving the donor's own national/great-power interest. Drift should be measured by who benefits materially, not by which words are used.

findings.case: CLP critique of the 'LARK' cultural-nationalist position on Boston busing, 1976
findings.evidence: What lies at the basis of this entire analysis is the application of the 'Third World' concept to the USNA.
findings.implication: A concrete mass-work case diagnosing a nationalism-inflected class analysis (one national section cast as sole vanguard, another as mere auxiliary) as objectively Trotskyist-adjacent — real-world grounding for the tag-vector's load-bearing CLASS_ANALYSIS-vs-NATIONALISM axis.

findings.case: COUSML's 1977 polemic against the October League's adoption of Mao-derived 'Three Worlds Theory'
findings.evidence: Give up the proletarian revolution in the United States and unite with the U.S. imperialist bourgeoisie against its rival Soviet social-imperialism under the hoax of directing the 'main blow' at the Soviet Union
findings.implication: This is the closest real-world analog to the PatSoc Pipeline's Strasserite endpoint: a doctrine that begins as anti-revisionist/anti-superpower theory ends, mechanically, in an alliance with one's own imperialist bourgeoisie against a rival power — 'the fascist who thought he was a communist' in period-accurate form.

findings.case: CPUSA 2036 entryist takeover strategy
findings.evidence: the Infrared community has popularized the Twitter hashtag and internet meme CPUSA 2036 which describes their aim to take control of the CPUSA
findings.implication: Shows PatSoc drift targeting capture of an existing legitimate vanguard-party node (entryism) rather than founding a new fringe org — a distinct DT event type where a drift faction tries to flip an org's tag vector from inside rather than split off.

findings.case: Class-background composition of Chinese college students reported by Mao, January 1957
findings.evidence: students from working-class and poor and lower-middle peasant families account for less than 20 per cent
findings.implication: A concrete, dated ratio for how far the cadre-recruitment pool can drift from its proletarian base before it becomes a named ideological-trend problem — usable as a warning threshold for a CLASS_ANALYSIS-composition metric feeding the PatSoc drift chain.
findings.parameter: CLASS_COMPOSITION_DRIFT_THRESHOLD = 0.20 (worker/poor-peasant-family fraction of the student/cadre pipeline flagged as a problem when below this)

findings.case: Committee for Defense of Federico Cintron Fiallo — 'Divided Nation' line fight over Puerto Rico, mid-1970s
findings.evidence: El Comite-MINP whipped up the revisionist theory of the 'Divided Nation' claiming that the CDFCF should be under the complete leadership of the committee in Puerto Rico.
findings.implication: Documents OL capitulating to a nationality-first 'Divided Nation' line under threat of losing a coalition partner's sponsorship — a real case of a national-question line sliding toward NATIONALISM under coalition/legitimacy pressure rather than conviction, a mechanism worth modeling as an external-pressure drift trigger.

findings.case: Communist Manifesto's own taxonomy of reactionary pseudo-socialisms as pre-existing doctrine-drift nodes
findings.evidence: Feudal Socialism: half lamentation, half lampoon, half echo of the past, half menace of the future... What they upbraid the bourgeoisie with is not so much that it creates a proletariat, as that it creates a revolutionary proletariat.
findings.implication: Marx and Engels already mapped a taxonomy (Feudal/Petit-Bourgeois/'German-True' Socialism) of ideologies that borrow socialist language while defending pre-capitalist or petit-bourgeois property relations -- this taxonomy can seed the PatSoc Pipeline's intermediate node names/flavors rather than inventing new ones.

findings.case: Cyril Briggs, 'The American Race Problem,' pre-Bolshevik ABB-founding-era territorial nationalism, The Crusader, 1918
findings.evidence: This new solution is nothing more or less than independent, separate existence.
findings.implication: A race-first, territorial-sovereignty solution with no class analysis or worker-solidarity framing — the historical 'before' anchor on the NATIONALISM pole, useful for measuring how far the same author/organization (ABB) drifted toward CLASS_ANALYSIS just three years later once Comintern-aligned.

findings.case: German Workers Party (DAP) Vienna programme, August 1918 — direct NSDAP precursor
findings.evidence: disintegrating finance capital and the highly desirable productive national capital
findings.implication: This is the founding move of the PatSoc Pipeline: a proto-fascist party legitimizes itself with pseudo-anti-capitalist rhetoric by splitting capital into 'good/national' vs 'bad/parasitic-Jewish' rather than by class analysis. Model the drift-chain's first zero-cost node as exactly this substitution (capital-fraction framing replaces class framing) rather than an overt nationalism claim — it reads as economically radical while being class-blind.

findings.case: Hamburg 'National Bolshevism' (Wolffheim/Laufenberg), 1919-1921 — the historical PatSoc drift chain
findings.evidence: The councils system groups together all the workers ... behind the class interests which are the interests of socialism and the nation. The factory councils will become an element of national unity
findings.implication: This is the real-world PatSoc pipeline: the drift from class-council language to national-unity language happened through a smooth rhetorical bridge (councils as vehicles of BOTH class AND nation), not a sudden jump — confirming the pipeline should be a zero-cost DRIFT through shared vocabulary, not a discrete ideological break. Wolffheim later 'died in a concentration camp,' i.e. the pipeline's endpoint offered no safe harbor even in fascist terms — Strasserism was a dead end for its own adherents.
findings.parameter: Initial support 'several thousand Hamburg workers' vs 'a few hundred' for orthodox KPD locally — a concrete base-size number for the PatSoc current's early appeal before its 1920-21 collapse.

findings.case: Hoxhaist essay's explicit primary-source rejection of National Bolshevism/Patriotic Socialism
findings.evidence: We are fully opposed to 'National Communism', 'Patriotic Socialism', 'National Bolshevism', and other reactionary and revisionist trends
findings.implication: This is a contemporary ML current explicitly drawing the exact boundary line the PatSoc Pipeline is designed to cross — naming National Bolshevism/Patriotic Socialism as a discrete, nameable deviation category (not just 'bad nationalism' in general) validates giving the pipeline named, recognizable nodes rather than a vague drift meter.

findings.case: Huey Long's 'Share Our Wealth' / 'Every Man a King' populist program (Depression-era Louisiana)
findings.evidence: Although sometimes called socialist by patriotic socialists, the movement was very much not and did not seek an end to capitalism.
findings.implication: This is the real-world zero-cost entry node of the PatSoc pipeline: producerist redistribution rhetoric ('every man a king') that is retroactively claimed by patriotic-socialist currents specifically because it never required a class analysis, only a grievance against 'the rich.' Confirms the pipeline's first node should require zero TL investment and zero explicit CLASS_ANALYSIS tag to unlock.

findings.case: KPD's 'Red Referendum' (Roter Volksentscheid) — bloc with NSDAP/DNVP to dissolve the SPD-led Prussian Landtag, August 1931
findings.evidence: nine million voted for the Nazi – monarchist – Stalinist referendum – 37 per cent of the Prussian electoral roll
findings.implication: A documented real-world faction-flip: an orthodox Communist party tactically allied with fascists against reformists under 'united front from below' doctrine, and the bloc still failed to reach the threshold needed to act — good precedent for the PatSoc trap's participation-cost/failure-threshold math.
findings.parameter: Referendum turnout ~9,000,000 votes = 37% of the Prussian electoral roll — fell short of the majority needed to dissolve parliament; usable as a 'faction-flip participation fraction' and its failure threshold.

findings.case: KPD-Nazi fraternization meetings during the 1923 Ruhr crisis
findings.evidence: The KPD would even hold meetings with the Nazis. During one of these meetings, a Nazi orator rendered homage to the communists, but ironically advised them to rid themselves of the Jews.
findings.implication: Documents the literal historical event the PatSoc Pipeline dramatizes -- a communist party's national-bolshevist drift produced direct fraternization with fascists; the drift required no new theory, only nationalist reframing of existing anti-imperialist rhetoric, supporting a low/zero theoretical-labor cost for the pipeline.

findings.case: Lenin's Theses on the National and Colonial Question, Second Congress, 1920 — oppressed/oppressor nation ratio
findings.evidence: some 70 per cent of the world population belong to the oppressed nations which are either in direct colonial dependence, or appear as semi-colonial states
findings.implication: A period-accurate quantitative anchor for the oppressed/oppressor nation split underlying the entire national-question weighting in doctrine tree design — grounds the CLASS_ANALYSIS/NATIONALISM tension in a real demographic ratio rather than an arbitrary constant.
findings.parameter: ~70% of world population (~1.25 of an estimated 1.75 billion, 1920) classified as belonging to oppressed/colonial-dependent nations.

findings.case: Lenin, Speech on the Place of the Bund in the R.S.D.L.P., 2nd Congress, July 20 1903
findings.evidence: Federation is harmful because it sanctions segregation and alienation, elevates them to a principle, to a law.
findings.implication: The Bund's demand for federal (national-organizational) autonomy versus Lenin's insistence on unitary centralism is the historical prototype for a national-question drift node: a real doctrine fight where 'organize along national/communal lines' was rejected in principle as incompatible with class-based centralism, i.e., exactly the fork the PatSoc pipeline should punish structurally.

findings.case: Luther-to-Feder continuity of anti-usury 'socialism' -- the PatSoc attractor recurs across four centuries
findings.evidence: while the forms of anti-usury in 1530 and 1920 have much in common, their content differs qualitatively.
findings.implication: The 'blame finance capital, spare industrial capital' move recurred across ~400 years independent of any specific theoretical investment -- supports a near-zero cost for the PatSoc Pipeline, since it recycles a standing folk-economic reflex rather than requiring purchased doctrine.
findings.parameter: ~390 years (1530 to 1920) of recurrence of the same ideological attractor -- evidence for near-zero theoretical-labor cost design of the pipeline.

findings.case: Lyndon LaRouche's drift from Trotskyist SWP membership through cult leadership (NCLC) to informal CIA contact, USA, 1940s-1970s
findings.evidence: LaRouche willingly surrendered information to the CIA at various points in time, despite also believing his self-proclaimed communist activities put him at risk with intelligence agencies.
findings.implication: A real, documented zero-cost-looking drift chain (Marxist -> Trotskyist SWP -> self-declared 'intellectual vanguard' cult -> support for a segregationist counter-strike at Ocean Hill-Brownsville 1968-69 -> informal state-security collaboration) matching the PatSoc Pipeline's shape: each step looks like a small refinement, but the path ends with the actor cooperating with the state he claims to oppose.

findings.case: MAGACommunism / Strasserism convergence, 2022
findings.evidence: MAGA Communism is comparable to Strasserism given their mutual opposition to finance capital and "globalism" as well as their pro-rural, pro-small business, ultranationalist attitude
findings.implication: A live, dated (Aug 2022) contemporary case of the PatSoc Pipeline's terminal Strasserism node — anti-'globalist' rhetoric fully substituting for class analysis confirms NATIONALISM vs. CLASS_ANALYSIS as an observable, currently-occurring real-world drift axis, not just a theoretical one.

findings.case: National Bolshevism as a twice-occurring named historical current: interwar Germany (Niekisch, Paetel) and post-Soviet Russia (Limonov, Dugin)
findings.evidence: is a neo-fascist ideology that defends a social-democratic welfare state while being conservative, nationalist, and anti-communist
findings.implication: Confirms the PatSoc Pipeline's terminal Strasserism/Nazbol node has a real, independently-recurring historical referent rather than being invented - both iterations combine 'socialist' welfare rhetoric with nationalism and anti-communism, the exact tag-vector inversion (CLASS_ANALYSIS displaced by NATIONALISM) the mechanic needs to model.

findings.case: Otto Strasser's 1930 break with Hitler and the Stennes SA revolt of 1931 (Black Front / 'National Bolshevik' dissidents)
findings.evidence: the political leadership of the NSDAP in Munich has strayed from the idea of revolutionary National Socialism... The revolutionary force of the SA has been saturated with bourgeois liberal tendencies
findings.implication: A mirror-image PatSoc case: dissidents inside a fascist movement accused their own leadership of betraying 'revolutionary' socialism by courting big capital — the exact rhetorical shape the design wants for a drift chain's internal self-justification, just running the opposite direction (fascist-to-'left' framing).
findings.parameter: Hitler's SA-discipline decree issued 28 March 1931; Stennes rebelled and was expelled within about one week (early April 1931) — a days-to-one-week timescale for how fast a leadership purges an internal ideological deviation once it becomes an open revolt.

findings.case: PRRWO's repudiated 'divided nation' (Browder-line) thesis on Puerto Ricans in the U.S.
findings.evidence: Puerto Ricans in the U.S. were not part of a divided nation, but part of the multi-national proletariat
findings.implication: A concrete historical instance of an org holding — then studying its way OUT of — a national-question error that had already produced concrete bad policy (a Puerto Rico branch, self-declaring as 'the Party'). Shows the CLASS_ANALYSIS/NATIONALISM axis is reversible with sustained TL investment, not just a one-way ratchet toward the trap.

findings.case: PRRWO's repudiation of its own 'divided nation theory' (Young Lords, Dec 1970) at its July 1972 Congress
findings.evidence: we saw that in fact, Puerto Ricans in the U.S. were not part of a divided nation, but part of the multi-national proletariat
findings.implication: A CLASS_ANALYSIS-vs-NATIONALISM drift is reversible, but historically required both intensive theoretical study (Stalin's national-question texts) AND an organizational crisis (a split) to force it -- the trunk_shift/rectification mechanic should gate correction on crisis-plus-TL-investment, not TL alone.
findings.parameter: ~19-month cycle from nationalist line adoption (Dec 1970) to formal repudiation at Congress (July 1972); the precipitating split occurred in May 1972, 2 months before that Congress

findings.case: Paul Lensch's 'revolutionary Germany vs reactionary England' war-socialism, 1916
findings.evidence: the rise of this class is taking place amid the thunder of a revolutionary world war, but without the lightning of a revolutionary civil war
findings.implication: A named SPD right-winger reframing an imperialist war as inherently revolutionary/proletarian because it was fought by 'Germany' — the precise rhetorical move the PatSoc Pipeline should model: retaining socialist vocabulary while substituting nation for class as the historical subject.

findings.case: Polish Social-Democrats (S.D.K.P. and L.) propose 'cultural-national autonomy' at the 2nd RSDLP Congress, July 1903
findings.evidence: instead of self-determination they practically proposed the notorious 'cultural-national autonomy', only under another name
findings.implication: Lenin's specific complaint is that a formally-internationalist faction smuggled in a nationalist demand under euphemism ('under another name') -- exactly the disguised-drift pattern the PatSoc Pipeline should model: doctrine nodes that look like class-analysis positions but function as national-particularist ones.

findings.case: ProleWiki's patsoc purge and the founding of InfraWiki, Aug-Oct 2022
findings.evidence: In October 2022, following ProleWiki's purge of patsocs, the Infrared community started InfraWiki to promote MAGA communism and patriotic socialism.
findings.implication: Gives a dated latency for the PatSoc drift chain from public ideological hardening to organizational split/exit: roughly two months (slogan surfaces Aug 2022, expulsion-triggered split Oct 2022) — candidate tick-length for the PatSoc Pipeline's final flip once tags cross a threshold.
findings.parameter: ~2 months from public patsoc drift crystallizing to formal organizational split/exit

findings.case: Proletarian Unity League downplaying international line, splitting the NCM into two 'wings,' 1977
findings.evidence: this question has, in life, effectively divided the communist movement into a class-collaborationist wing and an internationalist wing
findings.implication: Shows a single doctrine-tag divergence (stance on Soviet class character / Angola solidarity) bifurcating an entire movement into two durable trunks — evidence that trunk-forks can crystallize around one line question rather than a broad platform.

findings.case: SPD 'national socialism' doctrine, pre-1914 right wing (von Vollmar, Haenisch)
findings.evidence: not only the establishment of a socialist government but even the building of a fully-developed socialist society could be carried out within the confines of a single nation state
findings.implication: This is the historical template for the CLASS_ANALYSIS→NATIONALISM axis predating 'socialism in one country' by decades (Blick notes von Vollmar reached this position '46 years before Stalin') — grounds a doctrine node ('socialism-in-one-nation') as a real, independently-arising attractor state, not a one-off Stalinist aberration.
findings.parameter: SPD Reichstag fraction voting for war credits, 4 Aug 1914: 110-strong delegation, unanimous vote.

findings.case: Same speech — Lenin's aspirational coherence claim for the centralist majority
findings.evidence: we shall be prouder still if our whole Party proves to be a compact, a highly compact, 90 per cent, majority
findings.implication: Gives a concrete (if rhetorical) numeric target for ideological coherence around a rejected-federation/centralist line — usable as a candidate ceiling for a 'trunk convergence' meter representing how unified a faction claims to be after a national-question fork is resolved one way.
findings.parameter: ~90% claimed coherence target for the centralist line after rejecting Bund federalism (2nd RSDLP Congress, 1903) — candidate ceiling for a post-fork trunk-convergence stat

findings.case: Seize the Time's documented revision of its 'principal contradiction' line after the Wounded Knee uprising, 1976
findings.evidence: Between 1973-76 we held that the principal contradiction was between the bourgeoisie and oppressed nationalities
findings.implication: Real organizations explicitly revised their CLASS_ANALYSIS/NATIONALISM tag-weighting mid-course based on a 'conjunctural' reanalysis of repression outcomes -- direct precedent for a theoretical_offensive mechanic that reweights doctrine tags rather than treating them as fixed at founding.
findings.parameter: ~3-year interval (1973-1976) before this org's own foundational doctrine line was formally revised

findings.case: Silber vs. Weather Underground/'white-skin-privilege' separatist-organizing thesis, 1976
findings.evidence: no matter how militant it may seem around issues of the moment and in its expressions of hatred for white supremacy, bourgeois nationalism will betray the real interests of the Black and Latin toiling masses
findings.implication: A direct period articulation of the CLASS_ANALYSIS vs. NATIONALISM load-bearing axis: militant-sounding separatist/nationalist organizing is diagnosed, in real time, as a drift vector that ultimately serves the ruling class — real-world precedent for the PatSoc pipeline's core mechanic.

findings.case: Silber's warning that separatist/national-specific party forms invert their own stated anti-racist intent
findings.evidence: the view, therefore, that advocates separate national party forms — while advanced in the name of the struggle against national chauvinism — actually liquidates the struggle against national chauvinism
findings.implication: A trap that presents as its opposite: a doctrine branded anti-chauvinist can mechanically produce the chauvinist/liquidationist outcome. The PatSoc pipeline's early nodes should be able to look virtuous (anti-racism, self-determination) while already accruing drift weight.

findings.case: Sino-Soviet polemic 'Peaceful Coexistence — Two Diametrically Opposed Policies', December 1963
findings.evidence: Isn't this arrogant nonsense of yours an expression of great-power chauvinism and power politics pure and simple?
findings.implication: Shows two factions using the identical vocabulary ('peaceful coexistence') to mean opposite things — a Reformist/great-power-chauvinist reading vs. a Scientific/proletarian-internationalist reading of the same term — exactly the load-bearing CLASS_ANALYSIS-vs-NATIONALISM ambiguity the doctrine tree's tag vector needs to be able to represent as the same surface doctrine diverging by trunk.

findings.case: Sino-Soviet polemic characterizing Khrushchov's foreign-policy line as a betrayal of internationalism, 1963
findings.evidence: Khrushchov's peaceful coexistence seeks to replace the proletarian world revolution with pacifism and thus renounces proletarian internationalism.
findings.implication: Documents the mechanism by which a ruling party's doctrine drifts from internationalism toward great-power national interest under the same label — a real historical case of exactly the CLASS_ANALYSIS-to-NATIONALISM slide the PatSoc pipeline should model, driven by state consolidation rather than any single dramatic betrayal.

findings.case: Spengler's 'Prussian Socialism' as the doctrinal content of the PatSoc/Strasserist pole
findings.evidence: Not Marx's theory, but Frederick William's Prussian practice which long preceded Marx and will yet displace him... trains the individual in his duty to the whole.
findings.implication: Gives the actual doctrinal content of the historical PatSoc pole: hierarchy/duty/blood displacing class analysis while retaining 'socialism' vocabulary -- usable as the literal tag-vector definition of the pipeline's terminal Strasserism node (high NATIONALISM, near-zero CLASS_ANALYSIS, property relations left intact).

findings.case: Stalin's 'Marxism and the National Question' (Bund precedent) invoked against separate national parties, 1976
findings.evidence: Experience has shown that the organization of the proletariat of a given state on national lines tends only to destroy the idea of class solidarity.
findings.implication: The canonical doctrinal anchor that the PatSoc drift chain must erode node-by-node; illustrates that doctrine nodes act as long-lived constitutional constraints (a ~60-70 year-old ruling still treated as binding).
findings.parameter: Cited as a 'judgment made 60 years ago' at time of writing (1976) — a ~70-year doctrinal half-life.

findings.case: Stalin's 1925 warning against Trotskyite/Zinovievite 'liquidationism', quoted approvingly by the CCP against the CPSU's 1963 line
findings.evidence: That is the path of nationalism and degeneration, the path of the complete liquidation of the proletariat's international policy
findings.implication: A near-verbatim historical precedent for the game's own 'Liquidationism' trap name — internationalism-abandoned-for-national-interest is explicitly labeled 'nationalism and degeneration' by the tradition the game is modeling, giving authentic flavor-text language for the trap.

findings.case: Stokely Carmichael on race-vs-class primacy, Black Power speeches
findings.evidence: In their present form neither communism nor socialism speak to the problem of racism. And to black people in this country, racism comes first, far more important than exploitation.
findings.source: /media/user/data/old-hdd/old-hdd/prolewiki/Exports/Library/Library_Stokely Speaks_ Black Power Back to Pan-Africanism.txt
findings.implication: A real, sympathetic historical articulation of prioritizing national/racial identity over class analysis — the exact CLASS_ANALYSIS-vs-NATIONALISM tension the tag vector must model as a legitimate crossroads, not an automatic villain-flag, prior to any PatSoc drift.

findings.case: The 'Ten Demands on the Kuomintang' charge that KMT officials retained the Three People's Principles as legitimating rhetoric while abandoning their substantive (democratic/livelihood) content
findings.evidence: officials are depriving the people of all democratic rights, which is tantamount to abandoning the Principle of Democracy
findings.implication: A direct historical template for the PatSoc pipeline: a nationalist doctrinal shell can be retained as the vehicle of legitimacy while its class content (democracy, livelihood) is silently hollowed out -- CLASS_ANALYSIS drops to zero while the NATIONALISM tag/rhetoric is unchanged or rises, exactly the drift the pipeline should detect.

findings.case: The New Voice's 1980 exchange with COReS/LPR on 'class analysis of racism' vs. the black-nation thesis
findings.evidence: A black people exists in the U.S. and it has a black people's movement, but we have never seen proof that there is a black nation
findings.implication: A real, non-cartoonish articulation of the CLASS_ANALYSIS pole: agreement on the existence of an oppressed people's movement and the need for black political power, while explicitly rejecting nationhood/self-determination terminology — shows the two trunks share substantial common ground and diverge on a narrow, specific theoretical claim.

findings.case: Three Worlds Theory traced to Browderism via the October League/CPML, 1978
findings.evidence: It is no accident that the leading proponent of the 'theory of three worlds', the October League (CPML) is carrying out a Browderite revisionist line in all areas.
findings.implication: A real drift-chain: an international-relations doctrine (Three Worlds) functions as the gateway node feeding a domestic national-chauvinist trunk (Browderism/class-collaboration) — a historical analog to the PatSoc Pipeline's abstract-doctrine-to-Strasserism drift.

findings.case: Traditionalist Worker Party, USA, 2013-2018
findings.evidence: The Traditionalist Worker Party (TWP) was a neo-Nazi party in the United States that existed from 2013 to 2018. It was a lead organizer of the Unite the Right rally.
findings.implication: A concrete named real-world endpoint fusing worker-appeal rhetoric with explicit Strasserism is the terminal analogue of the PatSoc Pipeline's Strasserism node and faction-flip outcome.
findings.parameter: Organizational lifespan 2013-2018 (5 years) before dissolution.

findings.case: Workers Viewpoint Organization's 1975 warning that undifferentiated anti-imperialist rhetoric converts into fascism
findings.evidence: at worst it can be turned into its opposite through fascist demagogy with its typically 'anti-government' and 'anti-business' facade
findings.implication: This is a period-accurate articulation of the exact PatSoc-to-Strasserism mechanism: a doctrine node that stops at anti-imperialism without CLASS_ANALYSIS backing shares surface rhetoric ('anti-government,' 'anti-business') with fascist demagogy and is explicitly flagged, in 1975, as convertible into its opposite -- strong justification for making the PatSoc Pipeline a zero-cost DRIFT (no new distinguishing rhetoric required to slide).

findings.case: 'Introducing The Communist', October 1939 — codification of 18 years of CCP experience into 'three magic weapons'
findings.evidence: the united front, armed struggle and Party building are the Chinese Communist Party's three "magic weapons", its three principal magic weapons for defeating the enemy in the Chinese revolution.
findings.implication: An internal Party journal is explicitly founded as the instrument for converting accumulated practical experience into codified, transmissible doctrine for new (less-consolidated) branches — the historical model for 'circulation as theoretical-labor return' (Iskra-style), where the periodical's purpose is stated as raising branches to full doctrinal consolidation, not just news.
findings.parameter: Doctrine took 18 years (1921-1939) of practice before being codified into 3 fixed doctrinal trunks — candidate DOCTRINE_CODIFICATION_LATENCY_YEARS = 18

findings.case: 'Methods of Work of Party Committees', Second Plenum of the Seventh CC, March 1949 — 'exchange information' doctrine
findings.evidence: "Exchange information." This means that members of a Party committee should keep each other informed and exchange views on matters that have come to their attention.
findings.implication: A named, numbered cadre-management method whose explicit failure mode is cadres lacking 'a common language ... on basic theoretical problems of Marxism-Leninism' — direct precedent for a coherence coefficient that measures whether cadre time invested in study actually produces shared doctrinal vocabulary, not just raw hours.

findings.case: 'Military Communism' material collapse, 1918-1921
findings.evidence: By 1921, the economy was operating at about 20% of the pre-war level... In 1918-19, at least 12 million people died of typhus
findings.implication: The material floor under the Scientific/Bureaucratic trunk's origin story is quantifiable: 80% economic collapse plus a 12-million-death epidemic is the actual opportunity-cost environment in which 'habits of command' replaced 'workers' democracy' (per the same source) — grounds the Bureaucratic-degeneration trap's trigger condition in a real siege-economy collapse, not abstract corruption.
findings.parameter: civil-war collapse baseline: economy at ~20% of pre-war output by 1921; 12M typhus deaths 1918-19, 20M further affected by 1922

findings.case: 'Youth Needs Experience' (Oct 1939) -- cadre competence framed as accruing through sustained grassroots practice, not doctrine alone
findings.evidence: if, for several years, one does work that is genuine, not false, then experience will come to those who do not have it.
findings.implication: Supports modeling Theoretical Labor's 'coherence' term as accruing slowly through practice-time rather than being purchasable instantly with study allocation alone -- a cadre with high study allocation but no practice years should have low coherence.
findings.parameter: 'several years' as the minimum grassroots-practice timescale for genuine cadre experience.

findings.case: 10th Congress Control Commission eligibility rule, 1921
findings.evidence: For the Central Control Commission - not less than ten years' party membership.
findings.implication: Seniority gating (10 years for central, 5 for oblast, 'since Feb 1917' for guberniia) on who may staff the correction body -- a coherence/experience floor could gate which cadre are eligible to sit on an in-game Congress tribunal.
findings.parameter: 10 years' party membership (central tier) / 5 years (oblast) / membership since Feb 1917 Revolution (guberniia, i.e. ~4 years at time of ruling) -- tiered seniority thresholds.

findings.case: 10th Congress Control Commission staffing rule, 1921
findings.evidence: a certain portion of the members of the control commissions are to be freed from all other work
findings.implication: Rectification/investigation capacity is explicitly bought with an opportunity-cost draw on cadre time (full-time investigators pulled off other duties) -- exactly the Theoretical Labor formula's cadre x allocation structure, applied to the correction mechanism itself.
findings.parameter: All CCC members, 3 oblast-level members, and 2 guberniia-level members are pulled full-time -- candidate TL-cost figure for running a Party Congress rectification cycle.

findings.case: 10th Congress creation of the Control Commissions, 16 March 1921
findings.evidence: At the centre, the commission is elected with a membership of seven full members and three candidate members.
findings.implication: Exact numeric skeleton for a Party Congress rectification/anti-corruption sub-body: small, senior-only, structurally independent of line administration ('cannot be members of party committees'). Good template for a Bureaucratic-degeneration-trap counter-institution.
findings.parameter: Central Control Commission = 7 full + 3 candidate members; minimum tenure 10 years (central) / 5 years (oblast) / since Feb 1917 (guberniia) — candidate seniority-gate values for a rectification body.

findings.case: 10th Party Congress ban on factions, Russian Communist Party (Bolsheviks), 1921
findings.evidence: the Bolshevik leadership, including Lenin, instituted a ban on factions in the party as Resolution No. 12 of the 10th Party Congress in 1921
findings.implication: Shows a Party Congress being used not just to correct a drifting trunk but to permanently foreclose future factional splitting - a precedent for a doctrine-tree congress action that trades away future flexibility (no more trunk_shift) for present unity.

findings.case: 10th RSDLP(b) Congress establishes the Control Commissions, 16 March 1921
findings.evidence: At the centre, the commission is elected with a membership of seven full members and three candidate members.
findings.implication: Concrete institutional sizing for a rectification/self-criticism body -- gives a defensible headcount ratio (7 full : 3 candidate at the top tier, shrinking to 3:2 at oblast and guberniia level) for the game's Party Congress correction-mechanism committee.
findings.parameter: Central Control Commission = 7 full + 3 candidate members; oblast = 3-5 full + 2-3 candidate; guberniia = 3 full + 2 candidate -- direct candidate values for a rectification-committee-size define.

findings.case: 1942 rectification document 'Oppose Stereotyped Party Writing' diagnoses dogmatism as Theoretical Labor spent without paired investigation
findings.evidence: A problem is the contradiction in a thing. Where one has an unresolved contradiction, there one has a problem... To pose the problem, you must first make a preliminary investigation and study of the two basic aspects of the problem.
findings.implication: Names the concrete failure mode for Theoretical Labor: TL invested in doctrine formalism without a paired investigation/mass-contact input produces 'stereotyped' output -- correct-looking but practically inert. TL spent in isolation from mass-line actions should carry a coherence penalty, mirroring 'you cannot SCOUT your way to intelligence.'

findings.case: 1981 Resolution's characterization of dogmatism as the Party's recurring 'infantile malady', predating and distinct from the Cultural Revolution
findings.evidence: our Party suffered again and again from the 'infantile malady' of turning Marxism into a dogma and deifying foreign experience -- a malady which could not but leave the Chinese revolution groping in the dark.
findings.implication: Names dogmatism (TL divorced from concrete investigation) as a PERSISTENT, RECURRING failure mode across the Party's whole history (1920s-30s, and again pre-GPCR), not a one-off. The theoretical-labor coherence penalty in the DT mechanic should be able to recur multiple times per playthrough, not just fire once.

findings.case: Alkalimat's opportunist tenure inside RWL, 1973-1975
findings.evidence: a year and a half after his entry into the RWL, he was purged in the course of the struggle against the RU line.
findings.implication: A doctrinally-misaligned cadre operated for 18 months before exposure and purge. Candidate timescale for how long a hidden opportunist/off-doctrine influence can persist inside an organization before a rectification event surfaces it.
findings.parameter: 1.5 years (18 months) — cadre-misalignment-to-purge detection latency.

findings.case: Bolshevik Iskra newspaper as party-building scaffold, 1900-1902
findings.evidence: Lenin established Iskra in late 1900, and through tremendous efforts for two years, Iskra developed the correct line for a number of major questions
findings.implication: Models Theoretical Labor as an infrastructure investment prior to formal doctrine-tree unlocks, not a one-time purchase: the party press had to run for two years developing line and drawing demarcations before the founding congress was possible ('Iskra became the Party and the Party became Iskra').
findings.parameter: 2 years (Iskra founded late 1900 -> RSDLP 2nd Congress 1902); the 'Organizing Committee' correction/congress-prep mechanism was reused a decade later at the 1912 Bolshevik-Menshevik formal split.

findings.case: Bolshevik Pravda (1912-1913) as a self-financed party press / theoretical-labor organ
findings.evidence: the paper of our Russian comrades, Pravda, for the years 1912 and 1913 is an example of this. It represented a highly active organisation of conscious revolutionary workers
findings.implication: The Comintern itself cites Pravda 1912-13 as the model of a party press funded and staffed by workers 'financing it from their own wages' — a directly documented case (the exact Iskra-type example the brief asked for) of Theoretical Labor as an opportunity-cost draw on cadre wages/time, sustained over a multi-year window (1912-1913), returning propaganda reach and organizational cohesion.
findings.parameter: ~2-year (1912-1913) sustained cadre-wage-financed party press as the historical unit for costing a 'Party Press' doctrine/theoretical-labor node.

findings.case: CPA (Australia) membership trajectory, 1945-1952
findings.evidence: In 1945 there were 16,280 members; in 1946, 13,450; in 1947, 12,108. The heavy government onslaught on the party in 1948-52 further reduced the party to about 6,000 members
findings.implication: A real cadre-count decay curve tied to state repression plus internal line-struggle cost — usable directly as a Theoretical Labor input trajectory (cadre count collapsing by ~63% over 7 years under sustained state pressure and post-strike demoralization).
findings.parameter: Membership: 16,280 (1945) -> 13,450 (1946) -> 12,108 (1947) -> ~6,000 (1948-52); annual membership turnover fell from 23% (1946) to 19% (1947) to 11% (1948) — cadre-count and churn-rate coefficients.

findings.case: CPI (Maoist) multi-tier organizational congress cadence, India, post-2004
findings.evidence: The highest body of the CPI (Maoist) is the All-India Party Congress, which is held once every five years.
findings.implication: A real, currently-active multi-tier congress cadence usable directly as the Party Congress action's base cooldown, scaled by organizational tier (national vs. regional vs. local).
findings.parameter: National congress cadence = 5 years; state/divisional committee cadence = 3 years; area-conference cadence = 2 years.

findings.case: CPUSA Central Committee's total capitulation to the ECCI Address, May 18, 1929
findings.evidence: The Central Committee accepts and endorses the Address to the American Party membership by the Executive Committee of the Communist International and undertakes to win the entire Party membership for the support of the Comintern Address.
findings.implication: Gives the literal historical text/template for a 'self_criticism' action's resolution state: full, unconditional, publicly-recorded submission to an external correcting body, immediately followed by an 'enlightenment campaign' to re-align rank and file.

findings.case: CPUSA Organizer's Manual (1935) base-unit meeting cadence and time budget
findings.evidence: Only in exceptional cases, when it is impossible to bring together the members every week, should we make exceptions from the rule of one meeting per week for each Unit.
findings.implication: A base-level, hard cadence figure for cadre time-cost: weekly meetings capped near 2.5 hours (stated elsewhere in the same chapter) is the smallest unit of theoretical-labor/organizational overhead in the historical Party -- a natural floor for a per-tick Theoretical Labor draw.
findings.parameter: Base-unit study/organizing cadence: 1 meeting/week, capped at ~2.5 hours

findings.case: Censorship-driven compromise on the MEC manuscript before Russian publication (1909)
findings.evidence: He had to agree to tone down some passages of the work so as not to give he tsarist censorship an excuse for prohibiting its publication.
findings.implication: Theoretical-labor output is not freely deployable — a repression/censorship tax degrades the fidelity of the doctrine actually delivered to cadre, a candidate friction term on TL spend under State pressure.

findings.case: Comintern Lenin School, Moscow, 1926-1929 (Harry Haywood's cohort)
findings.evidence: the school was to train sixty to seventy qualified students both in theoretical and practical subjects... It offered a full three-year course and a short course of one year.
findings.implication: Concrete cadre-training-duration data: elite cadre formation runs 1-3 years even at a fully resourced dedicated Comintern institution, grounding a high multi-tick Theoretical Labor cost for producing high-coherence cadre.
findings.parameter: Cohort size 60-70 students; course length 1 year (short) or 3 years (full) — candidate Theoretical-Labor duration coefficients.

findings.case: Comintern Third Congress 1921 -- real time cost of multinational deliberation
findings.evidence: Only by realizing these time-consuming translating and digesting procedures does it become understandable why some congresses lasted as long as forty-five days.
findings.implication: Gives a real upper-bound duration for a Party/International Congress event when many sections/languages are represented -- usable to scale the in-game Congress mechanic's tick-cost by the number of factions/organizations participating.
findings.parameter: Congress duration up to 45 days (Third Congress, 1921) -- candidate upper-bound tick-cost for a full multi-faction Congress event.

findings.case: Comintern Third Congress theses warn against over-extending cell/cadre structure faster than membership can absorb it
findings.evidence: It would be very easy, of course, simply to divide up the members of the organisation into small cells and groups according to some formal scheme and order them to take part in the general day-to-day Party work.
findings.implication: The theses explicitly flag this as 'worse than no beginning at all' — a historical warning that spending Theoretical Labor to build organizational structure faster than cadre coherence/discipline can sustain backfires, supporting a coherence-penalty if doctrine-tree node purchases outpace actual cadre count/training.

findings.case: Comintern cadre-formation timeline outside Russia, 1918-1923
findings.evidence: The education and training of this new layer of leaders began in 1918 and by 1923 was only just beginning to produce its first fruits.
findings.implication: Real ~5-year minimum lag between founding a cadre-training apparatus and it producing mature leadership -- theoretical labor should have a multi-year payoff horizon, not instant node unlocks.
findings.parameter: ~5 years (1918-1923) from cadre-formation start to first mature organizational output.

findings.case: Comintern's repeated pre-1929 rectification resolutions against CPUSA factionalism, 1925-1928
findings.evidence: the first prerequisite is complete and unconditional termination of the factional fight within the Communist Party, not in words, but in deeds
findings.implication: A real historical cadence of roughly annual 'soft' corrective pronouncements (1925 Fifth Plenum, 1926 Sixth Plenum, July 1927 resolution, 1928 Sixth Congress theses) that repeatedly failed before the 1929 hard intervention — a defensible basis for how many ineffective rectification attempts a Congress mechanic should allow before forcing a real leadership-cost event.
findings.parameter: ~4-5 corrective resolutions over 4 years (1925-1929, roughly annual) before the decisive May 1929 leadership removal.

findings.case: Comment on the Five-Level Cadre Conference (T'ao Lu-ch'ieh's report), March 30 1959
findings.evidence: If one commune can pay back its original advanced cooperative cash receipts of four million yuan to the original owners, why cannot others pay them back?
findings.implication: A real-time mid-Leap rectification comment delivered via the cadre-conference channel, naming over-extraction ('uncompensated appropriation of other people's labor') as illegitimate and demanding restitution — the Party Congress/Conference correction mechanic caught acting mid-trap, with a concrete monetary figure attached.
findings.parameter: 4,000,000 yuan cited as one commune's restitution figure (1959) for uncompensated labor appropriation

findings.case: Correction of Browder revisionism after Duclos's article, CPUSA, May-July 1945
findings.evidence: he was suspended as general secretary, and a secretariat of three—William Z. Foster, Eugene Dennis, and John Williamson—was chosen in his stead
findings.source: /media/user/data/old-hdd/old-hdd/prolewiki/Exports/Library/Library_History of the Communist Party of the United States.txt
findings.implication: Models the Party Congress rectification action in three concrete stages: leadership suspension within days of the critique, National Committee ratification about a month later, full reconstituting congress about two months after that - a plausible three-tier cost/cadence for the correction mechanic.
findings.parameter: Suspension 'a few days' after critique received (May 20, 1945); National Committee ratification ~1 month later (June 18-20, 1945); full congress reconstitution ~2 months after that (July 26-28, 1945).

findings.case: Cuban Communist Party's 1986 'Rectification of Errors' campaign under Fidel Castro
findings.evidence: Rectification is finding the way to resolve errors that are hundreds of years old.
findings.implication: A real named 'Rectification' campaign explicitly framed as reaching back further than the revolution's own errors (to colonial/pre-revolutionary prejudice) — a strong precedent for a Congress 'rectification' action that can target ideological sediment predating the current game state, not just recent mis-tunes.
findings.parameter: Campaign launched 1986, with the associated discriminatory law (Public Ostentation Law) repealed 1988 — ~2 years from rectification-campaign launch to a concrete legal-policy correction.

findings.case: DPRK doctrine on prioritizing ideological remoulding over other party work
findings.evidence: one must pay bigger efforts to the ideological remoulding and put it before all other works
findings.implication: An explicit doctrinal statement that ideological/theoretical work takes priority over other productive labor — supports modeling Theoretical Labor as a genuine opportunity-cost draw on cadre-hours (time spent studying is time not spent producing or organizing), consistent with the TL = cadre × study allocation × coherence formula.

findings.case: Draft Resolution on Demonstrations, 2nd RSDLP Congress, 1903
findings.evidence: preparations for armed demonstrations should be begun, strictly observing instructions of the Central Committee
findings.implication: Even in a minor procedural resolution from the eventual Bolshevik-aligned majority, insurrectionary preparation is explicitly reserved to central authority rather than local committees — an early textual anchor for the Insurrectionist trunk's centralizing tendency, years before October 1917.

findings.case: Eighth Congress (1956) framing the 1942 rectification campaign's lasting but incomplete effect on Party theoretical level
findings.evidence: since the campaign in 1942 to rectify the style of work in the Party, we have done much to strengthen Marxist-Leninist education within the Party.
findings.implication: Models rectification/Theoretical Labor investment as producing a durable but PARTIAL coherence gain ('raised its level... by another step,' not a full reset) even 14 years later -- argues against a DT design where one rectification campaign fully resets coherence; it should be a partial, compounding gain requiring repeated reinvestment.

findings.case: Great Leap steel-target correction cascade, 1958-59
findings.evidence: In 1957 we opposed adventurism. As a result, progress took a saddle-like form. In that year we had to lower production targets.
findings.implication: Mao names 'wave-like motion' as a deliberate policy rhythm (over-shoot, correct, re-launch) rather than steady-state planning — supports oscillating doctrine/trunk commitments rather than monotonic convergence, consistent with 'no optimal path.'
findings.parameter: Steel target cut from the Pei-tai-ho figure to 20 million tons (Sixth CPC Plenum) to 16.5 million tons (Shanghai Conference) within about one year — a documented 2-step successive downward correction cadence.

findings.case: Guardian Clubs press-building program, 1977
findings.evidence: the potential for doubling the Guardian's present circulation of approximately 25,000 already exists
findings.implication: A concrete party-press baseline and growth target. Gives Theoretical Labor's press/Iskra-node a real starting circulation and a 'doubling requires sustained organized cadre-time' framing for the TL-to-circulation conversion curve.
findings.parameter: baseline circulation ≈25,000; 2x target (50,000) framed as requiring dedicated organizational effort, not passive growth — candidate TL→circulation curve anchor.

findings.case: Guardian Clubs' missing study plan, 1977-1978
findings.evidence: Although founded in September, 1977, the clubs lacked a systematic study plan for the entire first year.
findings.implication: Shows a real 1-year lag between an org's founding and its Theoretical Labor curriculum actually operating — coefficient candidate for a delay between allocating cadre to 'study' and that allocation producing usable coherence/TL output.
findings.parameter: 1-year gap (Sept 1977 founding to a functioning study plan, which arrived just as the org's relationship with its parent paper was already dissolving).

findings.case: Guardian bureaus (1973) maturing into Guardian Clubs (1977)
findings.evidence: Ever since the formation of the first Guardian bureaus back in 1973 it was apparent that there were scores of readers and supporters ready and willing to take on concrete responsibilities
findings.implication: Documents a real 4-year gestation from informal support/reader network to a deliberate cadre-training structure, modeling the ramp-up lag before invested organizational effort compounds into a higher-tier Theoretical Labor apparatus.
findings.parameter: 4-year gestation, 1973 bureaus to 1977 Clubs.

findings.case: Iskra as the Bolsheviks' theoretical-labor engine preceding organizational unity
findings.evidence: Lenin proposed to begin the creation of a Marxist party in Russia with the publication of the newspaper Iskra, i.e., with spreading the revolutionary ideas of Marxism among the workers, and then to reinforce ideological unity by a material organisation
findings.implication: The historical sequencing matters for Theoretical Labor design: ideological consolidation (the press/study-circle layer) is explicitly modeled as preceding and enabling organizational (party) unity, not the reverse — TL spend should unlock coherence/doctrine nodes before it unlocks organizational-capacity nodes.

findings.case: January 1904 Party Council — failed mediation of the Bolshevik/Menshevik split
findings.evidence: there have been, I should think, as many as fifty mediators who tried to put an end to the dissensions in the Party
findings.implication: Ordinary appeal-based rectification has a very low historical success rate; a rectification/self_criticism mechanic that always partially heals a rift for a flat TL cost is too generous — most attempts should fail outright.
findings.parameter: ~50 mediation attempts over 5 months (Aug 1903-Jan 1904), 1 partial success — ≈2% success rate for undirected rectification attempts; candidate base success-rate for a self_criticism Congress action absent a structural fix.

findings.case: KAPD's provisional, non-voting admission to the Comintern, Third World Congress 1921
findings.evidence: the Executive Committee had contributed eight million marks for KAPD activities.
findings.implication: A heterodox trunk was kept inside the international apparatus (rather than being cut loose entirely) via a real material subsidy even while denied full voting rights — a historical number for the cost of maintaining a dissenting faction/trunk short of full expulsion, useful for costing a 'tolerate minority trunk' option at Party Congress.
findings.parameter: 8,000,000 Reichsmarks (1921) subsidy to sustain a provisional, sub-voting minority faction inside the international party structure.

findings.case: Kim Il Sung's 'core member' cell-nuclei doctrine for a one-million-strong mass party, North Korea, 1955
findings.evidence: It will be all the more gratifying if their number increases from five today to ten tomorrow and thus all Party members become core element
findings.source: /media/user/data/old-hdd/old-hdd/prolewiki/Exports/Library/Library_On eliminating dogmatism and formalism and establishing Juche in ideological work.txt
findings.implication: Models Theoretical Labor as doubling the trained-cadre fraction of an existing cell (5->10) rather than recruiting new bodies - the input is study/coherence-building among current members, matching TL = cadre x study allocation x coherence rather than raw headcount.
findings.parameter: Target core-member doubling per cell (5 -> 10); contemporaneous Party-to-population ratio = 1:10 (1,000,000 members / 10,000,000 population).

findings.case: Land-reform self-criticism convention for weighing achievements vs. mistakes, 1949
findings.evidence: do the achievements amount to 30 per cent or to 70 per cent of the whole?
findings.implication: This is a genuine historical numeric convention for self-criticism/rectification outcomes — a cadre or campaign is judged 'in the main' correct only above a 70% achievement threshold — directly usable as the pass/fail bar for a self_criticism correction action.
findings.parameter: SELF_CRITICISM_APPROVAL_THRESHOLD = 0.7 primary-achievement fraction (achievements must be ~70% of the whole for work to be 'in the main approved')

findings.case: League for Proletarian Revolution (LPR), critiqued by August 29th Movement at the 1977 Denver Party Building Forum
findings.evidence: NOT ONE ARTICLE out of 53 deals with the struggle of the oppressed nationalities in the U.S. Only 4 deal with the struggle of the working class and only one of the 53 articles deals with the woman question.
findings.implication: A real org's annual press output composition (53 articles/yr) is a legible, quantifiable proxy for line-narrowness; a 'theoretical labor' coefficient could be scored by output-topic distribution rather than raw article count.
findings.parameter: 53 articles/year total output for a 7-year-old sect, with <2% touching the national question and ~2% touching the woman question — candidate floor for 'line breadth' before an org is legible as sectarian-stagnant.

findings.case: League for Proletarian Revolution (M-L) Rectification Campaign, self-criticism on cadre overcommitment, Oct 1978
findings.evidence: our cadres definitely have more tasks over their shoulders than what they can really fulfill in a correct manner
findings.implication: The rectification/self_criticism correction mechanic's real historical cost was diagnosing cadre over-allocation (too many tasks per unit of Theoretical Labor), not a single dramatic event -- Theoretical Labor should model an over-commitment penalty (diminishing returns per cadre once tasks-per-cadre exceeds a threshold).
findings.parameter: rectification campaign published as a serialized 3-part Central Committee report over ~10 months (Dec 1977-Oct 1978)

findings.case: Lenin's 'Materialism and Empirio-Criticism' as an emergency theoretical-labor mobilization against Machism/Bogdanov (1908)
findings.evidence: The book is the outcome of a prodigious amount of creative scientific research carried out by Lenin during nine months.
findings.implication: A single top cadre was pulled entirely out of organizational work for the better part of a year to fight a philosophical deviation — grounds the opportunity-cost draw in TL = cadre x study allocation x coherence at the level of one high-value cadre-unit.
findings.parameter: theoretical-labor cost of one major doctrinal counter-work ~= 9 cadre-months, citing 200+ source titles

findings.case: Lenin's 'Reply to a Reader' on Iskra's publication cadence, Feb 1902
findings.evidence: a revolutionary organ that really deserves the name of periodical (appearing, not once, but at least two or four times, a month)
findings.implication: Gives an explicit minimum output cadence for the flagship theoretical/agitational organ that anchored the whole Bolshevik faction -- usable as the historical baseline circulation-frequency coefficient for a party-press Theoretical Labor sink/output.
findings.parameter: 2-4 issues per month cited as the minimum for a paper to count as a real 'periodical' rather than occasional leaflets -- candidate cadence for an in-game party-press TL output cycle.

findings.case: Lenin's critique of Kautsky's distortion of Marx's state theory (State and Revolution, Ch.2, 1917)
findings.evidence: the opportunism now prevailing trains the members of the workers' party to be the representatives of the better-paid workers, who lose touch with the masses
findings.implication: Names the material mechanism of a Liquidationism/Reformist trap precisely: the labor-aristocracy effect (better-paid strata) captures party leadership and severs it from the base — a ready-made wage-differential trigger condition for that trap.

findings.case: Lenin's three-period periodization of RSDLP history (WITBD Conclusion, 1901)
findings.evidence: The first period embraces about ten years, approximately from 1884 to 1894. This was the period of the rise and consolidation of the theory and programme of Social-Democracy.
findings.implication: A real historical ratio of ~10 years of pure theory-building ('Social-Democracy existed without a working-class movement') before a 3-4 year mass-upsurge payoff period (1894-98) — anchors how long Theoretical Labor investment can run before any mass-line return, useful for TL opportunity-cost pacing.
findings.parameter: ~10 years pure theoretical-labor accumulation (1884-94) : ~4 years mass-upsurge payoff (1894-98) ≈ 2.5:1 ratio.

findings.case: Lenin, The State and Revolution, Preface to the First Edition, August 1917
findings.evidence: The question of the state is now acquiring particular importance both in theory and in practical politics.
findings.implication: Lenin diverted significant time to a major theoretical text at the height of a live insurrectionary crisis (between February and October 1917) specifically because unresolved theory (the state question) was creating practical vulnerability — a direct historical case for Theoretical Labor as opportunity-cost: cadre-time spent on doctrine competes with, but also insures, action-time.

findings.case: Lovestone on Stalin's failed partial-concession cycle before the 1937 purges
findings.evidence: These secondary concessions did not serve to allay the dissatisfaction which was becoming more rampant.
findings.implication: A documented failed rectification cycle: partial self-criticism/concessions (a mild Party-Congress-style correction) proved insufficient to resolve accumulated line-tension, forcing escalation to a full Trap — models a rectification-cost threshold below which correction cannot actually discharge tension.

findings.case: Mao on cadre policy in 'The Role of the Chinese Communist Party in the National War', 1938
findings.evidence: Cadres are a decisive factor, once the political line is determined.
findings.implication: Direct textual grounding for treating Theoretical Labor as gated on cadre count: the doctrine ('line') is necessary but inert without a cadre stock able to execute it — matches TL = cadre_count x study_allocation x coherence.

findings.case: Mao's 1960 marginal reading notes disputing the Soviet political-economy textbook, predating the public Sino-Soviet split
findings.evidence: This is our main difference with the revisionists, who claim that in capitalist societies such things as municipal public enterprises are actually socialist elements, and argue that capitalism may peacefully grow over to socialism.
findings.implication: A doctrine-tree divergence event can be seeded quietly, years in advance, as marginal annotation/critique of an existing canonical text (a real 'study circle' artifact) well before it manifests as a visible faction flip or public rupture -- supports a slow, hidden 'drift accumulator' feeding the eventual trunk_shift.

findings.case: Minnesota Farmer-Labor propaganda campaign, CPUSA Central Executive Committee, March 1924
findings.evidence: We found that we could carry on this criticism and that we could intensify it.
findings.implication: Foster reports a measurable shift in ten farmer-leader allies' position within a 3-4 month window of sustained leafleting/conference work — a concrete short ROI timescale for converting Theoretical Labor investment into ally-pull on the doctrine-tag axis.
findings.parameter: ~3-4 months from CEC propaganda decision to a March 12, 1924 conference where 10 farmer leaders shifted from opposing to conditionally supporting the Party program.

findings.case: NCM/'primacy of theory' current vs. dogmatist reproduction, 1976-1980
findings.evidence: In 1976, the anti-dogmatist, anti-revisionist movement stood at the crossroads of either developing new bases for a genuine communist party or else replicating the essential course of the NCM under a new cover.
findings.implication: A 4-year natural experiment (the author's own retrospective) showing that unearned/rote 'unity' collapses into the same organizational-over-political pathology as its predecessors absent genuine cadre theoretical development — supports distinguishing TL quantity (cadre-hours) from TL quality/coherence in the formula.
findings.parameter: 4-year window (1976-1980) cited before the verdict ('most forces... have taken the latter course') was rendered.

findings.case: NNMLC 'Guardian Clubs' founded without a study plan, Sept 1977-1978
findings.evidence: the clubs lacked a systematic study plan for the entire first year
findings.implication: Theoretical Labor can be nominally allocated (a study organization exists, cadre are enrolled) yet functionally near-zero for a full year if unsystematized -- models a TL 'waste state' distinct from zero allocation: cadre-hours spent at a near-zero coherence multiplier.
findings.parameter: 1 full year (Sept 1977-1978) of nominally-allocated but structurally ineffective theoretical labor

findings.case: October League warning to the Communist Youth Organization against becoming a pure study circle, Jan 1977
findings.evidence: there are some petty-bourgeois elements who will try and turn this fine organization into a study circle of intellectuals only and away from its role as a fighting organization of the masses
findings.implication: Theoretical Labor investment divorced from mass practice was itself a recognized 1970s failure mode -- the TL resource should have a countervailing pull toward practice/mass-work, not scale as a pure multiplier, or it silently produces an 'intellectualist' sub-trap.
findings.parameter: ~200 CYO members at its 1976 founding convention were enough to rebuff a rival faction's ('Revolutionary Wing') recruitment ambush -- a concrete cadre-count threshold for base resilience against sectarian incursion

findings.case: October League's April-1976 campaign to convert its paper, The Call, from monthly to weekly publication
findings.evidence: In the month of March, The Call received some 120 articles written by people across the country
findings.implication: A concrete Iskra-analog circulation/output ramp figure: 120 submitted articles (30+ from first-time worker correspondents) and volunteer committees in 30 cities within a 6-month resourcing push — directly usable to calibrate the TL-cost/output curve of expanding party-press frequency.
findings.parameter: 6-month ramp from monthly to weekly; 120 articles/month by the ramp's end; committees active in 30 cities; >50% of first-year budget raised via $5-$10 individual donations.

findings.case: October League's Call newspaper class-reach figures, 1977
findings.evidence: 75% of the newspapers are sold to working-class and minority people.
findings.implication: A concrete 'class reach' fraction for a propaganda node's actual audience versus its notional print run. A companion figure in the same document (~50% distributed at factory gates) gives a second, stricter proletarian-penetration metric — both usable as press-node audience-conversion coefficients.
findings.parameter: 75% working-class/minority readership; ~50% factory-gate circulation — candidate class-reach coefficients for a Theoretical Labor press node.

findings.case: Organizing Committee for a Marxist-Leninist Party's Second Unity Conference, pre-founding-congress process (1977)
findings.evidence: The program at that time will be taken to the masses of workers for discussion and debate, especially in study circles and Call discussion groups.
findings.implication: A real pre-Congress funnel had discrete stages: internal draft, internal discussion, a staging 'Unity Conference,' public program release, mass study-circle/newspaper-group review, then the founding Congress. The Party Congress mechanic could cost Theoretical Labor across staged sub-events rather than as one lump purchase.
findings.parameter: A 'Second Unity Conference' functioned as an explicit penultimate staging step before the first Party Congress — candidate two-stage congress cadence (Unity Conference -> Founding Congress) rather than a single event.

findings.case: Origin of the 'Ten Great Relationships' line, 1955-56 cadre consultation preceding the Great Leap Forward
findings.evidence: we sought out over thirty cadres for consultation. As a result of those discussions we proposed the "ten great relationships"
findings.implication: Gives a concrete historical cadre count for a doctrine-defining Theoretical Labor draw: a major line document was produced by convening roughly thirty senior cadres, not the whole Party — usable as a minimum viable TL 'draw size' for a trunk-level doctrine node.
findings.parameter: DOCTRINE_CONSULTATION_CADRE_COUNT ≈ 30 ('over thirty cadres for consultation', 1955-56)

findings.case: PLA casualty/kill-ratio, 1946 civil war
findings.evidence: in order to wipe out 10,000 enemy troops we have to pay a price of 2,000 to 3,000 casualties of our own
findings.implication: A concrete numeric cost-ratio for offensive action, usable as a baseline attrition coefficient for any mechanic trading cadre/force losses for territorial or intelligence gains.
findings.parameter: 20-30% casualty ratio (2,000-3,000 own losses per 10,000 enemy destroyed).

findings.case: Pacific Collective vs. Workers Congress (M-L) polemic over cadre-time allocation to 'advanced' workers vs. mass agitation, 1978-79
findings.evidence: whether we therefore should make socialist ideas widely available to workers, through the best agitation that we are capable of in this period, so that the catalyst of those ideas can be added to the crucible of class struggle
findings.implication: A real, documented opportunity-cost debate over cadre time: concentrate propaganda narrowly on a small 'advanced' stratum vs. spread agitation broadly to grow the advanced stratum over time — directly models the Theoretical Labor trade-off between deep study-circle investment and broad mass-facing output.

findings.case: Postscript to The State and Revolution, November 1917
findings.evidence: It is more pleasant and useful to go through the "experience of revolution" than to write about it.
findings.implication: Theoretical Labor is an opportunity-cost draw against the same cadre-time as action — Lenin permanently abandoned the planned Chapter 7 once material crisis (October Revolution) hit. Let acute-crisis ticks zero out TL allocation entirely rather than merely tax it.
findings.parameter: Written Aug-Sept 1917 (~2 months, 6 chapters); Chapter 7 planned but never written — TL allocation effectively went to 0% for, and permanently after, the crisis tick.

findings.case: Profintern affiliation dues formula, 1921
findings.evidence: at least 1 per cent. of the total income of the organisations which receive into their central treasury 50 per cent. or more of the membership dues
findings.implication: Theoretical Labor's opportunity-cost draw can be scaled inversely to organizational centralization exactly as this sliding scale does — more centralized affiliates owe a smaller percentage (1%), fragmented/decentralized ones owe more (up to 5%), which could translate into the coherence multiplier of the TL formula.
findings.parameter: Sliding affiliation levy: 1% (≥50% dues centralized) up to 5% (<10% dues centralized) of total income.

findings.case: RWL Second National Conference, January 1976 — correction of the Mark Smith centrist line
findings.evidence: At that time, he was removed from all positions of leadership and placed in isolation as a special case for transformation.
findings.implication: A real Congress-correction outcome that is neither full expulsion nor no-op: demotion plus mandatory individual re-education. Gives the Party Congress mechanic a middle rectification tier between 'self_criticism' (cheap) and 'trunk_shift/expulsion' (expensive).

findings.case: RWL's five internal line struggles, 1973-1976
findings.evidence: We have come to our present state of development through the course of five major line struggles over the past two years.
findings.implication: A real cadence for internal congress-equivalent events in a young, fast-forming vanguard org: roughly one major line struggle every 5 months. Useful as the Congress-trigger frequency during a 'founding volatility' phase before a doctrine stabilizes.
