findings.parameter: 5 line struggles / 2 years ≈ 1 per 4.8 months — candidate Congress-cadence coefficient for newly-founded/high-volatility organizations.

findings.case: RWL's stated method for handling hostile/incorrect criticism, 1976 self-summation
findings.evidence: We will instead seek to find the 5% which is correct and deepen it through the course of principled struggle.
findings.implication: Models a partial-credit rule for self-criticism: even a largely wrong or hostile critique should yield a small usable doctrine-vector nudge rather than an all-or-nothing accept/reject outcome at Congress time.
findings.parameter: 5% (0.05) — partial-credit fraction extractable from an otherwise-rejected criticism during rectification.

findings.case: Red International of Labour Unions (Profintern) congress cadence and threshold, Moscow 1921
findings.evidence: Congresses take place as much as possible, at least once a year... at the demand of organisations representing no less than one-third of the members
findings.implication: A concrete real-world cadence (annual ordinary congress) plus a numeric threshold (1/3 of membership) for triggering an extraordinary correction congress — directly usable as the Party Congress mechanism's default tick-interval and its emergency-trigger rule.
findings.parameter: Ordinary congress: at least once/year. Extraordinary congress trigger: petition by organisations representing ≥1/3 of total membership.

findings.case: Right opportunism as the historically dominant US failure mode (MLOC 'Central Task,' 1975)
findings.evidence: the overriding danger historically within the communist movement has been right opportunism in the form of economism
findings.implication: Provides a US-specific asymmetry claim for weighting trap probabilities: in a US-hegemony scenario the Reformist/Liquidationist trunk trap should be tuned as the historically dominant failure mode, with the ultra-left/Adventurist trap being real but rarer.
findings.parameter: 'close to fifty years' (~1930s-1975) of chronic right-opportunist dominance cited as the historical baseline.

findings.case: SPD organizational infrastructure by 1912 (pre-war Reformist trunk)
findings.evidence: the SPD had one million members, over 15,000 full time party workers, assets worth more than 21 million gold marks, 90 daily newspapers and 62 printing offices
findings.implication: A mature Reformist-trunk party is a genuine theoretical-labor economy at scale: 15,000 full-time cadre, dozens of papers, a full press apparatus (plus a 'massive Central Socialist School' noted in the same passage). Gives a concrete cadre-count ceiling for a fully-invested Reformist trunk before the crisis of 1914 — useful as a TL-accumulation baseline/cap.
findings.parameter: candidate TL infrastructure baseline: ~15,000 full-time party cadre + 90 daily papers/62 printing offices supporting a 1M-member reformist party circa 1912

findings.case: Same document: contrast between Stalin's terse July 3, 1941 war speech and the Party's own tendency toward padded, empty writing
findings.evidence: Stalin's speech on July 3 was only the length of an editorial in our Liberation Daily. Had any of our gentlemen written that speech, just imagine! It would have run to tens of thousands of words at a minimum.
findings.implication: Verbose, form-heavy doctrine output is itself a diagnostic tell of low-coherence Theoretical Labor -- a design heuristic that a doctrine node's 'cost' or output volume should be inversely, not positively, correlated with real coherence gain.

findings.case: Same preface — diagnosis of Second International opportunism as the target of the theoretical labor
findings.evidence: elements of opportunism that accumulated over the decades of comparatively peaceful development have given rise to the trend of social-chauvinism which dominated the official socialist parties
findings.implication: Names the Reformist-trunk trap mechanism precisely: opportunism accumulates gradually ('decades of comparatively peaceful development') and only becomes visible as catastrophic failure (social-chauvinism, support for the imperialist war) under crisis. Supports modeling the Liquidationism trap as a slow drift with delayed, crisis-triggered detonation rather than an instant flip.
findings.parameter: Decades-scale drift: Second International reformism accumulated 'over the decades' (roughly 1889-1914, ~25 years) before its 1914 social-chauvinist collapse — candidate slow-burn timescale for the Reformist/Liquidationism trunk trap versus PatSoc's fast drift chain

findings.case: Same preface — the two editions of The State and Revolution
findings.evidence: The present, second edition is published virtually unaltered, except that section 3 had been added to Chapter II.
findings.implication: A canonical theoretical output, once produced, proved stable — one added section across ~16 months (Aug 1917 to Dec 1918) spanning the whole October seizure of power and Civil War onset. Contrasts with a 'zero-cost' PatSoc drift chain: real theoretical labor purchases durable doctrine that resists cheap revision, unlike drift nodes that should be gameable at near-zero TL cost.
findings.parameter: Doctrine revision cadence: 1 substantive addition (one section) across ~16 months between 1st and 2nd editions of a canonical text — candidate floor for how 'sticky' a purchased Doctrine Tree node should be against subsequent low-cost edits

findings.case: Second Congress of the Comintern, National and Colonial Question commission — live thesis amendment on the floor, July 1920
findings.evidence: The Commission checked over Comrade Lenin's Theses and also Comrade Roy's supplementary Theses
findings.implication: Direct model for the Party Congress mechanic's format: a standing commission pre-drafts amendments line-by-line, the floor votes clause-by-clause, and the whole doctrine document is patched rather than replaced — trunk_shift/theoretical_offensive should read like clause amendments, not full rewrites.
findings.parameter: Session opened evening, closed at 2:30 a.m. — a single marathon sitting processed a full thesis-amendment slate; candidate for one 'Congress tick' costing an entire late-night session rather than being instantaneous.

findings.case: Second National Call/El Clarin Conference on Agitation and Propaganda, April 1977
findings.evidence: more than 400 revolutionary journalists and organizers to this city on April 2-3.
findings.implication: A real scale reference for a 'theoretical offensive'/party-school mobilization event: 400 cadre from ~40 city chapters, 30 parallel workshop tracks. Useful to calibrate the Theoretical Labor cost of a Congress-scale gathering versus routine study-circle activity.
findings.parameter: 400 cadre delegates, ~40 chapters, 30 workshops — reference scale for a Congress-tier mobilization event.

findings.case: Sixteen Articles Concerning Work Methods — cadre fear list, May 1959
findings.evidence: None other than these six things: dismissal from office, demotion, expulsion from the party, divorce by your wife, imprisonment and decapitation.
findings.implication: Mao's own enumerated cost-schedule for cadre self-censorship — a ready-made severity ladder for pricing the personal-risk side of Theoretical Labor, i.e. why honest study/criticism is not a free opportunity-cost draw even absent an external trap.
findings.parameter: 6 discrete named penalty tiers (demotion through execution) cited as the standing deterrent against candid internal criticism.

findings.case: Sixth Comintern Congress transition-path taxonomy, 1928
findings.evidence: The variety of conditions and ways by which the proletariat will achieve its dictatorship in the various countries may be divided schematically into three main types.
findings.implication: Official Comintern doctrine itself explicitly rejects a single correct path, instead branching tactics by material development level (advanced-capitalist / medium-developed / colonial-semicolonial) — direct textual precedent within the theory itself for 'no optimal path,' since even orthodoxy conditions doctrine on material context rather than ranking trunks.

findings.case: Sixth National Congress of the CPC, dual rectification, July 1928
findings.evidence: The Sixth Congress liquidated the 1927 Right Capitulationism of Chen Tu-hsiu and also repudiated the 'Left' putschism which occurred in the Party at the end of 1927 and the beginning of 1928
findings.implication: A single Party Congress explicitly correcting deviations on BOTH sides of the line (Right capitulationism AND Left putschism) in one sitting, roughly 11 months after the Aug 7 1927 emergency conference first ousted the Right line — evidence that the Congress correction mechanic can target multiple simultaneous trunk failures, not just one.
findings.parameter: ~11 months from Aug 7 1927 emergency conference to the Sixth Congress (July 1928) dual rectification

findings.case: Soviet "Political Economy" textbook print runs, 1954-55
findings.evidence: the first edition of the Political Economy textbook, published at the end of 1954 in over six million copies, was rapidly sold out.
findings.implication: Gives a real circulation-scale figure for canonical party-school/study-circle curricular material, corroborating that Theoretical Labor infrastructure operates at millions-of-copies scale for a mass party — a ceiling reference distinct from the smaller underground-press archetype.
findings.parameter: 6,000,000+ copies of the first edition (1954), used in "Party schools and study-groups" per the second-edition foreword.

findings.case: Stalin's 1937 Central Committee plenum, tiered cadre-training courses
findings.evidence: Further, four-month Party courses must be established in each regional center to give secretaries of units Party training and to re-equip them.
findings.source: /media/user/data/old-hdd/old-hdd/prolewiki/Exports/Library/Library_On the shortcomings of party work and measures to eliminate Trotskyist and other double-dealers_Our Tasks.txt
findings.implication: A tiered curriculum of concrete durations scaled to cadre seniority is a direct real-world Theoretical Labor cost table — deeper doctrine-tree tiers should cost proportionally more study-time the higher the cadre's rank/coherence target.
findings.parameter: Course durations of 4, 6, and 8 months by cadre tier; leadership strata sized at 3,000-4,000 (top), 30,000-40,000 (middle), 100,000-150,000 (lower rank) — candidate cadre-population-tier coefficients.

findings.case: Structure of the Tenth Plenum itself: ~2 months of small-group study/debate at Peitaiho and Peking preceding a brief formal plenary vote
findings.evidence: This conference did not open today: it has already been in session for over two months. It met for a month in Peitaiho and has continued for almost a month since transferring to Peking.
findings.implication: Real Congress procedure separates a long Theoretical-Labor-costly deliberation phase from a short formal ratification phase (3-5 days) -- supports modeling a Congress/trunk_shift event as two sequential sub-phases with very different TL costs, not one atomic action.
findings.parameter: ~2 months deliberation phase, 3-5 day plenary/ratification phase.

findings.case: The 1923 'New Course' resolution and the Lenin Levy -- a rectification vote diluted by mass recruitment
findings.evidence: Only 1 per cent of the Bolshevik Party of 1924 had been members before March 1917!
findings.implication: A real Congress-level rectification resolution (workers' democracy, more frequent congresses, control commissions) passed unanimously but was never enforced, while 250,000 new recruits were admitted specifically to outvote the Left Opposition -- models rectification as vulnerable to being nullified by TL-diluting mass recruitment, and shows 'vote passed' must be decoupled from 'effect applied.'
findings.parameter: 250,000 new Party members admitted Feb-May 1924 (Lenin Levy), reducing the pre-1917 veteran share to just 1% of total membership -- candidate coherence-dilution coefficient for a recruitment-vs-purity tradeoff.

findings.case: The 1938 all-Party study emulation drive announced at the Sixth Plenum
findings.evidence: our Party's fighting capacity will be much greater and our task of defeating Japanese imperialism will be more quickly accomplished if there are one or two hundred comrades with a grasp of Marxism-Leninism which is systematic and not fragmentary
findings.implication: Gives a concrete absolute headcount (100-200 deeply-trained cadres) judged sufficient to lead a revolution of 'several hundred million' — a candidate coherence-gate: TL's 'coherence' term could require crossing an absolute high-study cadre count, not just an aggregate study-hours pool.
findings.parameter: 100-200 deeply-studied cadres cited as the threshold needed to lead a nationwide movement, 1938

findings.case: The Call newspaper circulation growth, CPUSA(ML), 1972-1977
findings.evidence: A monthly newspaper with a circulation of about 5,000 has been transformed into a weekly, (and with this issue, a 20-page weekly), with a circulation of close to 15,000.
findings.implication: A real 5-year growth curve for a vanguard press, usable as a Theoretical-Labor/mass-reach growth-rate baseline (3x circulation growth plus a cadence upgrade from monthly to weekly) coinciding with transition from pre-party propaganda organ to 'central organ of the Party.'
findings.parameter: 5,000 (year 0, monthly) -> ~15,000 (year 5, weekly) regular circulation; best-selling special issues sold 20,000+ copies; a 1977 conference drew 400 attendees.

findings.case: The Erfurt Program (SPD, 1891) — the founding Reformist-trunk doctrine text
findings.evidence: The German Social Democratic Party therefore does not fight for new class privileges and class rights, but for the abolition of class rule and of classes themselves
findings.implication: The program pairs maximalist revolutionary framing with a purely parliamentary, gradualist demands list (suffrage, 8-hour day, tax reform) and zero mention of seizing state power — the real historical template for a Reformist-trunk doctrine node: revolutionary vocabulary as decoration over an entirely legalist action set, which is exactly what later collapses into Liquidationism.

findings.case: The March 1957 rectification movement (a distinct, later campaign) renaming the target triad
findings.evidence: This time the movement will be aimed at three bad styles, bureaucracy, sectarianism and subjectivism.
findings.implication: Two rectification movements 15 years apart (1942, 1957) swap one deviation (stereotyped writing -> bureaucracy) while keeping subjectivism and sectarianism constant -- evidence for a periodic, ~15-year major-rectification cadence nested above much more frequent low-level criticism sessions.
findings.parameter: ~15-year gap between major named rectification campaigns (1942 -> 1957).

findings.case: The Toiler's institutional lifecycle: CLP organ (1919) to UCP trade-union paper to merger into The Worker, Jan 1922
findings.evidence: As a reflection of the unifying spirit of the new Workers Party of America in January 1922, The Toiler was merged with the bi-weekly The Workers Council.
findings.implication: Party-press infrastructure is itself an object of trunk-unification politics: organ mergers are a visible marker of Party-Congress-driven trunk convergence, useful as a DT-tree side effect when trunk_shift/unity resolutions fire.
findings.parameter: 113 issues produced over 'more than two years' -- roughly weekly cadence -- before the CLP/UCP-era organ was retired into a unified paper.

findings.case: The Yan'an Rectification Movement (Zhengfeng), 1942-1945, preceding the 1945 Resolution on Party History and the 7th Congress
findings.evidence: Launched in 1942, it was a tremendous success.
findings.implication: A sustained ~3-year rectification-to-congress cycle (1942 campaign -> 1945 Resolution + 7th Congress) suggests Theoretical Labor investment for a durable doctrinal correction operates on a multi-year horizon, not an instant purchase.
findings.parameter: ~3 years (1942-1945) from rectification campaign launch to codified Resolution + Party Congress

findings.case: Third Congress 1921 floor-time asymmetry between the ruling tendency and a minority delegation
findings.evidence: Since speaking time was limited to ten minutes per person, we applied the following tactic: we split up our report and had several comrades share the task of reading our report.
findings.implication: A real congress mechanic where the ruling tendency's rapporteur (Trotsky) got a multi-hour report while an entire minority delegation was capped near ten minutes per speaker -- usable to weight a Party-Congress 'floor time'/persuasion-bandwidth mechanic by current orthodoxy share rather than a flat per-faction cost.
findings.parameter: Trotsky's world-economic-situation report ran ~3 hours 15 minutes vs. the KAPD delegation capped near 10 minutes/speaker and ~1 hour total on the trade-union theses.

findings.case: UCP 2nd Convention, Kingston NY, Dec 24 1920-Jan 2 1921: a rebuke-vote on the Central Executive Committee's report
findings.evidence: we do not wish our acceptance to be construed as complete endorsement of the report, as we consider that in many cases... the CEC has failed in the correct performance of its important task.
findings.implication: A real historical template for the self_criticism/rectification correction mechanic inside a Party Congress: leadership's report was accepted but paired with a no-confidence amendment via roll call, i.e. correction without full leadership overthrow.
findings.parameter: Congress ran 9 sessions over 10 days; the rebuke amendment carried 26-14, and a motion to strike it was defeated 3-22 (7 not voting, 8 refused, 2 absent); annual conventions mandated for May 10 per the CLP/UCP constitutional pattern.

findings.case: UCP national payroll, Nov 1920 (per financial statement footnote)
findings.evidence: Summing the wage bills — national, local, and editorial — for Nov. 1920 produces a total of $4528... this implies a payroll of 25.2 full-time individuals.
findings.implication: A concrete historical cadre-count-times-wage baseline for calibrating Theoretical Labor: ~25 FTE cadre supporting a nationwide underground party of ~5,800 dues-paying members.
findings.parameter: 25.2 FTE cadre; $45/week average party salary; roughly 1 paid cadre per 230 members.

findings.case: UCP party-press cost breakdown, Nov 1920: The Toiler (trade-union organ) vs. the internal Official Organ
findings.evidence: Official Organ 976.17 ... E.L.O. [English Labor Organ (Toiler)] 2691.23
findings.implication: The mass-facing trade-union paper cost nearly 3x the internal doctrinal organ -- historical evidence that theoretical-labor investment concentrated on external agitation press over internal cadre formation, a real split ratio for how TL 'study allocation' divides between press and party-school spending.
findings.parameter: Toiler monthly cost $2,691.23 vs. Official Organ $976.17 (≈2.76:1), against total monthly party expenses of $15,735.98.

findings.case: WVO's 'Party Building and Anti-Revisionist Premises' (1975) — Bolshevik cadre-pool sizing via the Iskra model
findings.evidence: about 20 Marxist workers circles were united into the St. Petersburg League of Struggle for the Emancipation of the Working Class
findings.implication: A concrete historical cadre-aggregation figure (20 circles merging into one league) usable to calibrate the minimum cadre-count threshold before a cadre-party (vs. mass-party) formation is credible in the doctrine tree's resourcing model.
findings.parameter: ~20 independent study circles merged into one city-wide league as the first cadre-party building block (pre-1903 Russia).

findings.case: WVO's periodization of Bolshevik flow/ebb cycles as the basis for theory-vs-practice emphasis
findings.evidence: Such was the case with the Bolsheviks during the stormy and sometimes grey years after 1906 (1905-07 period of flow, 1908-11 period of ebb)
findings.implication: Gives a real 2-3 year alternation cadence between mass-upsurge ('flow', practice-weighted TL) and quiescence ('ebb', theory-weighted TL) — a candidate objective trigger for shifting TL allocation weight rather than leaving it to pure player choice.
findings.parameter: Flow period 1905-1907 (2 yrs) / ebb period 1908-1911 (3 yrs).

findings.case: Why the one successful mediator (Krzhizhanovsky, pseud. 'Travinsky') succeeded
findings.evidence: his attention has been occupied almost exclusively by that work and he has had no share in the dissensions
findings.implication: The one rectification success came from an actor structurally outside the factional theoretical-labor economy (spending cadre-time on practical work, not doctrine debate) — suggests a rectification bonus for cadre with low prior TL-spend on the contested doctrine axis.

findings.case: Workers Viewpoint Organization's polemic tracking the RCP's repeated doctrinal reversals on theory vs. practice, 1974-1977
findings.evidence: within the Party empiricism now represents a greater error than dogmatism
findings.implication: Documents an org oscillating between 'practice-practice-practice,' 'theory is principal,' and 'theory in its own right' inside a 3-year window — real-world evidence that reactive trunk-switching (gaming the tree rather than committing) gets punished by rival orgs as opportunism, supporting the no-optimal-path design law.
findings.parameter: Named 'Second Period' of theory/practice line-struggle: 1972-1975 (3 years).

findings.case: Workers' Party of Korea Sixth Congress (October 1980) convened after a year-long preparatory campaign
findings.evidence: adopted a resolution to convoke the Sixth Congress of the WPK in December 1980, marking the 35th anniversary of its founding
findings.implication: The Congress here was preceded by roughly a year of explicit preparation (a 100-day production campaign, monument construction, document drafting) before convening — congresses in this tradition are not sudden correction events but planned, resourced set-pieces. Six congresses across 35 years of party history (~1 every 5.8 years on average) gives a second real-world congress-cadence data point to weigh against CPC's fixed 5-year constitutional cadence.
findings.parameter: ~1-year preparatory cycle for a single Party Congress (resolution Dec 1979 -> Congress Oct 1980); average congress-to-congress interval implied by '6th Congress, 35th anniversary' ≈ 5.8 years.

findings.case: Yang Hsien-chen 'two combine into one' vs Mao 'one divides into two' purge, Talk on Questions of Philosophy, 1964
findings.evidence: there is not a single commune, a single hsien, a single department of the Central Committee, in which one cannot divide into two
findings.implication: A literal doctrinal line-struggle fought over the dialectics formula itself, centered on the Higher Party School (Yang was its President since 1955) — the exact site of institutional Theoretical Labor. Yang's 'harmony' formulation was publicly denounced starting July 1964 for 'minimizing the importance of struggle and contradiction,' making this a clean historical case of a Scientific-trunk doctrine node being purged for insufficient CLASS_ANALYSIS/struggle content.
findings.parameter: doctrine formulated early 1960s by the Higher Party School president, publicly purged from July 1964 — multi-year lag between deviant doctrine and correction

findings.case: East Wind and Seize the Time collectives dissolve to merge into the League of Revolutionary Struggle, Jan-April 1979
findings.evidence: unity can be achieved when struggle is conducted in a principled manner, relations are based on equality, criticism and self-criticism are practiced, and points of principle are distinguished from secondary differences
findings.implication: A contrast baseline to the OCIC/PRRWO coercive purge cases: self-criticism and dissolution CAN function as a healthy, voluntary correction/absorption mechanic when conducted principled and non-coercively — shows the same mechanic (self_criticism, org dissolution) has both a benign and a captured/weaponized mode.
findings.parameter: Two independent local collectives dissolved into the national org within ~3 months of each other (Jan and April 1979) via voluntary merger rather than purge — contrast timescale to the coercive OCIC/PRRWO cases.

findings.case: RCP Second Party Congress formally repudiates the 'center of gravity' doctrine, early 1978
findings.evidence: This formulation and the policy it represented was wrong. It was formally repudiated by the Second Congress of the RCP in early 1978.
findings.implication: A concrete, dated example of a Party Congress performing exactly a doctrine-tree 'trunk_shift'/rectification act — formally striking a load-bearing doctrinal phrase adopted at the Founding Congress — showing Congresses can retract specific doctrine nodes, not just reshuffle personnel.
findings.parameter: Founding Congress (1975) to Second Congress ('early 1978') ≈ 2.5-year congress cadence for the RCP's first two Congresses, with a named doctrine-node retraction as the corrective payload.

findings.case: 'Bombard the Headquarters' — Mao's big-character poster, August 1966
findings.evidence: Viewed in connection with the Right deviation in 1962 and the wrong tendency of 1964 which was 'Left' in form but Right in essence, shouldn't this make one wide awake?
findings.implication: The canonical case of a rectification campaign escalating into an attack on the Party's own apparatus by its supreme leader — framed as the third recurrence of an unresolved two-line struggle, suggesting a strikes-counter before the trap/faction-flip fires rather than a single-shot trigger.
findings.parameter: 3 escalating line-struggle episodes cited across 4 years (1962, 1964, 1966) before the trap/faction-flip event.

findings.case: 'Exceptionalism' as the named ideological lever behind Lovestone/Bittelman right-deviation errors, per the 1929 ECCI Address
findings.evidence: the ideological lever of right errors in the American Communist Party was the so-called theory of 'exceptionalism'
findings.implication: Names a precise, historically-attested trap mechanism distinct from adventurism: believing the general theory doesn't apply to one's own country pulls doctrine toward Right-deviation/Liquidationism regardless of surface-level Left rhetoric — a candidate hidden tag that silently discounts CLASS_ANALYSIS pull even while a faction claims militancy.

findings.case: 'Sugar-coated bullets' corruption warning at the Second Plenary Session, 1949
findings.evidence: they cannot withstand sugar-coated bullets; they will be defeated by sugar-coated bullets. We must guard against such a situation.
findings.implication: Explicit, contemporaneous naming of the Bureaucratic Degeneration trap at the moment of greatest trunk success — the trap fires from victory/power itself, arguing for a trap-check condition keyed to resource accumulation rather than only to crisis or defeat.

findings.case: 10th Congress resolution 'On the Syndicalist and Anarchist Deviation,' condemning the Workers' Opposition, 16 March 1921
findings.evidence: to consider propagation of these ideas as incompatible with membership in the Russian Communist Party
findings.implication: A real, dated, named-deviation-and-expulsion event — the mechanically-triggered Autonomist trap ending should mirror this: a Congress resolution that names the heresy, supplies the theoretical refutation, and converts further advocacy into an automatic expulsion state.
findings.parameter: Deviation named and banned in a single Congress resolution, same day (16 March 1921) as the Control Commission's founding — candidate 1-tick trap-trigger-plus-correction-institution pairing.

findings.case: 10th RCP Congress Control Commission report during the Kronstadt crisis (March 1921)
findings.evidence: Of 200 cases investigated by the Commission, 50 turned out to be of criminal character.
findings.implication: A real Party-Congress self_criticism/rectification body auditing corruption produced a concrete rate — usable as a tunable 'corruption discovery rate' inside the Party Congress correction mechanic.
findings.parameter: internal-corruption rate found by Congress Control Commission = 50/200 = 25%

findings.case: 1979 National Minority M-L Conference (OCIC) — skipped collective self-criticism
findings.evidence: Not having collective c/sc in effect stifled criticisms of the conference and the PC, and prevented participants from hearing each others' impressions.
findings.implication: A documented case where a real Congress-equivalent event skipped its mandatory collective self-criticism step under time pressure, producing distrust and a 'false unity' vote on resolutions. Supports making the self_criticism sub-step of the Party Congress mechanic non-skippable, with a real time/TL cost attached.

findings.case: Akimov and Martynov (Rabocheye Dyelo) walkout at the 31st sitting, 2nd Congress
findings.evidence: Comrades Akimov and Martynov refused to withdraw their statement, and walked out of the Congress, amidst the delegates' general cry: "Absolutely unwarranted!"
findings.implication: A minority-of-2 walkout is small in vote-share (~4%) but the walkout itself was treated as the illegitimate act, not the majority's ruling. Model defection as carrying a legitimacy/narrative cost to the defector, not just a resource loss to the party.
findings.parameter: 2 of 51 delegates (~4%) walked out after the Bureau ruled refusal-to-vote 'abnormal and impermissible' — candidate minimum defection-fraction for a walkout event.

findings.case: August 29th Movement's 1976 polemic describing a recurring pre-Congress pattern across OL, RU, and the Communist League
findings.evidence: the immediate reversion to standard right opportunism once the 'party' is constituted, along with the purging of those elements taken in temporarily by the 'left' feint
findings.implication: Documents Congress-mechanic gaming as a recognized historical pattern: orgs perform a temporary left tilt to absorb allies pre-Congress, then purge them post-Congress. Party Congress / trunk_shift mechanics need a gaming-resistance check (sustained practice, not just declared line, over multiple ticks).

findings.case: August 29th Movement's public self-criticism of its own 'political line is the key link' error (1977)
findings.evidence: As we can see, our whole analysis leading to 'political line as the key link' was shallow, arrogant, sectarian, subjective and metaphysical, and as such must be repudiated.
findings.implication: Real rectification retracted a specific named doctrinal position, not a generic guilt confession — the Party Congress 'self-criticism' action should let a player retract a specific prior tag-vector weight rather than only resetting a generic coherence stat.

findings.case: BASOC's diagnosis of 'ultra-centralism' as organizational prop of dogmatism/sectarianism (citing RU), 1980
findings.evidence: Leaders with minority viewpoints, for instance in RU, were unable to circulate their views to the membership as a whole, and members who shared a minority viewpoint were forbidden from meeting together.
findings.implication: The Bureaucratic-degeneration trap's operational trigger, per real practice, was a specific mechanism (banning minority-faction communication) rather than a vague 'high centralization' stat — suggests keying the Bureaucratic trap to an explicit 'internal dissent suppressed' flag.

findings.case: BWC Central Committee's handling of the 1974 factional revolt (vs. the 'revolutionary bloc')
findings.evidence: When the CC opposed this view and insisted on following the principle of 'curing the sickness to save the patient,' they branded it as 'centrism and conciliation' and then resigned.
findings.implication: 'Unity-criticism-unity'/curing-the-sickness is the historical rectification doctrine set against expulsion-first purges; a faction's refusal of correction-in-place (demanding amputation, then resigning to form a rival group) is itself a legible trap symptom worth flagging mechanically.

findings.case: Benjamin Gitlow's account of the post-expulsion narrowing of the International Labor Defense (ILD) into a Party clique, Jan. 1930
findings.evidence: Every means so well known to the Party wreckers, every trick and manipulation with which they are so familiar, was utilized in order to exclude the 'renegades' from the district and national conferences.
findings.implication: Written by an already-expelled Lovestoneite watching his own prior mass-organization work (the ILD) get systematically stripped from his faction via committee-packing -- a documented, named cost of losing a line struggle: expulsion doesn't just cost Party position, it costs access to the mass infrastructure the losing faction helped build, with no correction path shown. NOTE: assigned as 'Gitlow's account of the Lovestone expulsion' but the actual document is his account of the ILD's post-expulsion purge, not the expulsion event itself.

findings.case: Big-business patronage conditioning Hitler's 1931 rapprochement with finance capital on purging the Strasser brothers
findings.evidence: Schacht had made his cooperation with Hitler dependent upon the latter's sacrificing the Strasser brothers
findings.implication: Trap-correction can be externally imposed by material patrons rather than chosen by internal theoretical reasoning — a Party Congress rectification event triggered by resource dependency, not conviction, is historically real and worth modeling as a distinct correction pathway.
findings.parameter: Rhenish-Westphalian coal syndicate paid Gregor Strasser RM 10,000/month beginning spring 1931 specifically to keep an internal counterweight to Goebbels/Goering — a concrete cadre-patronage figure.

findings.case: Black Workers Congress 1975 split led by Don Williams — concrete defection-fraction numbers
findings.evidence: 98% of those persons belonging to his camp were also suspended from the organization
findings.implication: A rare case with hard defection-fraction data: the breakaway faction's own members overwhelmingly refused to renounce factionalism when given the chance (98% suspended vs. 2% resigned in good standing), and the combined dissident factions totaled under one-quarter of the org.
findings.parameter: Breakaway camp = <1/7 of total membership; combined dissident factions = <1/4 of total membership; 98% of breakaway members suspended vs. 2% resigned.

findings.case: Bolshevik Organizing Collective's polemic against the Revolutionary Workers League's self-criticism (1976)
findings.evidence: The essence of this is false repudiation and glossing over of errors.
findings.implication: Real organizations gamed self-criticism with generic phrase-mongering that cost nothing and changed nothing — the rectification/self-criticism action should be codeable as 'failed' (TL spent, no coherence or trap-distance gained) unless it names a specific line and a specific cost.

findings.case: Bolshevik co-optation position reversed the instant they became a Congress minority (Paragraph 12 debate, 2nd Congress)
findings.evidence: we began to advocate when we found ourselves in the minority on the question of the personal composition of the central bodies
findings.implication: Doctrine positions on procedure (majority thresholds, veto rights) are demonstrably power-contingent, not principled, even among the historical vanguard. The Doctrine Tree's 'no optimal path' law should let a trunk's own stated procedural doctrine flip depending on whether it currently holds Congress power.

findings.case: Browder naming the successor CPUSA line after his own ouster
findings.evidence: To give this whole development its full significance, one must identify it by its scientific name. It is the trend of anarchosyndicalism.
findings.implication: The expelled cadre of one trap (Liquidationism/'Browderism') diagnoses the correcting leadership's NEW line as a different named deviation — a documented overshoot-past-center dynamic the doctrine tree should allow: rectification from one trap can swing into an opposite-pole trap rather than landing on a stable center.
findings.parameter: Browder cites factionalism as 'for 15 years not a major problem of our Party' (i.e. a ~15-year stability window, 1930-1945) preceding this sudden post-Convention reversal within about 7 months (July 1945 Convention to Feb. 1946 expulsion).

findings.case: Bureaucratic degeneration named from inside the party by Kronstadt rebels (Izvestia #11, March 1921)
findings.evidence: The Communist Party has swelled greatly in numbers since it took power in its own hands, but it was lost a great deal in quality because of this.
findings.implication: A first-person historical description of the Bureaucratic-degeneration trap trigger: rapid membership growth without a matching coherence/screening mechanism dilutes the ideological core and severs leadership from the base.

findings.case: CPA Australia unity tickets and bureaucratic absorption into the ALP mainstream, mid-1950s (Liquidationism trunk)
findings.evidence: the CPA was now winning union positions not by distinguishing itself from the ALP officials, as it had generally done in decades past, but by merging into the mainstream.
findings.implication: Diagnostic signature for a Liquidationism trap: official positions/bureaucratic strength kept growing (union jobs won) while the party's actual working-class-recruiting growth stalled — power without political identity. A doctrine tree should be able to detect 'offices held' outpacing 'cadre organically recruited' as the trap's tell.

findings.case: CPA Australia's 1954 'Party Consolidation' — scapegoating individuals instead of structural rectification
findings.evidence: Blake and Henry were removed from the Secretariat even though they had 'given no sign of resuming their earlier activities.'
findings.implication: A real 'rectification' event that failed: pinning blame for systemic ultra-leftism/liquidationism on two named individuals left the underlying bureaucratic culture untouched and 'reinforced the authoritarianism of the remaining leaders.' Confirms that the Party Congress self_criticism action should have a failure mode — a rectification that targets persons rather than the doctrine/structure should not actually repair the trunk's trajectory.

findings.case: CPA Ironworkers-union ballot-rigging self-justified by consequentialist doctrine, early 1950s
findings.evidence: the long term objectives of the socialist movement could not be jeopardised by the errors or failures of our short term policies
findings.implication: Shows how a trunk's own theory can retroactively justify internal corruption (rigged ballots) as protecting the 'real' historic mission — and how this self-justification prevented any course-correction ('the rank and file would catch up... the perspectives of party and masses... drew further apart'), i.e. doctrinal self-justification actively blocks the Party Congress self-criticism mechanic from firing.

findings.case: CPA officials overriding a rank-and-file conference vote to end the 1949 coal strike
findings.evidence: The Northern District Board of Management, which they controlled, met within thirty minutes and rejected the decision of the conference
findings.implication: The mechanical trigger for the Bureaucratic Degeneration trap: leadership suppressing mass-line information and overriding a democratic result the instant it goes against them, with near-zero decision latency — a clean historical model for the trap's activation condition (override speed as a tell).
findings.parameter: Override decision made within 30 minutes of the conference vote it reversed — near-zero latency, usable as a threshold for detecting bureaucratic-override behavior distinct from ordinary Congress deliberation.

findings.case: CPUSA cadre Sam Darcy's 1937 pamphlet defending the Moscow show trials as eyewitness, calling for a parallel domestic purge
findings.evidence: must not remain passive but eliminate Trotsky and his agents root and branch out of America
findings.source: /media/user/data/old-hdd/old-hdd/www.marxists.org/history/usa/parties/cpusa/anti-trotsky/An Eye Witness at the Wreckers Trial - Darcy.pdf
findings.implication: A cadre's doctrinal commitment to defending a foreign purge directly generated a domestic vigilance/purge imperative -- shows how a trap's rhetoric self-propagates outward from its origin trunk into an entirely separate organization's internal culture, useful for modeling trap 'contagion' between allied factions.

findings.case: CPUSA in the United Auto Workers, from the 1937 Flint sit-down victory to Earl Browder's 1944 dissolution of the party
findings.evidence: the party liquidated its independent work and presence. The shop units, trade union fractions and shop papers were abolished; in 1944, Browder dissolved the party itself.
findings.implication: A textbook Liquidationism trap for the Reformist trunk: deep union integration and leadership access (not weakness) bred the abandonment of independent organization, ending in literal self-dissolution. Trigger condition should be 'access/prestige accumulated without maintained independent structure,' not simply accumulated reformist-tag points.
findings.parameter: Roughly a 10-year arc from peak mass influence (1937 Flint strike victory) to near-total expulsion (1946 Reuther's narrow win; ~12 'communist-dominated' unions later expelled from the CIO) — candidate incubation timescale for the Liquidationism trap.

findings.case: CPUSA's May 20, 1944 unanimous dissolution into the Communist Political Association (Browder's Liquidationism)
findings.evidence: I hereby move that the Communist Party of America be and hereby is dissolved... Motion to dissolve the Communist Party. Carried unanimously.
findings.implication: THE canonical historical Liquidationism trap -- and critically, it was triggered by unanimous grassroots consensus (every State/District convention had already endorsed it before the national vote), not top-down coercion, meaning traps can spring from genuine consensus-driven drift, consistent with 'no optimal path.'
findings.parameter: 220 regular + 173 alternate delegates from 44 states; party had existed 25 years (per Foster's opening remarks) before the trap sprang.

findings.case: Central Committee's own admission, two months into the Cultural Revolution, that its dispatched 'work teams' had massively misjudged the correct line
findings.evidence: Almost ninety per cent of the work teams throughout the whole country have committed general mistakes in their orientation.
findings.implication: A hard number for how badly even centrally-briefed, loyal executors can misjudge doctrine in real time -- supports a high 'doctrine execution noise' floor: a 90% institutional error rate is itself evidence that 'no optimal path' isn't achievable even by correctly-aligned agents, only by structural luck/adjustment.
findings.parameter: 90% mis-orientation rate among CR work teams (Aug 1966) -- candidate ceiling for an execution-noise coefficient.

findings.case: Chang Kuo-tao's 1935 assertion of 'independence' from the CCP Central Committee during the Long March, ending in his 1938 defection to the Kuomintang secret police
findings.evidence: asserted his 'independence' of the Central Committee of the Party and as a result 'asserted' himself into betraying the Party and became a Kuomintang agent.
findings.implication: A real trap-ending case for a Dissociation/Liquidationism-style trigger: the mechanical event is asserting factional autonomy from the central line, not a single betrayal act; the note records material cost ('heavy losses to the Fourth Front Army'), so the trap should be materially expensive, not just a faction-flip flag.
findings.parameter: ~3 years from initial split (1935) to full defection (spring 1938) -- candidate 'drift-to-defection' timescale for a slow trap.

findings.case: Chang Kuo-tao's favoritism-based cadre policy and eventual defection to the Kuomintang (through the 1935-38 period)
findings.evidence: Following the line of "appointing people by favouritism," he gathered personal favourites round himself to form a small clique, and in the end he turned traitor to the Party and decamped.
findings.implication: A named historical case where HOW cadre/Theoretical-Labor allocation was spent (patronage vs. merit) directly produced a faction-flip and defection to the enemy camp — supports gating the trap-ending on cadre-policy quality, not just doctrine content.

findings.case: Chang Wen-tien's (Lo Fu's) coerced capitulation letter after being purged as Peng Dehuai's ally, August 1959
findings.evidence: I must sever relations with my reactionary self of yesterday.
findings.implication: Shows rectification/self_criticism as a discursive toll extracted from a cadre re-entering good standing, distinct from a pure Theoretical-Labor cost — the correction mechanic should have a standing/trust cost dimension, not only a TL-point cost.

findings.case: Cheka executes prisoners the night before an abolition decree is published, January 1919
findings.evidence: That night, before the decree was published, the Cheka executed hundreds of prisoners, and, with White armies pressing from all sides the decree was later revoked
findings.implication: A security apparatus created to serve the revolution (Cheka, 1918) had, within a year, gained enough operational autonomy to defy a Party/Soviet decree outright — the precursor pattern to Bureaucratic Degeneration: an instrument-of-command node that outgrows political control under material siege conditions (Civil War), not through ideological choice.

findings.case: Chen Duxiu's 'Right capitulationism' and the 1927 counter-revolutionary massacre (Shanghai/Wuhan, April-July 1927)
findings.evidence: The total membership of the Party, which had grown to more than 60,000, fell to a little over 10,000.
findings.implication: A single catastrophic Right-line failure cost the Party ~83% of its membership in months. This is a concrete magnitude anchor for how severe a Reformist-trunk collapse (Liquidationism trap) should be when it actually triggers.
findings.parameter: Party membership 60,000 -> ~10,000 (83% loss) in a single catastrophic Right-line episode, 1927

findings.case: Civil War material collapse feeding bureaucratization, Russia 1918-1922
findings.evidence: By 1921, the economy was operating at about 20% of the pre-war level... In 1918-19, at least 12 million people died of typhus
findings.implication: Quantifies the 'scarcity' precondition of Bureaucratic-degeneration in extremis: a 5x contraction of output plus mass death from disease is the historical calibration point for how severe material collapse must be before the trap becomes near-inevitable rather than merely risked.
findings.parameter: Economic output ≈20% of pre-war level by 1921; 12M typhus deaths 1918-19, a further 20M affected by typhus/cholera/scarlet fever by 1922.

findings.case: Combat Bureaucracy, Commandism directive, Jan 5 1953
findings.evidence: a certain provincial people's government has sat on well over seventy thousand letters
findings.implication: A hard number for bureaucratic-blindness severity: tens of thousands of mass complaint-letters (a direct intelligence channel) left unread by a single provincial government. Gives a concrete magnitude for a Bureaucratic Degeneration trap trigger threshold tied to ignored mass-communication volume, distinct from mere sentiment.
findings.parameter: 70,000+ unread mass letters in one province (1953) as an observed bureaucratic-blindness magnitude

findings.case: Comintern leadership corruption timescale, Trotskyist retrospective analysis
findings.evidence: It had taken the Bolsheviks thirty years... to build the Bolshevik Party. A mere seven years after the revolution, the great leader and teacher of the Communist International had become its executioner
findings.implication: A striking build/rot asymmetry: ~30 years of cadre-building (theoretical labor accumulation) versus ~7 years to full bureaucratic capture and reversal of function. Directly usable as a decay-rate parameter for the Bureaucratic Degeneration trap — rot should compound roughly 4x faster than the accumulation it consumes.
findings.parameter: build:rot ratio ≈ 30 years (Bolshevik Party formation) : 7 years (Comintern capture, 1917 revolution → 1924 corruption)

findings.case: Communist Party of Peru - Shining Path's (Gonzalo) doctrine of universal revolutionary violence directed at the peasantry
findings.evidence: The violence that the Shining Path claims to derive from Marxism-Leninism is primarily directed towards the Peruvian peasantry, a historically subjugated class.
findings.implication: A real historical Insurrectionist/Adventurism trap case where the trapped faction's violence turned on its own claimed social base rather than the state — a distinct failure signature from mere military defeat: legitimacy destroyed via loss of the very mass relationship (M_r) the movement depended on, echoing the EH thesis directly.

findings.case: Communist Workers' Party's 1979 Greenboro anti-Klan march, cited as Adventurism by BASOC (1980)
findings.evidence: Provocative and militant slogans of 'Death to the Klan!' were combined with no preparation for an assault upon the march itself, with tragic consequences.
findings.implication: Direct historical anchor for the Insurrectionist trunk's Adventurism trap ending: maximalist agitational slogans issued without material/organizational readiness produced a real, fatal ambush (the Greensboro massacre). Adventurism trigger should key off high militancy-tag combined with low organizational-readiness, not militancy alone.

findings.case: Contemporary ML polemic against Shining Path (Sendero Luminoso) adventurism
findings.evidence: Exploding bombs in public places and hanging dead dogs against Deng Xiaoping is not revolutionary praxis, it's petty-bourgeois adventurism.
findings.implication: A present-day case where a self-described Maoist current is judged by other MLs as having fallen into the Adventurism trap specifically for spectacle-violence divorced from mass organizing — shows the label remains an active diagnostic category, reinforcing "Adventurism" as the correct trap name for the Insurrectionist trunk.

findings.case: Control Commission mandate, 10th Congress, 1921
findings.evidence: dissemination of unfounded and unsubstantiated rumours and insinuations that bring disgrace to the party or to individual party members
findings.implication: The historical correction body's mandate explicitly includes counter-disinformation/counter-slander duties inside the party — a crossover for a rectification mechanic that also polices internal informant/provocateur suspicion, not just doctrinal deviation.

findings.case: Deng Zihui's (Teng Tzu-hui's) 'Right deviationist' economic line and the roster of truly incorrigible defectors, 1955
findings.evidence: the incorrigible are few, such as Chen Tu-hsiu, Chang Kuo-tao, Kao Kang, Jao Shu-shih and also Chen Kuang and Tai Chi-ying.
findings.implication: Named senior cadre (Deng Zihui) with a genuinely wrong Right-line ('four big freedoms') was rectified via public self-criticism, not expelled; only a handful of named individuals across three decades of CCP history are treated as truly beyond recovery. The trap/faction-flip ending should be rare relative to recoverable rectification.
findings.parameter: ~6 named 'incorrigible' defectors across ~30 years of top CCP leadership -- a low irrecoverable-defection fraction

findings.case: Disappearance of CPUSA cadre Juliet Stuart Poyntz, June 1937
findings.evidence: she could no longer approve of things under the Stalin regime in Russia, that she was withdrawing from party activity
findings.implication: A senior cadre's ideological break from the party preceded catastrophic, irreversible cadre loss (disappearance/presumed liquidation) within roughly a month — grounds a hard timescale for how fast dissent can convert into total cadre loss under a Scientific-trunk Bureaucratic-degeneration trap.
findings.parameter: ~1 month from Poyntz's May 1937 break with the party to her early-June 1937 disappearance — candidate defection-to-liquidation timescale for the most severe trap outcome.

findings.case: Dissolution of the Yuzhny Rabochy group at the 2nd RSDLP Congress, August 1903
findings.evidence: The result of the vote was thirty-one for, five against and five abstentions
findings.implication: A real congress vote forcibly absorbing a sub-line ('historical entity' with its own circle loyalty) into the unified party — concrete precedent for a Party-Congress trunk_shift mechanic that resolves a faction by vote rather than letting it coexist indefinitely.
findings.parameter: congress vote margin to dissolve a faction = 31 for / 5 against / 5 abstain (~75.6% supermajority)

findings.case: Earl Browder / CPUSA dissolution, 1944 (Teheran line)
findings.evidence: Browder engineered the dissolution of the CPUSA, instead creating the Communist Political Association
findings.implication: Historical template for the Reformist trunk's terminal 'Liquidationism' trap: total organizational dissolution into class-collaborationist politics under cover of 'changed conditions.' The 36-year recovery struggle that followed (per the same document) is the cost of falling in.
findings.parameter: CPUSA dissolved 1944, reconstituted 1945 (~1 yr) but per the author 'liquidationism was never exposed and routed' — 36 years cited as the ensuing recovery struggle (1944-1980).

findings.case: Earl Browder's Feb. 1946 appeal against expulsion for 'liquidationism/Browderism'
findings.evidence: I understand that the motion was rejected by a vote of 64 to 52, but that later on the question was re-opened in order to refer the matter to the Yonkers Club.
findings.implication: Even a leader already stripped of national office and publicly denounced survived a first local expulsion vote by a modest margin (55%/45%) — rectification/trap-correction votes should be modeled as contested, not automatic landslides, even after national leadership has already turned.
findings.parameter: 64-52 vote (55.2% vs 44.8%) against immediate expulsion at first local-club vote, later reopened — candidate contested-rectification vote-split baseline.

findings.case: Earl Browder's dissolution of the CPUSA (May 1944) on a 'common interest between the proletariat and the big bourgeoisie' theory, denounced by the Party's own special convention and reversed
findings.evidence: Browder in May 1944 presided over the dissolution of the Communist Party of the U.S.A., the party of the U.S. proletariat, and formed a non-Party organization
findings.implication: A textbook, literal Liquidationism trap-ending -- the party dissolving itself as the mechanically-terminal event, triggered by exactly the class-collaborationist reasoning the DT thesis warns against. Correction came from below (Party opposition led by Foster), not from Browder himself.
findings.parameter: dissolution (May 1944) -> Party's denunciation resolution (June 1945, ~13 months) -> Browder's formal expulsion (Feb 1946, ~21 months after dissolution) -- candidate trap-to-correction timescale.

findings.case: Earl Browder's dissolution of the CPUSA into the Communist Political Association (May 1944) and its reversal after Jacques Duclos's critique (April-July 1945)
findings.evidence: I hereby move that the Communist Party of America be and hereby is dissolved
findings.source: /media/user/data/old-hdd/old-hdd/prolewiki/Exports/Library/Library_History of the Communist Party of the United States.txt
findings.implication: The clean historical archetype for the Liquidationism trap ending: an elected leader's opportunist 'class peace' doctrine culminates in literally voting the vanguard party out of existence, with the dissolution vote itself lasting only minutes.
findings.parameter: Dissolution-to-full-correction latency ≈ 14 months (May 1944 dissolution -> July 26-28 1945 reconstitution).

findings.case: Four Clean-ups Movement, Mao diagnosing cadre bureaucratization, 1965
findings.evidence: Now that you have founded a party, entered cities, and become bureaucrats, you are no longer adept at launching mass movements
findings.implication: A crisp, dated trigger statement for the Bureaucratic Degeneration trap: victory + urbanization + institutionalization directly causes loss of mass-organizing skill, framed by Mao himself as the mechanism (not merely a side effect).

findings.case: Freedom Road Socialist Organization retrospective on the New Communist Movement's collapse (Dennis O'Neil, 2000)
findings.evidence: Ultra-left and left opportunist errors were the main factor in the massive collapse of the new communist movement. They took such forms as arrogance, sectarianism and dogmatism.
findings.implication: The dominant historical trap-trigger was internally-generated overconfidence (arrogance/dogmatism), not external repression — the doctrine tree's trap-trigger weighting should bias toward self-inflicted rigidity over state action.

findings.case: Full Bund walkout from the 2nd RSDLP Congress, 1903, over Paragraph 2 (federation)
findings.evidence: it was the rejection of this Paragraph 2 that caused the Bund to withdraw from the Party
findings.implication: A single up-or-down vote on the federation-vs-unity axis (the Autonomist trunk's core tension) triggered an entire national-organization faction's total exit. Model a hard threshold where an autonomy-vs-federation vote below a set margin instantly expels an affiliated bloc rather than a gradual drift.
findings.parameter: One binary vote → 100% of one delegation's votes (~5/51, ~10%) exited the party immediately — candidate instant-defection trigger for the Autonomist trunk's federation axis.

findings.case: Full Cultural Revolution trap-cascade duration, May 1966 to October 1976, versus the additional lag to formal doctrinal resolution
findings.evidence: The 'cultural revolution', which lasted from May 1966 to October 1976, was responsible for the most severe setback and the heaviest losses suffered by the Party, the state and the people since the founding of the People's Republic.
findings.implication: A triggered trap can run for a full decade before the faction driving it is forcibly removed (Gang of Four arrested Oct 1976), and it takes still longer before doctrine is formally rectified. Trap cascades should have a long minimum duration and a separate, longer 'full resolution' timescale from 'power broken.'
findings.parameter: Cascade to power-break: 10 years (May 1966-Oct 1976). Power-break to correction start: 2 years (Third Plenum, Dec 1978). Correction start to doctrinal resolution: 2.5 years (June 1981). Total trigger-to-resolution: ~15 years.

findings.case: Garvey's UNIA salary structure as material capture of a nationalist trunk, per C. Lorenzo, 1921
findings.evidence: Garvey gets $12,000 a year as President-General of the UNIA, and $10,000 more as President of the Black Star Line, Inc.
findings.implication: Concrete historical figures showing a nationalist-trunk leader converting liberation rhetoric into personal enrichment — the material mechanism underlying Strasserism/PatSoc-endpoint drift. A leader-compensation-to-treasury ratio is a plausible mechanical trigger for the PatSoc pipeline's final elite-capture node.
findings.parameter: $12,000/yr UNIA president salary + $10,000/yr Black Star Line salary (total $22,000/yr) vs. ordinary UNIA officer salaries of $3,000/yr, c. 1921.

findings.case: Get Organized! speech, naming bureaucratic/warlord isolation as a correctable fault, 1943
findings.evidence: Whether it is the tendency towards bureaucracy in local work or towards warlordism in army work, the fault is of the same nature, namely, isolation from the masses
findings.implication: Both cadre-facing (bureaucratism) and army-facing (warlordism) deviations are unified under one diagnosis — isolation from the masses — and both are explicitly stated to be correctable via criticism/self-criticism, giving the Bureaucratic Degeneration trap a documented escape hatch (unlike the fixed PatSoc-pipeline faction flip).

findings.case: Global Marxist-Leninist realignment after the fall of the Gang of Four, Dec 1976
findings.evidence: The crushing of the 'gang of four,' who tried to seize power after Chairman Mao died, has been hailed by communists all over the world as a momentous achievement.
findings.implication: A trunk-shift/realignment triggered by an exogenous shock in a reference state (China) rather than domestic line-struggle — dozens of parties worldwide (Albania's Hoxha, Australia's E.F. Hill, Germany's KPD) pivoted their public line within one news cycle. Model an external-shock trunk-shift trigger distinct from the slow internal-deliberation Congress path — note this same Hoxha-aligned bloc reversed hard against China within ~2 years (see the Three-Worlds finding), illustrating doctrine-tree whiplash driven by external referents.

findings.case: Gramsci on party bureaucracy/general-staff ossification, Prison Notebooks Q7 §77 (1930-31)
findings.evidence: Quest'ultima è la forza consuetudinaria più pericolosa: se essa si organizza come corpo a sé, solidale e indipendente, il partito finisce con l'anacronizzarsi.
findings.implication: Quote translates roughly as: 'The latter [the party bureaucracy] is the most dangerous force of habit: if it organizes itself as a body unto itself, solidary and independent, the party ends up becoming anachronistic.' This is Gramsci's own diagnosis of bureaucratic ossification and is the direct theoretical source for the Scientific trunk's Bureaucratic-degeneration trap — it should fire when apparatus/cadre-control nodes accumulate without corresponding mass-base nodes.

findings.case: Great Leap Forward steel-target collapse across successive 1958-59 conferences (Peitaiho, Wuchang, Shanghai, Lushan), in Mao's own 1961-62 retrospective
findings.evidence: Steel output for 1959 was set at 30 million tons at Peitaiho. The Wuchang meeting lowered this to 20 million.
findings.implication: A crown example of an Insurrectionist/Adventurist trap in its literal death spiral: a headline target is deflated in four discrete steps across four successive conferences within about a year, each step itself a mini rectification — a strong template for how a game trap should decay its own targets stepwise once triggered rather than snapping to a single failure state.
findings.parameter: Target walkback: 30M -> 20M -> 16.5M -> 13M tons across 4 conferences within roughly one year (mid-1958 to June 1959) — candidate TRAP_TARGET_DEFLATION_STEPS = 4, cumulative reduction ≈ 57%

findings.case: Hu Yaobang's 1981 60th-anniversary speech diagnosing Mao's late-life errors that produced the Cultural Revolution
findings.evidence: he became over-confident and more and more divorced from reality and the masses and, in particular, from the Party's collective leadership
findings.implication: Names the precise trap-trigger mechanism for a 'Bureaucratic degeneration' trunk-ending: loss of contact with the masses combined with suppression of collective correction — i.e., the trap is triggered by the leadership itself defeating the game's own correction mechanic (collective leadership), not by an external shock.

findings.case: Hundred Flowers policy criteria for 'fragrant flowers' vs. 'poisonous weeds', 1957
findings.evidence: we must carefully distinguish between what is really a poisonous weed and what is really a fragrant flower
findings.implication: A doctrine tree needs an explicit, stated discrimination test for whether a given ideological expression is generative or corrosive rather than banning dissent outright — this is a historical precedent for a defusal mechanic (criteria-based) as opposed to blunt suppression, which the text explicitly says backfires.

findings.case: IWW constitutional anti-bureaucratization rule limiting officer terms, described in Industrial Pioneer, May 1921
findings.evidence: There are no permanent officials, the term of office being limited to one year. Ex-officials must work at least six months at the point of production before they are eligible to hold office again.
findings.implication: A real, tested structural counter-mechanic to Bureaucratic degeneration: mandatory 1-year term limits plus a 6-month rank-and-file cooling-off requirement before re-election. Gives concrete numeric knobs for an Autonomist-trunk resistance stat against that trap.
findings.parameter: Officer term limit: 1 year; mandatory cooling-off at the point of production before re-eligibility: 6 months.

findings.case: January 1967 New Year editorial, one year after the Cultural Revolution's formal trigger, still calling to escalate rather than correct
findings.evidence: These people reversed right and wrong, juggled black and white, encircled and suppressed revolutionaries, clamped down on different views, practised white terror and applauded themselves for doing so.
findings.implication: One year after trigger the cascade is still escalating (the editorial's title literally calls to 'carry through to the end'), not correcting. Design should enforce a minimum 'commitment latency' -- a floor of turns/ticks after a trap triggers before any correction impulse becomes available at all.
findings.parameter: At t+1 year (Jan 1967) post-trigger (May 1966), escalation rhetoric still rising -- candidate floor of >=1 year (in-game ticks) before a trap becomes escapable.

findings.case: John Pepper (Jozsef Pogany), Comintern operative central to CPUSA's 1920s factional fights, executed in Stalin's Great Purge
findings.evidence: Arrested 29 July 1937. Sentenced to be shot 8 February 1938 by the Military Collegium of the Supreme Court of the USSR on charges of participation in a counter-revolutionary organization.
findings.implication: Surviving one trunk's factional struggle (Foster-Ruthenberg fight) did not protect a cadre from a LATER, unrelated trap (Moscow purge) -- traps should be modeled as sequential/compounding across a cadre's career rather than single resolved-or-not events.
findings.parameter: Arrest-to-execution: ~194 days (~6.4 months); posthumous rehabilitation: 30 May 1956, ~18 years 10 months after execution -- concrete purge-trap terminal and reversal timescales.

findings.case: KKE (Communist Party of Greece) factional purges, 1920s
findings.evidence: the party threw out of its ranks members considered to be of the 'menshevik' mould and later on, when things in Comintern got settled up, Trotskyists followed them out
findings.implication: Sequential purges (right-deviationists first, left-oppositionists later, timed to match the Comintern's OWN internal line-settlement) show that a party congress's rectification power can be exercised twice in the same direction against different wings over time — a serial trap-triggering mechanic rather than a single binary event, and purge timing is externally paced by the parent international, not internal readiness.

findings.case: KKE/EAM and the 1944 power question, Greek Civil War prelude
findings.evidence: it saw EAM gaining the support of 70% of the Greeks - it didn't know what to do with it
findings.implication: A concrete case where measured mass support (I_c reading of 70%, well above any Water threshold) still produced organizational paralysis and eventual retreat rather than a seizure of power — proof that high Intel Confidence alone doesn't prevent a Liquidationism-style trap if the doctrine/leadership lacks a theorized plan for what to do with high receptivity. Useful counter-example: M_r/I_c and DT tag-vector readiness are independent axes.
findings.parameter: EAM mass support ≈ 70% of the Greek population (self-reported, 1944), yet KKE chose retreat over a bid for power

findings.case: KPD March Action, central Germany, March 1921 (Bela Kun's putsch)
findings.evidence: The call led to little response, and as a result the Communists suffered serious casualties and many of their members and supporters were arrested. Following this adventurist action, there was a decline in the membership of the KPD.
findings.implication: Canonical Insurrectionist-trunk Adventurism trap: a Comintern-directed premature uprising against low mass receptivity produced casualties, arrests, and membership decline — the exact 'act without relationship' failure mode the doctrine tree's trap is meant to punish.

findings.case: KPD paralysis during the 1923 Ruhr crisis (aftershock of 1921)
findings.evidence: Having 'burnt its fingers' in March 1921, the KPD hesitated to respond decisively to this revolutionary situation.
findings.implication: A trap's damage doesn't end at the immediate cost — the 1921 Adventurism scar produced excess caution that caused a DIFFERENT failure (missed revolutionary window, effectively a Liquidationist symptom) two years later. Design point: trap history should apply a temporary action-hesitancy penalty/bias toward the opposite trunk, not just a one-time cost.
findings.parameter: ~2.5-year lag between the 1921 trap and its opposite-direction consequence (October 1923 missed uprising).

findings.case: KPD trade-union cadre Dahlem defends the RGO's maximalist, no-united-front line, RILU Central Council 1932
findings.evidence: No, there can be no united front with the leaders of the Social Democracy and the ADGB, with the assistants and henchmen of fascism.
findings.implication: Concrete illustration of a doctrine node that forbids the one tactically correct move (transitional/united-front demands) as a matter of principle — precisely the 'no optimal path' design law in action: the Adventurist trunk structurally cannot take the move that would save it, by its own internal logic.

findings.case: Khrushchev's 'Secret Speech' (20th Congress, Feb 1956) and the June 1957 'Anti-Party Group' purge, USSR
findings.evidence: The Central Committee then purged Malenkov, Molotov, and Kaganovich from the Presidium.
findings.implication: Shows a corrective congress mechanism being captured by the drifting trunk itself: the anti-revisionist faction won a Presidium vote but lost when Khrushchev invoked the larger Central Committee instead - the correction mechanism can be gamed by whoever controls the bigger body, not just used against the drifter.

findings.case: Lenin diagnosing the psychological root of liquidationism, 1911
findings.evidence: The connection between liquidationism and the general philistine mood of "weariness" is obvious.
findings.implication: Lenin frames the drift into liquidationism as caused by cadre fatigue/demoralization rather than conscious betrayal ('not just "weary" but... treacherous as well' follows only after prolonged inaction) -- suggests a 'weariness/attrition' meter as the actual driver that pushes a faction toward the Liquidationism trap, with treachery as the downstream label.

findings.case: Lenin's 'subservience to spontaneity' thesis linking Economism and Terrorism as mirror-image failures (WITBD Ch.3 sec. D, 1901)
findings.evidence: both forms of subservience to spontaneity we have mentioned are nothing but the beginning of the implementation of the notorious Credo programme
findings.implication: Two opposite-looking deviations (tailist reformism and adventurist terrorism) are named as the SAME underlying failure approached from different trunks — historical grounding for 'no optimal path': abandoning conscious theoretical leadership converges on a trap regardless of drift direction.

findings.case: Lenin's 1914 polemic defining adventurist groups (Svoboda, Borba, and others, 1901-1914 Russia)
findings.evidence: they proved adventurist in the sense that they had no stable or serious principles, programme, tactics, organisation, and no roots among the masses
findings.implication: This is the primary-source definition to hang the Insurrectionist trunk's Adventurism trap on: the diagnostic isn't 'too violent' but 'no roots among the masses' plus 'no stable principles' — i.e., a doctrine node vector that spikes tactics/violence tags while cadre-network/M_r-linked tags stay near zero should be the trigger condition.

findings.case: Lenin's critique of Socialist-Revolutionary theoretical incoherence, 'Revolutionary Adventurism II' (1902)
findings.evidence: their greatest skill consists in their ability to speak without saying anything
findings.implication: Defines adventurism not only as premature armed action but as theoretical vacancy (freedom from class analysis) masquerading as radicalism — broadens the trap trigger to a 'CLASS_ANALYSIS score below threshold AND radical rhetoric above threshold' condition distinct from Marighella's purely tactical sins.

findings.case: Lenin's definition of Socialist-Revolutionary 'adventurism' (Basic Thesis Against the SRs, Nov-Dec 1902)
findings.evidence: an internally contradictory, unprincipled, unstable trend which conceals emptiness behind high-sounding promises and is therefore inevitably doomed to bankruptcy
findings.implication: Gives a precise, quotable historical template for an Insurrectionist-trunk Adventurism trap ending: unprincipled tactics dressed in maximalist rhetoric, structurally doomed regardless of the sincerity of the cadre involved.

findings.case: Lenin's own definition of "adventurism," 1914
findings.evidence: tiny groups, which sprang up for an hour, for several months, with no roots whatever among the masses (and politics without the masses are adventurist politics).
findings.implication: Lenin's operational definition of "adventurist" is explicitly about mass-rootlessness and ephemerality, not violence per se — the Adventurism trap should fire on low mass-relationship combined with high doctrinal-purchase velocity, tying the DT trap directly to the EH mechanic's M_r.
findings.parameter: The correct-line "Pravdist" trend united "four-fifths" of class-conscious workers (5,300 of 6,700 workers' groups) over two-and-a-half years (1912-14) — candidate correct-line adoption-rate/timescale versus adventurist groups' near-instant collapse.

findings.case: Lenin's right vs. 'left' liquidationism, RSDLP 1907-1912 (cited 1980 by CPUSA/ML)
findings.evidence: Liquidationism from the right leads to liberal, bourgeois politics. Liquidationism from the "left" leads to anarchy and adventurism.
findings.implication: Directly grounds the 'no optimal path' design law: two opposite, symmetrically fatal deviations (right=Reformist/liberal capitulation, left=Adventurist/sectarian isolation) bracket the only viable path (illegal nuclei + legal strong-points), which is narrow and requires constant correction rather than being a stable equilibrium.

findings.case: Lenin/Stalin doctrine of open self-criticism as the antidote to bureaucratic ossification
findings.evidence: The attitude of a political party toward its own mistakes is one of the most important and surest criteria of the seriousness of the party
findings.source: /media/user/data/old-hdd/old-hdd/prolewiki/Exports/Library/Library_On the shortcomings of party work and measures to eliminate Trotskyist and other double-dealers_Concluding Speech.txt
findings.implication: This grounds 'self_criticism' as a Party Congress correction action distinct from 'rectification' or 'trunk_shift': it is specifically about admitting error publicly and using it to train cadre, contrasted explicitly against covering up mistakes to 'spare' cadre morale (which the text calls a 'rotten and dangerous theory').

findings.case: Loss of the Central Soviet Area, Kiangsi, during the Fifth Encirclement Campaign (1934, 'pure defensism'/dogmatist line of the returned-students leadership)
findings.evidence: the Red Army was reduced from 300,000 to a few tens of thousands, the membership of the Chinese Communist Party fell from 300,000 to a few tens of thousands
findings.implication: This is a directly quantified adventurism-then-dogmatism trap: a documented ~90%+ attrition of both army and cadre base is a hard upper bound for how punishing the engine's most severe trunk-trap (analogous to Insurrectionist/Adventurism) should be allowed to get before a Congress-correction fires.
findings.parameter: TRAP_ATTRITION_FRACTION ≈ 0.9 (Red Army and Party membership both fell from 300,000 to 'a few tens of thousands', 1934-35)

findings.case: Lovestone's June 1937 reckoning with the Tukhachevsky/Gamarnik/Rudzutak/Yagoda purges
findings.evidence: In a regime under which people are compelled to say and repeat with synthetic glee what they do not mean, what they actually disapprove, it is impossible to avoid a situation in which people make it a practice of not meaning what they say.
findings.implication: An expelled cadre's own theoretical reckoning identifies enforced doctrinal conformity as the generative mechanism of the very 'betrayal' the regime later punishes — a clean mechanical description of the Bureaucratic-degeneration trap: coerced assent breeds the disloyalty it fears.

findings.case: Lovestone-faction account of the International Labor Defense's capture by a party clique, Jan 1930
findings.evidence: the national convention would amend the constitution of the ILD by eliminating the declaration that the ILD is a non-partisan organization.
findings.implication: A mass organization was procedurally converted from a broad-class institution into a narrow factional instrument via constitutional amendment -- a clean trigger condition for the Bureaucratic-degeneration trap: using internal rule-making power to exclude dissidents while claiming organizational continuity.

findings.case: Lovestoneite ('International Communist Opposition') critique of the CPSU's decade-long failure to reabsorb its opposition, 1937
findings.evidence: it has not succeeded in dissolving the various oppositional movements and reabsorbing their elements into the party, but rather in consolidating and embittering them
findings.implication: Documents a FAILED rectification loop: repeated expulsion-only correction (never reintegration) narrows the leadership base and hardens opposition each cycle. Design law: a Party Congress rectification that only expels should carry an escalating future-instability cost, distinct from a rectification that reintegrates.

findings.case: Lushan Plenum 1959: Peng Dehuai's dissent derails a rectification conference; the Party institutionalizes a 'Special Case Review Commission' to investigate the resulting purge
findings.evidence: We have now formed two Special Case Review Commissions to clarify problems, to elucidate the problems and then convince people.
findings.implication: A rectification/Congress mechanic can be captured mid-session by a factional purge, freezing the original corrective agenda ('the work was affected') and spawning a separate, slower adjudication track for the purged. Model trap capture as displacing -- not just failing -- the intended correction action.
findings.parameter: Purged officials named as a fixed 4-person 'Anti-Party Group' plus 1 more under linked investigation (5 total); Mao states the case review must wait 'until the next Plenum or the one after that' -- appeal/reversal latency measured in PLENUMS (years), not ticks.

findings.case: MLOC / 'CPUSA(M-L)' (Barry Weisberg), 1975-1983
findings.evidence: in December 1978, he declared that his grouplet was the political party of the American working class, the "Communist Party of the USA/Marxist-Leninist."
