findings.implication: A complete lifecycle case for the Reformist/Liquidationist trap: grouplet declares itself vanguard party, later admits 'no Marxist-Leninist center' exists, then dissolves via a formal Second Congress with members left to 'float as individuals' into the Democratic Party's left wing rather than correcting course — the trap has no self-repair path once fully entered.
findings.parameter: 1975 founding -> Dec 1978 self-declared party (3 yrs) -> Jan 1981 public admission of failure -> mid-June 1983 dissolution via Second Congress (8-year total lifespan).

findings.case: Mao's warning that revisionism/Right opportunism is more dangerous than dogmatism, 1957
findings.evidence: Revisionism, or Right opportunism, is a bourgeois trend of thought that is even more dangerous than dogmatism.
findings.implication: Confirms the doctrine tree's 'no optimal path' design law from primary theory itself: the two opposite-trunk failure modes (dogmatic rigidity vs. revisionist liquidation) are both named as dangers by the same authority in the same document, so neither trunk direction can be treated as simply safer.

findings.case: March Action 1921 defeat -- membership and casualty cost of the adventurist insurrection
findings.evidence: The KPD's membership fell to 180,000 and later rose again to over 200,000 in 1922.
findings.implication: Concrete cost/recovery-time numbers for an Adventurism-trap trigger: a real mass party lost membership after a top-down insurrectionary defeat but partially rebuilt within about a year.
findings.parameter: Membership fell then recovered to 200,000+ within ~1 year (1922); separately, 34 workers killed and 1,500 taken prisoner when police retook the occupied Leuna works -- candidate trap-cost/recovery-tick coefficients.

findings.case: Marighella's 'Seven Sins of the Urban Guerrilla' (1969)
findings.evidence: The fifth sin of the urban guerrilla is rash action. The guerrilla who commits this sin loses patience, suffers an attack of nerves, does not wait for anything, and impetuously throws himself into action, suffering untold defeats.
findings.implication: A ready-made 7-item checklist (inexperience, boasting, vanity, overestimating strength, rash action, attacking when the enemy is angriest, failure to plan) that maps directly onto Adventurism trap trigger conditions for the Insurrectionist trunk.
findings.parameter: 7 discrete named failure-conditions, usable as parallel Adventurism-risk boolean/threshold checks

findings.case: Martynov and Akimov walk out of the Second RSDLP Congress, August 1903
findings.evidence: The Congress invited Akimov and Martynov to withdraw their statement, but they rejected the proposal and walked out.
findings.implication: Documents a Party Congress rectification offer being explicitly refused, ending in walkout/split rather than reintegration — the historical prototype for a Congress correction attempt that fails, producing a permanent faction break instead of a self_criticism resolution.

findings.case: Marxist-Leninist Organizing Committee Central Committee's 1977 self-rectification of a 'left'-opportunist tendency inherited from the Black Workers Congress
findings.evidence: They remain '100% pure Bolsheviks', in a closet in New York or Los Angeles
findings.implication: Describes the Insurrectionist/Scientific over-purity trap concretely: orgs that maximize doctrinal correctness while having zero mass presence, self-described by their own later leadership as a 'left' line requiring formal CC-level rectification — a real rectification-mechanic precedent.
findings.parameter: Cited timescale for scientific-socialism/working-class fusion 'through various forms': 12 years.

findings.case: May 18, 1929 CC Decision removing both Lovestone and Bittelman from Party work
findings.evidence: Comrades Lovestone and Bittelman, as extreme factionalists of the Majority and Minority, to be removed for a time from work in the American Communist Party
findings.implication: The rectification event punished BOTH faction leaders symmetrically regardless of who held the nominal majority — a real precedent that a Congress/rectification mechanic should impose real leadership-cost on winning and losing trunks alike, preventing 'win the internal fight, pay no price' degenerate play consistent with the 'no optimal path' law.

findings.case: Military equalitarianism ('dividing the forces into six routes, resisting on all fronts') during the Fifth Counter-Campaign, 1934
findings.evidence: Military equalitarianism reached its extreme point in our fifth counter-campaign against "encirclement and suppression" in 1934.
findings.implication: A specific, nameable doctrinal choice (spread-thin defense-everywhere) is identified as the proximate trigger of the trap, distinct from the underlying dogmatism — good precedent for a doctrine-tree node whose selection mechanically triggers a trap check rather than the trap being a vague accumulated-tag threshold.

findings.case: NCM's homophobic exclusion policy, adopted in imitation of Comintern/Chinese/Cuban 'tradition' (per family-tree retrospective)
findings.evidence: Instead of practicing dialectics and the mass line, they tailed and reinforced backward sentiments among the people.
findings.implication: An imported doctrinal tag (borrowed foreign-line orthodoxy) directly cost mass base and cadre — concrete precedent that a tag vector's rigidity has a measurable cadre-attrition cost, not just an abstract coherence score.

findings.case: OC West Majority self-criticism presentation on the 'racist errors' campaign, July 1980
findings.evidence: We must break the racist conspiracy that divides the OCIC, our movement and the working class, and commit ourselves to a thorough-going process of criticism and self-criticism.
findings.implication: A primary-source instance of self-criticism escalating into a loyalty-confession ritual (contemporaneous with, and part of, the OCIC purge chain above) — shows the 'self_criticism' correction mechanic can be captured and weaponized into a consolidation tool rather than functioning as genuine rectification.

findings.case: October League's 'party without a program' sequencing error, criticized by WVO 1976-77
findings.evidence: The OL's call to form a party without a program, congress or central committee and democratic centralism, was no small slip.
findings.implication: Names a process/sequencing choice (build organizational apparatus before doctrine coheres into a program) as itself trap-triggering, independent of tag-vector content — supports letting a Doctrine Tree trap fire on structural/sequencing violations (e.g., purchasing organizational nodes before required doctrine prerequisites), not only on ideological-content thresholds.

findings.case: October League's cynical 'purge' of M. Nicholaus, mid-1970s
findings.evidence: This "purge" had no principled basis at all. Ask the Klonsky renegade clique who iced Nicholaus out, why they had to get rid of him
findings.implication: A documented case of a rectification/self-criticism mechanism used cosmetically — expelling a cadre member to hide the leadership's own continuity with a discredited line rather than genuinely correcting course. Argues for a mechanical distinction between cosmetic and substantive Party-Congress self-criticism outcomes.
findings.parameter: ~2 years (1975-1977) the leadership clique had been quietly trying to 'dissociate' from its own 1972-75 line before the purge.

findings.case: Old Bolshevik defendant Muralov's eight-month resistance to confessing at the 1937 Piatakov-Radek 'Parallel Center' show trial, Moscow
findings.evidence: why, after fighting against the Party since 1923, did you decide after all to give testimony? Why, after eight months after your arrest, did you keep silent, denying everything?
findings.source: /media/user/data/old-hdd/old-hdd/www.marxists.org/history/usa/parties/cpusa/anti-trotsky/An Eye Witness at the Wreckers Trial - Darcy.pdf
findings.implication: Real historical confession-under-interrogation timescales (3 months for Radek, 8 months for Muralov) give a usable range for how long a trapped cadre resists before a doctrinal contradiction forces public capitulation -- useful as the 'trap resolution clock' once a cadre is caught between loyalty and evidence.
findings.parameter: Confession delay after arrest: Radek ~3 months; Muralov ~8 months.

findings.case: Organizing Committee for an Ideological Center (OCIC), three sequential purge campaigns, 1979-1980
findings.evidence: over the course of 1980 one-third or more of the O.C.I.C membership was expelled or driven to resign and a highly disciplined sect of about 200-250 members created
findings.implication: A textbook Bureaucratic-degeneration trap chain: anti-federationism (destroys internal checks) → anti-white-chauvinism (purges ~1/3 via confession ritual) → anti-petty-bourgeois-intellectual (turns on the remaining minority cadre) — each node escalating consolidation with zero real line content, directly modeling a multi-node zero-cost drift chain analogous to the PatSoc Pipeline.
findings.parameter: 3 campaigns across ~12-18 months (1979-1980); ≥1/3 of membership purged/driven out, ending at a consolidated sect of 200-250 — candidate trap-completion timescale and purge-fraction for the Bureaucratic-degeneration trunk trap.

findings.case: Origin of Soviet bureaucratism, Trotsky's 5-factor diagnosis (via Blunden), 1923
findings.evidence: the international isolation, the cultural backwardness of the masses, their war-weariness and the low level of the economy... the absorption of the Communist cadre in state administration and their habit of command
findings.implication: This is a literal, itemized causal chain for the Bureaucratic-degeneration trap — isolation + scarcity + war-weariness + cadre absorbed into administration — that can be encoded directly as trap-trigger preconditions (e.g. rising 'cadre-in-administration' fraction plus falling external-solidarity flow).

findings.case: PRRWO leadership's doctrinal narrowing into absolutized 'onlys' immediately preceding its 1976 purge wave
findings.evidence: It had already begun to push in practice its famous 'onlys' (only party building, only propaganda, only the advanced)
findings.implication: A trap's onset is marked by legitimate tasks collapsing into a single absolutized doctrine node ('only X') that crowds out all others -- a detectable linguistic/behavioral telltale the game's existing Ideological Trap Detector meter could key on.

findings.case: PRRWO's Diego Pabon line (theory-as-cover-for-CPUSA-absorption)
findings.evidence: he dogmatically insisted that we study all of the 40 volumes of Lenin and do nothing else
findings.implication: Excess theoretical-labor investment divorced from practice was used as camouflage to funnel the org toward liquidation into a revisionist party — a Scientific-trunk trap variant where 'more study' is not neutral but actively steers toward Dissociation/co-optation.

findings.case: PRRWO's Felipe Luciano 'crisis orientation' / charismatic-leadership line, demoted Aug 1970, purged 1971
findings.evidence: during periods of intense flow things would work well, but in between, nothing would function
findings.implication: A textbook Adventurism-trap signature: organizational capacity that exists only during crisis peaks and collapses in the troughs, tied to one personality. Later Luciano literally became a Hollywood actor playing a 'P.R. revolutionary' — a vivid trap-to-defection arc.
findings.parameter: Demotion-to-purge gap: about 1 year (Aug 1970 demoted -> purged 1971).

findings.case: PRRWO/RWL 'Revolutionary Wing' collapse, 1976 — WVO's account of the purge
findings.evidence: purging 2/3 of their membership and leadership, that CIA agents and cops were strategically placed one by one, post by post, in key spots of their organizations!
findings.implication: A consolidating 'left' trap trend explains its own collapse by inventing mass infiltration rather than admitting line failure — the accusation-of-infiltration becomes the purge mechanism itself. Model a trap-collapse event that spikes a paranoia/repression stat and drives a large one-time defection.
findings.parameter: purge fraction ~0.67 (2/3) of membership+leadership at trap-collapse — candidate defection-fraction coefficient for the Adventurist/Insurrectionist trap ending.

findings.case: PRRWO/RWL purge spiral, spring–summer 1976
findings.evidence: With each wave of purges, the remaining PRRWO/RWL leadership became more and more frenzied.
findings.implication: Trap collapse is not a single check but a self-reinforcing feedback loop: each purge raises internal paranoia, which triggers the next purge. The DT trap-trigger should be an escalating multi-tick spiral, not a one-shot threshold test.

findings.case: Party membership/army collapse after two historical trunk-traps: the 1927 KMT-CCP split (Right capitulationism) and the 1934 Fifth Encirclement defeat (Wang Ming's 'Left' adventurism)
findings.evidence: The total membership of the Party, which had grown to more than 60,000, fell to a little over 10,000.
findings.implication: Gives two concrete catastrophic attrition fractions for trunk-trap outcomes on opposite trunks (Right capitulation vs. Left adventurism) -- usable as calibration anchors for how severe a DT trap ending's cadre-loss should be; both land near a 10-20% cadre-survival floor.
findings.parameter: 1927 Right-capitulationist trap: ~83% membership loss (60,000->10,000). 1934-35 Left-adventurist trap (Wang Ming): Red Army 300,000->30,000 (90% loss); Party 300,000->40,000 (87% loss).

findings.case: Post-Lushan 'Left' errors (Great Leap boastfulness/'communist wind') partially corrected at the 1962 7,000-cadre conference, then the correction itself reversed 8 months later at the Tenth Plenum
findings.evidence: 'Left' errors, characterized by excessive targets, the issuing of arbitrary directions, boastfulness and the stirring up of a 'communist wind', spread unchecked throughout the country.
findings.implication: Names the exact failure mode for a Desert-state cascading through a whole hierarchy: 'boastfulness' is upward data falsification propagating into real policy. Also shows a correction can itself be undone by a subsequent Congress event in well under a year -- correction is not monotonic.
findings.parameter: Partial correction (Jan 1962, 7,000-cadre conference) reversed by the Tenth Plenum ~8 months later (Sept 1962) -- candidate minimum 'correction half-life' before a relapse event can fire.

findings.case: Potresov's 'the party no longer exists' argument, answered in Lenin's 'Those Who Would Liquidate Us' III, 1911
findings.evidence: you cannot liquidate 'what is already beyond liquidation and actually no longer exists as an organised whole'
findings.implication: This is the verbatim historical liquidationist claim: the underground apparatus is already dead in substance, so formally dissolving it changes nothing. Direct trigger-condition template for the Liquidationism trap ending -- it fires on a *rationalization* (the party is already gone), not an open declaration of surrender.

findings.case: Premature party-building as sect-formation (NY Communist Workers' Organization critique of RU/PRRWO/BWC, 1974)
findings.evidence: "party-building" arose in groups such as the RU as an escape from failure, a self-delusion: they had failed to build a political movement
findings.implication: Names premature doctrine/organization-building itself (spending Theoretical Labor on party-founding nodes before sufficient mass-relationship prerequisites exist) as a trap trigger — it converts subjective failure into an 'objective' alibi and produces isolated sects rather than a vanguard.
findings.parameter: China 1921: the 12 founders had ~2 years of prior mass-organizing ties (May 4th 1919 movement, 90,000 Shanghai workers on strike) before declaring a party; Russia: 8-year gap between Lenin's 1895 shift to mass agitation and the RSDLP's actual 1903 founding, with an explicit 1898 'premature' founding attempt later disavowed.

findings.case: RGO Ruhr miners' strike call, January 1931 — claimed vs delivered mass mobilization
findings.evidence: The RGO claimed that the 506 delegates represented 121 pits and no less than 180 000 miners
findings.implication: The strike the RGO actually delivered covered only 22 pits and 65,000 miners — over the trap's course, claimed doctrinal support fell to roughly a third of stated support in practice. A useful numeric penalty for how far an Adventurist trunk's self-reported strength should be discounted when it actually tries to act.
findings.parameter: Claimed mobilization 180,000 miners vs delivered 65,000 (~36%) — claimed-to-actual mass-support discount ratio for an ultra-left doctrine node under real test.

findings.case: Rise of Stalinism as bureaucratic degeneration of an isolated workers' state, 1923-1928
findings.evidence: It is a great irony of history that precisely the dull-wittedness of the Stalin faction, their incompetence as revolutionary leaders, was the essential secret of their success
findings.implication: The Bureaucratic Degeneration trap should trigger from structural conditions (isolation + repeated defeat entrenching an apparatus) rather than from a villain's competence stat — the historical argument is explicitly that incompetence, not skill, was what let the trap close.
findings.parameter: 21.3 million Soviet deaths in WWII, including three-quarters of pre-war Party membership — an extreme-outcome cost anchor for what an uncorrected isolation/degeneration path can eventually cost, useful for calibrating the trap's worst-case severity scale.

findings.case: Socialist Party 'Left Wing' Mass Action surge under Louis Fraina's Revolutionary Age, 1919, per C.M. O'Brien's 1928 retrospective
findings.evidence: grew to thousands of members within a few months. Enthusiasm was at boiling point, understanding almost zero.
findings.implication: Textbook Adventurism-trap trigger: explosive tag/membership growth with zero theoretical-labor investment. The contemporaneous prescribed antidote — 'study classes... so that the membership may have opportunity to become informed on the fundamentals of socialism' — is literally the theoretical-labor purchase mechanic proposed as trap-prevention in real time.
findings.parameter: Left Wing grew 'to thousands... within a few months' (fast uncontrolled drift rate); the CP later expelled the whole Proletarian group 'two months later... without any warning' — a 2-month expulsion-notice interval.

findings.case: St. Petersburg liquidators' 1910 'petition campaign' for freedom of association
findings.evidence: altogether 1,300 signatures were collected, the petition campaign having met with absolutely no response in any of the Party organisations regardless of factions and trends
findings.implication: Concrete historical case of a low-cost, purely-legal mass action returning near-zero organizing yield across every faction — grounds a low M_r-return floor for legalist/reformist action types, distinct from AGITATE_LOCALLY's higher yield.
findings.parameter: 1,300 signatures collected over a winter (1910) nationwide campaign -> ~0 measured political response; candidate floor value for 'low-yield legal action' ROI coefficient.

findings.case: Stalin's diagnosis of patronage/kinship cliques (Mirzoyan in Kazakhstan, Vainov in Yaroslavl), 1937 plenum
findings.evidence: The former dragged along with him from Azerbaijan and the Urals, where he formerly worked, into Kazakstan thirty or forty of his 'own' people, and placed them in responsible positions in Kazakstan.
findings.source: /media/user/data/old-hdd/old-hdd/prolewiki/Exports/Library/Library_On the shortcomings of party work and measures to eliminate Trotskyist and other double-dealers_Concluding Speech.txt
findings.implication: This is a primary-source mechanism for the Scientific trunk's Bureaucratic Degeneration trap: a leader importing 30-40 personally loyal cadre creates 'conditions of kinship' that structurally exclude criticism and self-criticism. A doctrine-tree trigger for this trap could key off a 'personal loyalty network size' stat divorced from merit/political criteria.
findings.parameter: Patronage-network size cited as symptomatic: ~30-40 personally-loyal transplanted cadre installed in leadership positions per case.

findings.case: Stalin's own 1926 warning against factional 'bloodletting', quoted against him in the Lovestoneite Workers Age editorial on the 1937 Moscow Trials
findings.evidence: The method of lopping off, the method of bloodletting, is dangerous and contagious: today, this one is lopped off; tomorrow, that one; the next day, someone else.
findings.implication: A leader's own explicit, self-aware warning against escalating purge cycles, later ignored by that same leader, is a clean model for the Bureaucratic degeneration trap: the mechanic should let a faction accumulate 'warned but ignored' flags that raise future trap probability rather than resetting risk after each purge.
findings.parameter: Stalin's warning dated 1926, roughly a decade before the Moscow Trials it retroactively describes.

findings.case: Structural (not moral) account of bureaucratic degeneration's trigger conditions, Bolshevik Party 1923-1933
findings.evidence: It is a great irony of history that precisely the dull-wittedness of the Stalin faction, their incompetence as revolutionary leaders, was the essential secret of their success.
findings.implication: Directly grounds the design law that the Scientific trunk's Bureaucratic-Degeneration trap should trigger on structural conditions (external defeat + isolation + cadre absorption into administration), not on player skill or an intent flag -- repeated failure mechanically empowers the apparatus faction regardless of its competence.
findings.parameter: Four cited structural trigger conditions: failed external revolution, international isolation, cultural/economic backwardness, and cadre absorption into state administration -- candidate weighted inputs for the trap's trigger function.

findings.case: Suppression of the Makhnovist/anarchist movement, Ukraine/Russia, 1918-1921 (Voline)
findings.evidence: 1 – The weakness of the anarchist movement (in number and coordination); 2- The absence in the country of a worker's movement organized before the revolution.
findings.implication: Voline's own post-mortem gives two explicit, weighable trigger conditions for the Autonomist trunk's Dissociation trap: low organizational coordination AND absence of a pre-existing mass base — both encodable as threshold checks rather than narrative flavor.

findings.case: The 'Lenin levy', Feb–May 1924
findings.evidence: 250,000 people - loyal but politically uneducated workers, petty officials, careerists and all kinds of opportunists were admitted to membership, to be used as a battering ram against the Left Opposition
findings.implication: A precise cadre-coherence dilution event: mass recruitment used as a factional weapon collapses average ideological coherence and can be modeled as a sudden divisor drop in Theoretical Labor's coherence term, deliberately triggered from above to defeat a rival trunk.
findings.parameter: 250,000 new members admitted in ~3 months; only 1% of the resulting 1924 party had been members before March 1917 — a ~99:1 dilution of pre-revolutionary cadre by post-revolutionary recruits.

findings.case: The 'anti-guerrilla-ism' line inside the CCP military leadership, 1932-35 (root of the Fifth Campaign disaster)
findings.evidence: Its first stage was military adventurism, in the second it turned into military conservatism and, finally, in the third stage it became flightism.
findings.implication: A single wrong ideological premise cascades through three distinct, mechanically different failure modes over time rather than staying static — this is the direct historical precedent for a multi-node zero-cost drift chain (like the PatSoc Pipeline) where each node is a different, escalating symptom of the same root error.
findings.parameter: Full 3-stage drift chain spanned ~3 years (1932-1935) before Congress correction — candidate DRIFT_CHAIN_LENGTH_YEARS = 3

findings.case: The 1942 Yenan Rectification Movement naming the three canonical deviations
findings.evidence: Subjectivism, sectarianism and stereotyped Party writing are no longer the dominant styles, but merely gusts of contrary wind
findings.implication: The direct historical Ur-text for the DT trap taxonomy -- a fixed triad of named deviations (subjectivism/dogmatism-empiricism, sectarianism, formalism) that recurs across campaigns with substitutable membership, supporting a design where trap categories are stable archetypes but specific triggering behaviors are era-dependent.

findings.case: The 1944 dissolution of CPUSA into the Communist Political Association under Earl Browder
findings.evidence: THE COMMUNIST POLITICAL ASSOCIATION is a non-party organization of Americans which, basing itself upon the working class, carries forward the traditions of Washington, Jefferson, Paine, Jackson, and Lincoln
findings.implication: A textbook, documentary Liquidationism trap: the founding charter itself substitutes national-patriotic lineage for class-vanguard language and renounces party form ("non-party organization"). The trap trigger condition should key on exactly this substitution -- when a Reformist trunk's core doctrine text drops CLASS_ANALYSIS tags in favor of national-coalition tags.
findings.parameter: Regular National and State Conventions convened every two years (Article VII Sec.1; Article VI Sec.2) -- a real baseline number for Party Congress cadence.

findings.case: The 1981 Resolution's own verdict reversing the Lushan/Peng Dehuai purge -- the historical disanalogy the design brief flags
findings.evidence: The resolution passed by the Eighth Plenary Session of the Eighth Central Committee of the Party concerning the so-called anti-Party group of Peng Dehuai, Huang Kecheng, Zhang Wentian and Zhou Xiaozhou was entirely wrong.
findings.implication: A Congress-level trunk_shift/rectification verdict is not guaranteed correct at ratification -- only a LATER Congress (22 years afterward) reversed it. Model Congress verdicts as revisable-with-lag rather than ground truth; track a 'verdict confidence' that can flip on a subsequent rectification.
findings.parameter: 22 years elapsed between the wrong verdict (Aug 1959 Eighth Plenum) and its formal reversal (June 27, 1981 Sixth Plenum) -- a candidate outer bound for how long a bad Congress ruling can persist unchallenged.

findings.case: The Communist Party of America's own 1921 editorial dismissing internal critics of its armed-force program as agent provocateurs
findings.evidence: because its program contained the clause quoted — and therefore must have been written by agent provocateurs — would have to admit that history gives no example
findings.implication: A real precedent for reflexively branding internal dissent as enemy infiltration rather than engaging it -- a strong Adventurism/Insurrectionist trunk trap trigger: rising 'provocateur paranoia' should itself count as a trap-precursor signal, independent of any actual infiltration.

findings.case: The Cultural Revolution (1966-1976) as a rectification campaign that overshot into crisis
findings.evidence: the most severe setback … suffered by the Party, the state and the people since the founding of the People’s Republic
findings.implication: Shows the correction mechanism itself (mass rectification against bureaucratism) can overshoot into a decade-long adventurist/chaotic trap when uncontrolled — rectification actions should carry their own runaway-risk parameter rather than being modeled as strictly risk-free.
findings.parameter: 10 years (1966-1976) duration of a rectification campaign that overshot into self-described crisis

findings.case: The Cultural Revolution, officially bounded May 1966 to October 1976
findings.evidence: The "cultural revolution", which lasted from May 1966 to October 1976, was responsible for the most severe setback and the heaviest losses suffered by the Party, the state and the people
findings.implication: The CCP's own post-mortem gives a precise 10.5-year duration for its worst trap-state, with a further 2 years (to the Dec 1978 Third Plenum) before full doctrinal reversal — a strong real-world upper bound for how long a Bureaucratic-Degeneration-class trap can persist uncorrected before the system either breaks or corrects.
findings.parameter: 10.5 years trap duration (May 1966-Oct 1976) + 2 more years to full doctrinal correction (Dec 1978)

findings.case: The Great Leap Forward's 'communist wind', 1958
findings.evidence: "Left" errors, characterized by excessive targets, the issuing of arbitrary directions, boastfulness and the stirring up of a "communist wind", spread unchecked throughout the country.
findings.implication: Names four concrete, detectable symptom-tags of an onsetting Left/Adventurist trap (excessive targets, arbitrary directives, boastfulness, forced-collectivization pressure) — a usable template for trigger conditions on an Adventurism trap state rather than a single scalar threshold.

findings.case: The Lushan Conference, July 1959: Mao's turn against Peng Dehuai's criticism of the Great Leap Forward
findings.evidence: he erred in initiating criticism of Comrade Peng Dehuai and then in launching a Party-wide struggle against "Right opportunism"
findings.implication: The Party's own later verdict names this trap-labeling itself as the error ('entirely wrong'), not the underlying economic critique. A trap-detection mechanic must allow for the possibility that the TRAP LABEL is the corrupting move, not the doctrine it targets.

findings.case: The first Lushan Conference (1959), convened to correct Great Leap economic policy, hijacked in its entirety by the P'eng Te-huai line-struggle/purge
findings.evidence: Twenty days was not long enough and we abandoned the question of work.
findings.implication: A real case of a corrective Congress/Plenum event entirely captured by an internal purge, with its original policy agenda abandoned -- a 'distracted Congress' failure mode: rectification/Congress events should carry an opportunity-cost risk of crowding out the substantive policy work they were convened to do.
findings.parameter: ~20-day plenum length cited as the minimum viable Congress-event duration.

findings.case: The standing rectification doctrine ('unity-criticism-unity') applied uniformly, including to Wang Ming's three 'left' opportunist lines
findings.evidence: our aim in exposing errors and criticizing shortcomings, like that of a doctor curing a sickness, is solely to save the patient and not to doctor him to death.
findings.implication: The historical correction mechanic is reintegrative by default (self-criticism + rehabilitation), not punitive expulsion -- expulsion/faction-flip is reserved for actual defection (Chang Kuo-tao) or self-dissolution (Browder). Rectification/self_criticism should be a materially cheap correction path; only a rarer 'defection' event should trigger the trap-ending/faction-flip.

findings.case: Theoretical core of the Workers' Opposition condemnation — the 'producer' category error
findings.evidence: the concept of 'producer' lumps together the proletarian with the semi-proletarian and with the small commodity producer
findings.implication: The historical trap-trigger condition is an explicit CLASS_ANALYSIS failure (collapsing distinct classes into one undifferentiated category) — a clean rule for detecting Autonomist-trunk drift: trap fires when a doctrine node's class-analysis tag falls while its producerist/guild tag rises.

findings.case: Third Congress 1921 retroactive verdict on the March Action
findings.evidence: The 'theory of the revolutionary offensive' has been registered among the infantile disorders. The KAPD has been excluded from the Third International.
findings.implication: The trap-verdict was issued after the fact by the same body that had encouraged the action -- Congress correction mechanics can retroactively reclassify an action as a trap, so trap-triggers should be able to fire on delayed institutional judgment, not only immediate mechanical thresholds.

findings.case: Third Congress of the Comintern's post-March-Action rectification appeal, 17 July 1921
findings.evidence: if the isolated vanguard has no option but to fight, it must try to avoid any armed confrontation with the enemy, for only the masses can ensure victory for the proletariat over the armed White Guards
findings.implication: A documented rectification event immediately following an adventurist trap (the March Action): correction takes the form of a specific new engagement rule (don't accept battle while isolated) rather than a renunciation of militancy — a template for what a 'rectification' Congress outcome should mechanically change.
findings.parameter: March Action (adventurist trap trigger), March 1921, -> Third Congress rectification appeal, 17 July 1921: roughly a 4-month lag between trap trigger and formal Congress-level correction.

findings.case: Third Period 'social fascism' line as the ideological trigger of the Adventurist trap, KPD 1928-1933
findings.evidence: the theory and practice of 'social fascism' erected a barrier between Communism and the reformist workers.
findings.implication: Names the exact trigger condition for an Adventurism trap node: a doctrine that recodes potential allies (reformist workers) as equivalent to the primary enemy (fascists) mechanically forecloses united-front actions, walling the trunk off from the only base large enough to prevent catastrophe.

findings.case: Trotskyist infiltration of the Catalonian anarchist movement, Spanish Civil War 1936-39
findings.evidence: The Trotskyist organization had infiltrated the anarchist movement in Catalonia and incited a revolt against the Loyalist government under the slogans of "Socialist Republic" and "Workers' Government."
findings.implication: A concrete historical Adventurism-trap trigger: premature insurrectionary action against an allied anti-fascist coalition fractured the broader front ("civil war within a civil war") and aided the fascist enemy — strong grounding for firing the Insurrectionist trunk's trap when an insurrectionist doctrine node is exercised against local united-front conditions.

findings.case: Wang Ming 'Left' line dominance and Tsunyi Conference correction, 1931-1935
findings.evidence: The erroneous "Left" line dominated the Party for a particularly long time (four years) and brought extremely heavy losses, with disastrous consequences
findings.implication: One of the clearest quantified trap-cost cases in the whole corpus: a documented four-year period (4th Plenum Jan 1931 to Tsunyi Jan 1935) in which the dominant line inflicted catastrophic losses, only ending via a Party conference (Tsunyi) installing new leadership — a textbook Party Congress correction event with duration AND cost both dated.
findings.parameter: trap duration: 4 years (Jan 1931-Jan 1935); trap cost: ~90% attrition of Party/Red Army/base-area strength, corrected via the Tsunyi Conference

findings.case: Wang Ming's 'Left' adventurism and the failed defense against the KMT's Fifth 'Encirclement and Suppression' Campaign (1933-34)
findings.evidence: The Red Army of 300,000 men was reduced to about 30,000 and the Communist Party of 300,000 members to about 40,000.
findings.implication: Quantifies the cost of the mirror-image Left/Insurrectionist trap: ~90% military and ~87% party-strength destruction from premature adventurist offensive doctrine. Useful as the calibration ceiling for the Adventurism trap-ending's material cost.

findings.case: Weathermen-style "terrorist tendency" in the North American student movement, c. 1969-70
findings.evidence: The terrorist movement was but a flash in the pan. Within a year, it had seen its day.
findings.source: /media/user/data/old-hdd/old-hdd/prolewiki/Exports/Library/Library_Nation or Genocide_ The Struggle of the Native People Against Canadian and American Imperialism.txt
findings.implication: Contrasts two trap failure-speeds within one case: the Adventurist/terrorist deviation collapsed within about a year, while the opportunist "Economist" deviation it curdled into is described as persisting five-plus years — grounds different decay/persistence rates for a fast violent trap versus a slow reformist-liquidationist trap.
findings.parameter: Adventurist/terrorist trend burnout ~= 1 year; successor Economist deviation persisted 5+ years unresolved at time of writing.

findings.case: William Z. Foster's public defense of the 1937 Piatakov-Radek Moscow Trial confessions
findings.evidence: The inescapable conclusion from an examination of the whole situation is that the confessions were voluntary and genuine.
findings.source: /media/user/data/old-hdd/old-hdd/www.marxists.org/history/usa/parties/cpusa/anti-trotsky/Questions and Answers on the Piatakov-Radek Trial -Foster.pdf
findings.implication: A leading American Communist committed his public reputation in print, distributed as a mass pamphlet ('Read and Distribute'), to certifying show-trial confessions later established (post-1956, and definitively post-1991) to have been coerced -- a clean case of a Scientific/orthodox-trunk trap with total doctrinal lock-in and, as published, no visible correction mechanism (no self-criticism clause, no hedge).

findings.case: Franklin Opportunist Group split from the Revolutionary Union, 1970-72 (adventurist/insurrectionist trunk)
findings.evidence: as a result of this struggle, while a third of the organization was lost, the RU made rapid progress theoretically, politically and organizationally
findings.implication: Concrete cost data for an Insurrectionist/Adventurism trunk trap: the 'most oppressed are most revolutionary → armed struggle now' line cost the parent org roughly a third of its membership, and the breakaway org itself proved organizationally non-viable — usable as calibration for the Adventurism trap's membership-cost and terminal-org-lifespan parameters.
findings.parameter: ~1/3 of RU membership lost in the split; the breakaway Venceremos organization collapsed 'in about two years' — concrete cost and trap-terminal-org-lifespan figures.

findings.case: 'Real Tigers' debate — hotheaded vs. cool-headed factions during Leap triumphalism, Dec 1958
findings.evidence: Some people in our country are a bit too hotheaded at present. They are in no mood to allow a cooling off period
findings.implication: An internal debate explicitly framed around optimism-bias risk during a triumphalist moment — the historical seed of Insurrectionist/Adventurist drift, with Mao's corrective move (heat up the over-cautious, cool down the reckless) modeling rectification as a bidirectional dial rather than a one-way brake.

findings.case: 'The Current Situation and the Party's Tasks' (Oct 1939) -- three simultaneous slogans warning against opposite deviations at once
findings.evidence: Persist in resistance and oppose capitulation", "Persist in unity and oppose a split", and "Persist in progress and oppose retrogression
findings.implication: A textbook multi-line-struggle template mapping directly onto DT's four trunks needing symmetric warnings: capitulation (Reformist/Liquidationism risk), split (Insurrectionist/Autonomist risk), retrogression (Scientific/Bureaucratic-degeneration risk) are named as co-equal live dangers in one document -- supports 'no optimal path' as historically lived, not just designed-in.

findings.case: 'We Must Stress Unity and Progress' (1940): Mao's two-front doctrine against both capitulation (Right) and sectarian splitting (Left) inside the united front
findings.evidence: Without a programme for unity and progress, resistance must sooner or later turn into capitulation or end in defeat.
findings.implication: Models the 'no optimal path' design law directly: over-indexing on any single one of the three named axes (resistance/unity/progress) independently produces a distinct failure mode. Single-axis doctrine maximization should be structurally punished, not just balanced by tuning.

findings.case: 11th Plenum of the 8th CC (Aug 1-12, 1966) formally adopting the GPCR's 'Sixteen Points' decision
findings.evidence: THE Eleventh Plenary Session of the Eighth Central Committee of the Communist Party of China was held in Peking from August 1 to 12, 1966.
findings.implication: A Congress-level trunk_shift event has an exact, short session length even though its consequences (a decade-long trap cascade) are enormous -- trigger cost and cascade cost should be radically decoupled in the mechanic; a cheap-to-execute Congress action can unlock a very expensive downstream trap.
findings.parameter: Session length: 12 days (Aug 1-12, 1966) to ratify a trunk_shift whose cascade ran 10 years.

findings.case: 1904 RSDLP Party Council session: Central Committee vs. Menshevik-controlled editorial board deadlock
findings.evidence: we see no other honest way to end our dissensions than by appealing to the congress
findings.implication: Real precedent for the Party Congress correction mechanic being invoked specifically when factional deadlock makes ordinary Party function impossible ('On the present basis, any positive work is quite impossible') — trunk_shift/rectification should be triggerable by a functional-collapse condition, not only a fixed timer.

findings.case: 1920-1921 trade-union militarization dispute inside the Bolshevik party
findings.evidence: This led to a serious factional dispute in the communist party between the Marxist-Leninists and the Trotskyites between 1920-1921.
findings.implication: A concrete secondary line-struggle (coercion vs. persuasion of trade unions) erupting only ~3-4 years after the shared revolutionary victory shows that trunk divergence isn't limited to founding-moment splits — the doctrine tree should allow post-victory line struggles over administrative method, not just pre-revolutionary strategy.
findings.parameter: ~1 year duration of the acute factional dispute (1920-1921), occurring roughly 3-4 years after the 1917 revolution.

findings.case: 1927 Party referendum on Trotskyism inside the CPSU
findings.evidence: 724,000 Party members voted for the Bolsheviks, for the Central Committee of the Party, against the Trotskyites, and 4,000 Party members, or about one-half of one per cent, voted for the Trotskyites
findings.source: /media/user/data/old-hdd/old-hdd/prolewiki/Exports/Library/Library_On the shortcomings of party work and measures to eliminate Trotskyist and other double-dealers_Concluding Speech.txt
findings.implication: A real, sourced defection-fraction number for a losing trunk inside a mass party at the peak of internal debate: even generously padding with all non-voters, Stalin's own accounting tops out near 1.4% sympathizers. Use this as a floor/ceiling calibration point for how small a real minority-trunk faction can be while still being treated as a serious internal security threat.
findings.parameter: Trotskyist-trunk vote share in 1927 CPSU referendum: 4,000/730,000 voting members = ~0.55% direct; generously estimated sympathizer ceiling ~12,000/854,000 total membership = ~1.4%.

findings.case: 1942 Rectification Movement inside the CCP (dogmatists vs. the membership) resolved by the Seventh National Congress, 1945
findings.evidence: Within a few years, by the time the Chinese Communist Party held its Seventh National Congress in 1945, unity was achieved throughout the Party as anticipated
findings.implication: Gives a concrete historical cadence for the 'rectification campaign leads to a Congress that ratifies the new line' correction mechanic — the fix was not instantaneous but took multiple years of sustained internal struggle before the Congress sealed it.
findings.parameter: Rectification-to-Congress cadence: 1942 rectification movement -> 1945 Seventh Congress = 3 years to full Party unity — candidate RECTIFICATION_TO_CONGRESS_YEARS = 3

findings.case: 1952/53 and 1954/55 cooperative 'expand-then-axe' policy whiplash
findings.evidence: there was an expansion in the winter of 1952, only to be opposed as a rash advance in the spring of 1953
findings.implication: A documented ~1-year rectification/reversal cadence that damaged mass trust (peasants: 'You were the ones who told us to set up the co-operatives, and now you are the ones who tell us to disband them') — trunk_shift reversals should carry an M_r/legitimacy penalty, not be free.
findings.parameter: ~1-year oscillation period (winter expansion / spring correction), repeated twice (1952-53, 1954-55) before the line stabilized.

findings.case: 2nd Convention of the United Communist Party of America, Kingston/Overlook Mountain NY, Dec. 24 1920 - Jan. 2 1921
findings.evidence: Original motion to accept report of the CEC was then voted upon with the following results: 24 in favor, 2 against, 4 not voting, and 10 refusing to vote.
findings.implication: A real recorded self_criticism/no-confidence roll-call against the sitting Central Executive Committee inside a live Congress, including a large abstention-as-protest bloc (10 of 40 refusing to vote). The self_criticism correction mechanic should model partial, contested outcomes (censure-but-retain) rather than a binary pass/fail of leadership.
findings.parameter: Total membership represented: 5,801 across 9 districts; convention size ~52 (42 delegates + 8 CEC + 2 others), a ratio of ~1 delegate per 138 members; 9-10 day congress with daily sessions 9am-10pm.

findings.case: 2nd RSDLP Congress 1903 — vote on placing the Bund item first on the agenda
findings.evidence: Thirty votes were cast in favour—this is the figure, as we shall see later, around which the votes of the Iskra-ists often fluctuated.
findings.implication: Congress-level trunk votes cluster around a stable 'hard core' bloc size that recurs across unrelated votes; model a persistent per-faction baseline vote-share rather than re-rolling support each vote.
findings.parameter: Core loyalist bloc ≈ 30/51 votes (~59%) recurring across at least 6 separate Congress votes — candidate baseline for a trunk's locked-in support fraction.

findings.case: 32 Shanghai Revolutionary Rebel Organizations' 'Urgent Notice', Jan 9 1967
findings.evidence: The Shanghai Workers' Revolutionary Rebel General Headquarters and 31 other revolutionary rebel organizations issued an 'Urgent Notice'
findings.implication: A concrete, dated, named-in-full instance of the 'no optimal path' design law: 32 distinct factional organizations, each with its own command structure, simultaneously claim to be executing Chairman Mao's correct line against the same enemy. No single trunk is privileged even among ostensibly aligned rebels — direct evidentiary support for refusing to let the doctrine DAG converge on one winning path.
findings.parameter: 32 co-existing named factional organizations claiming an identical doctrinal mandate in one city at one time (Shanghai, Jan 1967)

findings.case: 5th Comintern Congress (June 17-July 8 1924) mandate to 'Bolshevize' national parties via Shop Nuclei
findings.evidence: The Fifth Congress has confirmed this attitude of the Executive Committee and has made it one of the chief tasks of every party of the International to reshape itself on the basis of the Bolshevik form of organization
findings.implication: A real historical Congress-mandated trunk_shift: an international body forced a member party to restructure its base unit (territorial branch -> shop nucleus) on a hard deadline. Models the Party Congress mechanic as able to force a structural trunk_shift on subordinate organizations, not only a doctrinal one.
findings.parameter: Comintern Congress cadence implied here: 5th Congress, 1924, following the 2nd Congress (1920) -- multi-year cadence for the highest-level correcting body.

findings.case: 6th Comintern Congress 'Third Period' sectarianism (1928) self-criticized and reversed at the 7th Congress (1935)
findings.evidence: self-satisfied sectarianism, as we designate it in the draft resolution, more than anything else impedes our straggle for the realization of the united front
findings.implication: A textbook real 'Party Congress as correction mechanism' event: seven years of self-acknowledged sectarian error explicitly reversed by Congress resolution — a strong historical cadence anchor for a trunk_shift/self_criticism Congress event.
findings.parameter: 7-year gap between the erring 6th Congress (1928) and the correcting 7th Congress (1935); the 7th Congress was also the last Comintern congress ever held before its 1943 dissolution

findings.case: 9th Congress (1 April 1920) trade-union resolution vs. 10th Congress (16 March 1921) condemnation of the radicalized successor position
findings.evidence: the trade unions must be gradually converted into auxiliary organs of the proletarian state, and not the other way around
findings.implication: The 9th Congress passed an ambiguous compromise on party/union/state relations; under a year later the radicalized version of the losing position was condemned as a named heresy. Congress cadence should let one congress's unresolved compromise seed the next congress's trap-trigger.
findings.parameter: 9th Congress 1 Apr 1920 → 10th Congress 16 Mar 1921 ≈ 11.5 months — candidate default Party-Congress cadence (annual, not per-tick).

findings.case: AAUD (factory-council union) vs KAPD (party-form), Germany 1920-1923
findings.evidence: In the winter of 1920-1921 the AAUD alone had some 150,000 members (while the KAPD had about 40,000)... It would lose almost all of its members after 1923.
findings.implication: A rival trunk (Autonomist-style unitary organization) can massively outscale the vanguard-party trunk in raw membership (150k vs 40k) and STILL collapse to near-zero within ~2-3 years — direct evidence that trunk size is not a proxy for trunk durability, supporting 'no optimal path.'
findings.parameter: Peak AAUD ≈150,000 (winter 1920-21) vs KAPD ≈40,000; AAUD lost 'almost all' members by 1923 (≈2-3 year collapse horizon). Print runs up to 120,000 copies per pamphlet at peak.

findings.case: AAUD founding program, Leipzig, Dec 1920 (Autonomist trunk doctrine)
findings.evidence: The councils can be recalled at any time by the rank and file which bestowed their mandates. There is no place for so-called leaders except as advisors.
findings.implication: A clean historical doctrine-node text for the Autonomist trunk's founding tag vector: total recallability and leaders-as-advisors-only. Grounds the eventual Dissociation trap as the logical endpoint of this doctrine taken to its limit — total anti-hierarchy erodes the very coordination capacity needed to survive repression.

findings.case: Actual gap between the December 1908 Conference and the January 1912 Prague Conference
findings.evidence: the necessity for intensive work to re-establish the illegal organisation of the R.S.D.L.P.
findings.implication: The real inter-congress gap (Dec 1908 -> Jan 1912, ~37 months) blew past the 2-year Rule under the repression of the 'June Third regime' — congress cadence is repression-elastic, a design lever for slowing the trunk-shift/rectification mechanic when Repression is high.
findings.parameter: Actual observed gap ≈ 3 years 1 month vs. the 2-year Rule -> ~50% cadence slippage under sustained state repression.

findings.case: Attack on the National Miners' Union founding convention, Pittsburgh, Sept. 9-11, 1928 (Martin Abern/ILD report)
findings.evidence: More than 125 delegates were arrested, and it was Abern as the on-the-spot representative of the ILD who organized the campaign to get them out of jail.
findings.implication: Concrete material costs of executing a hard Third-Period dual-unionism trunk shift (breaking from AFL-affiliated UMWA): mass arrests, serious injury (one delegate beaten to permanent memory loss), and real defense expenditure — usable to calibrate the resource cost of an Insurrectionist-trunk tactical escalation.
findings.parameter: 675 delegates assembled; 125 arrests; total ILD defense cost over $600 ($250 legal fee + fines of $5-$25/head); nearly all released within 24-48 hours.

findings.case: BASOC's 'left in form, right in essence' framework for evaluating deviations (1980)
findings.evidence: Right errors slow the potential development of the revolutionary movement, while 'left' errors alienate the masses from revolutionary ideas and actions.
findings.implication: Direct primary-source validation of the 'no optimal path' design law: Reformist(right)-leaning and Insurrectionist/Autonomist(left)-leaning trunks fail via different but equally fatal mechanisms (slow atrophy vs. mass alienation), so no trunk or 'balanced center' should be strictly dominant.

findings.case: Black Workers Congress three-way split (MH 'revolutionary' bloc / Interim Committee / genuine lefts), 1974-75
findings.evidence: We represent about half of the organization, and have districts in every region of the country.
findings.implication: A clean, labeled three-way faction fracture with an explicit membership fraction, useful for calibrating split mechanics where no faction is a clear minority — models a scenario where doctrinal factions fragment roughly evenly rather than producing one dominant trunk.
findings.parameter: Genuine-lefts faction ≈ half the organization; the chairman's resignation to the split's emergency CC meeting spanned 'five months' — a concrete faction-fracture timescale.

findings.case: Black Workers Congress two-line struggle, Feb 1974-1975, dominant 'left' line under Donald Williams
findings.evidence: It was this line that guided the policies of the organization for over a year
findings.implication: A single dominant erroneous line (here 'left' opportunism on party building) persisted roughly a year before both it and its opposing right faction were purged together -- both factions were later shown to be 'twins,' arguing that trap-detection should watch for a trunk oscillating between left/right poles of the SAME central-task error, not just cross-trunk contamination.
findings.parameter: dominant erroneous line held organizational hegemony for 'over a year' before double purge of both left and right factions

findings.case: Black Workers Congress/Revolutionary Workers Congress two-line struggle, Feb-Nov 1974
findings.evidence: the die-hard dogmatists headed by Don Williams maintain that we had done nothing wrong, that the line 'was basically correct' and what we needed was 'more of the same.'
findings.implication: One unresolved two-line struggle fissioned into two opposite trunks simultaneously (a rigid dogmatist splinter AND an anti-theory 'go deep into the class' splinter, per the same document) — supports modeling trunk splits as branching DAG events from a single parent rather than linear drift along one axis.
findings.parameter: Two-line struggle 'dominated the BWC from February to November 1974' — roughly 9 months from open rupture to organizational split; candidate timescale for how long a live two-line contest runs before a trunk fork resolves.

findings.case: Bolshevik ('Pravdist') consolidation of Russian Social-Democratic workers' groups, 1912-1914
findings.evidence: Pravdism has united four-fifths of the class-conscious workers of Russia (5,300 Social-Democratic workers' groups out of 6,700 in two-and-a-half years).
findings.implication: A concrete real-world trunk-consolidation growth rate: from a fragmented multi-tendency field to ~79% coverage in 2.5 years. Useful as an upper-bound benchmark for how fast a coherent trunk's TL-purchased doctrine can plausibly dominate a contested ideological field.
findings.parameter: 79% (5,300/6,700) workers'-group consolidation achieved in 2.5 years (1912-1914).

findings.case: Boulder group's public repudiation of the 'Revolutionary Wing' (PRRWO-RWL) line, c.1978
findings.evidence: We take this opportunity to publicly repudiate and denounce the 'left in form, right in essence' line that we did carry out during that period.
findings.implication: A documented real trunk_shift: an organization narrates its own move off an Insurrectionist trunk via public self-criticism, explicitly naming 'isolation from the masses' and degeneration into 'worthless revisionist sects' as the cost already paid — grounds the trunk_shift Congress-outcome in a real text.

findings.case: CLP–CP reunification talks, Dec. 1919–Feb. 1920
findings.evidence: This majority, consisting of five or six officials, members of the NEC of the CP, is the only barrier to immediate unity.
findings.implication: A documented Party-Congress-style correction attempt stalled not on doctrinal distance but on a small entrenched leadership clique's veto — trunk_shift mechanics should model a leadership-friction blocker distinct from ideological-distance cost, since here base membership on both sides reportedly favored unity.
findings.parameter: 5-6 officials on a National/Central Executive Committee sufficient to block a unity vote — candidate minimum veto-bloc size for stalling a rectification/trunk_shift resolution.

findings.case: CPC Constitution (2002), Article 18/21 — formal Congress cadence
findings.evidence: The National Congress of the Party is held once every five years and convened by the Central Committee.
findings.source: /media/user/data/old-hdd/old-hdd/prolewiki/Exports/Library/Library_Constitution of the Communist Party of China (2002).txt
findings.implication: A codified, real institutional congress cadence is the direct analogue of the Party Congress correction-action cadence; five years is a strong default anchor for how often a full trunk_shift/rectification opportunity is available absent an owner-triggered emergency congress.
findings.parameter: National Congress every 5 years; Central Committee term 5 years; per-cycle Central Committee membership turnover capped at "not exceed one-fifth" of the total — candidate max churn-per-cycle.

findings.case: CPC Constitution (2002), Article 39 — graduated discipline ladder
findings.evidence: There are five measures for enforcing Party discipline: warning, serious warning, removal from Party posts, probation within the Party, and expulsion from the Party.
findings.source: /media/user/data/old-hdd/old-hdd/prolewiki/Exports/Library/Library_Constitution of the Communist Party of China (2002).txt
findings.implication: A graduated five-step discipline ladder — not a binary loyal/expelled state — is the real institutional model for rectification; self_criticism and lesser corrections should have intermediate states before the DT mechanic's full faction-flip/expulsion outcome.
findings.parameter: Probation period capped at "not exceed two years" — candidate max-duration for a partial-correction game state.

findings.case: CPI-M's 2010 internal rectification document and the expulsion of Lakshman Seth
findings.evidence: worried about 'the penetration of alien bourgeois and petty bourgeois values' which are 'manifested in a lavish lifestyle, building houses which are far above the minimum needs required'
findings.implication: A contemporary, named rectification document with a concrete cost outcome: a senior leader was first dropped from the State Committee, then expelled, then drifted to an opposing party (Trinamul Congress) — showing rectification without expulsion is possible first, escalating to expulsion only on continued drift. Good model for a graduated Congress correction ladder rather than binary purge/no-purge.

findings.case: Charles Ruthenberg's decade of continuous state prosecution, 1917-1927
findings.evidence: From 1917 on there was not one year which did not find him under indictment or sentence.
findings.implication: A concrete 'cost of trunk commitment' timescale: roughly a decade of continuous legal jeopardy is the historical price tag for a leader who never pivoted off an exposed, agitation-heavy trunk -- usable as a per-tick cost multiplier for staying on an Insurrectionist trunk under active state pressure.
findings.parameter: ~10-year span (1917-1927) of continuous indictment/arrest; Bridgman arrest 1922 -> Party legalized 1923 (~1-year Congress-to-legalization lag).

findings.case: Chen Tu-hsiu's Right capitulationism, 1927 defeat and 1929 expulsion
findings.evidence: voluntarily gave up the Party's leadership of the peasant masses, urban petty bourgeoisie and middle bourgeoisie, and in particular gave up the Party's leadership of the armed forces, thus causing the defeat of the revolution
findings.implication: A Liquidationism-trap analog with a documented drift-and-flip arc: Right-opportunist capitulation in 1927 caused the revolution's defeat, followed roughly two years later by expulsion and a hard turn into an anti-Party Trotskyist faction — a real precedent for a trap ending in a full faction flip rather than mere failure.
findings.parameter: ~2 years from 1927 capitulationist defeat to Nov 1929 expulsion and Trotskyist faction-flip

findings.case: Co-optation majority-threshold fight, Paragraph 12 of the RSDLP Rules, 1903
findings.evidence: The commission proposed raising the majority required for co-optation from two-thirds to four-fifths.
findings.implication: Concrete historical range for a co-optation/appointment majority-threshold dial in the Party Congress mechanic, spanning simple majority through unanimity, with real factions occupying every point on the scale.
findings.parameter: Co-optation majority requirement debated across 2/3 (66.7%), 4/5 (80%), and unanimity (100%) — candidate discrete settings for a trunk_shift/appointment veto dial.

findings.case: Collapse of the 4-org National Liaison Committee (PRRWO, RU, IWK, BWC) over the Bundism dispute with IWK, 1972-73
findings.evidence: unity finally degenerated when the IWK refused to attend any further NLC meetings and discontinued working with the local workteam in New York
findings.implication: Multi-org pre-party coalitions fractured over unresolved national-question disputes before any founding Congress could be held -- a DAG 'alliance' or 'united front' node should decay or dissolve automatically if member orgs' national-question tags diverge past a threshold.

findings.case: Cominform's September 1947 declaration forcing a simultaneous Reformist-to-Insurrectionist trunk shift across national Communist Parties (incl. CPA)
findings.evidence: The principal danger for the working class to-day lies in underestimating their strength and overestimating the strength of the imperialist camp
findings.implication: A single external directive retroactively validated a domestic minority faction's (Blake's) prior line and forced a wholesale trunk shift across parties overnight — models trunk_shift as sometimes externally imposed by a supranational body rather than an internal Congress choice, with a domestic faction 'winning' only because the wind changed.
findings.parameter: Single Cominform declaration, September 1947, synchronized a Reformist->Insurrectionist trunk shift across multiple national parties simultaneously.

findings.case: Comintern policy oscillation, 1923-1941 (six full reversals, e.g. 'social fascism' 1933 to Hitler-Stalin Pact 1939)
findings.evidence: Between 1923 and 1941 the Comintern line abruptly changed by 180 degrees six times
findings.implication: A real historical base rate for how often an externally-controlled doctrinal apparatus can whipsaw a subordinate party's trunk without any internal Congress-style debate — useful as a ceiling/warning figure: game trunk_shift events happening this frequently should read as 'bureaucratic capture,' not healthy correction.
findings.parameter: 6 complete 180-degree line reversals across 18 years (~1 every 3 years) with no accompanying self-criticism — candidate frequency ceiling for an externally-forced (non-Congress) trunk-flip event.

findings.case: Communist Labor Party constitution, Sept 5 1919 -- the founding split's anti-fusion membership pledge
findings.evidence: I am opposed to all political organizations that support the present capitalist profit system and am opposed to any form trading or fusing with any such organizations.
findings.implication: The CLP constitutionally precommitted cadre against tactical fusion/united-front action -- a textbook trunk-specific restriction (an Insurrectionist-leaning purity clause) that can be encoded as a doctrine-node effect that locks out certain diplomatic actions once purchased.
findings.parameter: Initiation fee $1.00; dues $0.50/month; NEC per diem $6.00/day; 1 international delegate per 20,000 members; fixed annual convention date (May 10).

findings.case: Constitution of the Communist International, 4 March 1919
findings.evidence: All parties, organisations and groups are given a period of eight months in which officially to declare their adherence to the Third International
findings.implication: The actual founding congress set a hard, dated grace period for factions to formally align to the new trunk before consequences (exclusion) applied — a concrete real-world cadence for a trunk_shift adoption window rather than instantaneous re-alignment.
findings.parameter: trunk-realignment grace period: 8 months (Comintern First Congress, March 1919)

findings.case: Council communism as an Autonomist historical current
findings.evidence: Council communists believe that there are six main steps to achieving council communism:
findings.implication: Direct primary grounding for an Autonomist trunk defined by explicit rejection of the vanguard-party mechanism in favor of workers'-council self-organization — gives the trunk a clean structural marker (no Party-Congress-style correction node at all) distinguishing it from the other three trunks.

findings.case: Economism (Rabocheye Dyelo/Martynov) vs Social-Democratic politics (Iskra/Lenin), 1901
findings.evidence: Class political consciousness can be brought to the workers only from without, that is, only from outside the economic struggle, from outside the sphere of relations between workers and employers.
findings.implication: This is the literal historical fork between a Reformist trunk (doctrine grows from the workers' own economic experience) and a Scientific trunk (doctrine must be imported via theory/agitation) — direct grounding for the CLASS_ANALYSIS vs economistic-tailism tag-vector split.

findings.case: Eighth National Congress of the CPC, Liu Shao-chi's political report, Sept 15 1956
findings.evidence: Eleven years have passed since the Seventh National Congress of our Party
findings.implication: The 8th Congress's 'chief contradiction is working class vs bourgeoisie via restriction/counter-restriction' line was itself later overturned pre-Leap. Gives a real congress-cadence data point (7th->8th = 11 years) for calibrating how rarely the Party Congress correction mechanic actually fires, before a major trunk reversal.
findings.parameter: 11-year interval between the 7th (1945) and 8th (Sept 1956) National Congresses

findings.case: Enumeration of the CCP's historical party splits through 1958
findings.evidence: Our party underwent four splits, involving Ch'en Tu-hsiu, Lo Chang-lung, Chang Kuo-t'ao and Kao Kang.
findings.implication: A concrete roster for calibrating trap-ending frequency: four major schisms across ~37 years of party history (1921-1958) is roughly one per decade -- a candidate base rate for how often a long-running DT campaign should expect a full trunk-trap event to actually fire.
findings.parameter: ~4 splits / 37 years ≈ 1 schism per 9 years -- base-rate candidate for trap-trigger frequency.

findings.case: Ethnic-federation bloc control of the Credentials Committee at the September 1919 Communist Party founding convention, Chicago
findings.evidence: they secured control of the committee by electing N.I. Hourwich, Alexander Stoklitsky, and D. Elbaum to offset the other 2 members, I.E. Ferguson and C.E. Ruthenberg
findings.implication: Control of the ADMISSIONS/credentialing chokepoint, not raw vote count, is how one faction locked rivals out entirely (Reed, Lore, Gitlow, Wagenknecht, Katterfeld, Boudin were barred outright). The trunk DAG should let node/vote access be gated by whichever faction currently holds a structural gatekeeping role, not just by TL spend.

findings.case: Foster vs. Olgin/Pepper factional dispute over Farmer-Labor-Party tactics, American Commission of ECCI, Moscow, April 29, 1924
findings.evidence: We have the Comintern to guide us.
findings.implication: A live, verbatim transcript of an external body hearing both trunk factions argue a live tactical fork before it hardens into a split — the direct historical model for a Party Congress arbitration mechanic (trunk_shift adjudicated by an outside authority rather than resolved internally).
findings.parameter: Workers Party had 17 federations; Minnesota Farmer-Labor caucus drew 75 of 250 delegates; Minnesota Third Party polled over 325,000 votes.

findings.case: Foster vs. Olgin/Pepper factional dispute, same 1924 ECCI hearing
findings.evidence: it will be a bad split that will isolate us... it will extend over a long period
findings.implication: Foster explicitly distinguishes short-term from long-term isolation cost of a mistimed trunk break. A trap/trunk-shift cost model should include a long decaying-isolation tail, not just an immediate one-time penalty.

findings.case: Foster's 1946 break with the Truman coalition, precipitating the anti-Browder line
findings.evidence: Foster called upon Wallace and Ickes to resign from Truman's Cabinet, knowing that such a development would wreck the Administration
findings.implication: A hard-left trunk pivot deliberately courted rupture with a coalition partner as tactic — the same act reads as principled vanguardism to one faction and as textbook Adventurism-trap recklessness to the other, illustrating that trap/trunk labeling is itself a site of factional contest, not an objective engine readout.

findings.case: Full trap-to-correction cycle for the Cultural Revolution: Gang of Four arrested Oct 1976, Third Plenary Session Dec 1978, Sixth Plenary Session Resolution 1981
findings.evidence: The Third Plenary Session of the Eleventh Central Committee held in December 1978 marked a decisive turning point
findings.implication: Gives the full historical latency of a complete trap-to-Congress-correction cycle at the scale of an entire national trunk-collapse: roughly two years from the trap's forcible end to the decisive correcting Congress, and about five years total to the fully ratified resolution — a useful outer bound for how long a 'Bureaucratic degeneration' trap can remain unresolved at maximum severity before the game forces a correction.
findings.parameter: Oct 1976 (clique smashed) -> Dec 1978 (decisive Plenum) ≈ 2.2 years; -> 1981 (full Resolution) ≈ 5 years total correction latency. Party size at time of correction: 39 million members. Candidate FULL_TRAP_CORRECTION_LATENCY_YEARS ≈ 5, PARTY_CADRE_COUNT_AT_CORRECTION = 39,000,000

findings.case: Gap between the 7th (1945) and 8th (1956) National Congresses of the CPC
findings.evidence: During the eleven years since the Seventh National Congress of our Party, many comrades and friends both here in our country and throughout the world have given their lives
findings.implication: Full National Congresses (as opposed to smaller Plenums) ran on an irregular, multi-year cadence historically rather than a fixed schedule -- a Congress-level major doctrine reset should be rare and expensive, with ordinary correction happening at a more frequent Plenum layer instead.
findings.parameter: Congress cadence ~11 years (7th->8th, 1945-1956); later 13 years to the 9th Congress (1969) due to Cultural Revolution disruption -- candidate long cooldown for a full 'trunk reset' Congress event, contrasted with annual-to-biennial Plenums for smaller corrections.

findings.case: Harry Haywood's 1930 polemic against the 'race question' line inside CPUSA/Comintern
findings.evidence: the October resolution of the E.C.C.I. by definitely establishing the Negro question in the United States as a national question
findings.source: /media/user/data/old-hdd/old-hdd/prolewiki/Exports/Library/Library_Against Bourgeois-Liberal Distortions of Leninism on the Negro Question in the United States.txt
findings.implication: A real, dated internal doctrine fight over exactly the CLASS_ANALYSIS-vs-NATIONALISM axis - the 'race question' framing is explicitly named as the opportunist/chauvinist deviation and the 'national question' framing as the corrected Marxist-Leninist line, with named factions on each side and a Comintern resolution as the arbitrating congress-level document.

findings.case: Irwin Silber's 'On Party Building' (The Guardian, June 1976) — the movement's own self-diagnosed decision tree
findings.evidence: Can we proceed directly to the organization of a multinational party or must we accept the inevitability of a variety of Marxist-Leninist organizations defined on the basis of nationality
findings.implication: Silber frames party-building explicitly as three unresolved binary choices (mass-work-vs-party-building primacy; cadre-threshold-before-founding; multinational-vs-national-specific form) — a real-world template for the DT trunk-selection menu, each choice load-bearing and none obviously 'correct.'

findings.case: Italian Communist Party's 70-year arc from Bordiga's 1921 founding to its 1991 dissolution into Eurocommunist liberalism
findings.evidence: the party continued to exist in revisionist form until 1991, when the fall of the Warsaw pact resulted in the leadership fully capitulating to liberalism
findings.implication: A single-party arc spanning from an Insurrectionist-trunk founder (Bordiga) to a fully Reformist/liquidationist endpoint gives a real upper-bound timescale for how long a trunk can drift if it is never corrected by a rectification congress.
findings.parameter: 70-year span (1921-1991) from insurrectionist founding to full liquidationist dissolution - an upper bound for uncorrected trunk drift.

findings.case: Jarvis-Bergman 'Menshevik' clique coup attempt in the RCP, triggered by China's post-Mao doctrinal shift, 1976-78
findings.evidence: The Jarvis-Bergman clique liked the revisionism coming out of China mainly because of their own revisionism. It fit them like a glove.
findings.implication: Shows a Reformist/Liquidationist trunk trap can be triggered by an external shock (an allied state's doctrinal reversal) rather than only internal drift — a design case for letting world-events (not just player choices) push factions toward trap thresholds.

findings.case: KAPD delegation, Third Congress 1921 -- failed attempt to build a cross-national minority bloc
findings.evidence: Everyone was afraid of being excluded from the Third International.
findings.implication: Models the Autonomist/Insurrectionist trunk's structural isolation: even ideologically sympathetic delegations would not risk formal opposition once exclusion was threatened -- trunk purity produces isolation as an emergent penalty, not a chosen one.
findings.parameter: The most sympathetic bloc (Spanish delegation) represented an org of ~1,100,000 members (~50% of organized Spanish workers) yet still would not accept the KAPD's party-form demand -- a scale mismatch between doctrinal agreement and formal alliance.

findings.case: KOR's entryist strategy toward official Polish structures, 1980
findings.evidence: the KOR counselled the workers to infiltrate them, to transform the bogus factory committees or peasants co-operatives and so forth into genuine bodies
findings.implication: A live, contemporaneous Reformist-trunk line (work within existing structures to transform them) competing directly against the Autonomist line the base actually took (total independence, rejecting all Party co-optation) — a clean two-trunk fork inside one movement, decided by rank-and-file rejection of the entryist line.

findings.case: Kautsky (quoted approvingly by Lenin) on the origin of socialist consciousness, What Is To Be Done? II, 1902
findings.evidence: socialist consciousness is something introduced into the proletarian class struggle from without and not something that arose within it spontaneously
findings.implication: This is the founding axiom of the Scientific trunk: consciousness (and by extension correct doctrine) is an input purchased by theoretical labor from outside spontaneous struggle, not something that accrues automatically -- justifies TL as a real, non-optional draw rather than a flavor stat.

findings.case: Khrushchev's 20th Congress 'Secret Speech' as a contested trunk-shift trigger (Feb 25, 1956)
findings.evidence: The fact that Natalia Sedova-Trotskaia learned of the supposedly "secret" speech immediately it happened suggests that the Trotskyites may have still had high-level informants in the CPSU.
findings.implication: A single leadership speech functioned as the trunk-shift trigger for the entire world communist movement (precipitating the Sino-Soviet split) independent of any real ground-truth verification of its claims — models trunk_shift as a top-down doctrine event that can fire without I_c confirmation.

findings.case: LPR (M-L) and COReS (M-L-M) 1979 merger announcement, explicitly contrasting failed vs. successful precedent mergers
findings.evidence: previous merger attempts in our country have been characterized by a lack of an open and vigorous ideological struggle among the participants, by an attitude of 'all unity, no struggle'
findings.implication: Names the causal mechanism distinguishing successful trunk-merges (OL+collectives->CPML; IWK+ATM->League for Revolutionary Struggle) from failed ones (the NLC; RU+PRRWO+BWC+IWK): cadre-level participation and a genuine prior rectification campaign, not top-down 'unity over the table.'

findings.case: LRS/Unity diagnosis of the RU→RCP rightist-to-ultraleftist flip
findings.evidence: The RCP's ultra-leftism today is simply the flipside of this old rightism.
findings.implication: Documents the same organization flipping from a Reformist trunk (tailing spontaneous economism as the RU) to an Insurrectionist trunk (isolated sectarian adventurism as the RCP) without passing through a stable center — supports treating trunks as attractors an org can be flung between, not a single permanent choice.

findings.case: Lenin's First Speech on the Congress Agenda, 2nd RSDLP Congress, July 18 1903
findings.evidence: the surmise that a rupture may take place over the programme is conjectural in the highest degree. No such tendencies have been discernible in the Party
findings.implication: Lenin, days before the actual Bolshevik/Menshevik rupture, confidently dismissed the possibility of a split over the programme — the fork that materialized (Article 1 membership, then Council composition) was on an axis nobody flagged as dangerous beforehand. Strong evidence for the design law 'no optimal path': even the most doctrinally sophisticated actor cannot foresee which node in the tree will fork the trunk.

findings.case: Lenin's Speech on the Place of the Bund, 2nd Congress, 1903 — federation vs. autonomy
findings.evidence: Federation is harmful because it sanctions segregation and alienation, elevates them to a principle, to a law.
findings.implication: The textbook statement of the Autonomist trunk's core temptation (federated/segregated organization) versus centralist unity — useful source text for the trap's flavor description and trigger condition (self-sanctioned 'obligatory partitions').
