findings.case: Lenin's critique of the gradualist ('Kautskyite') reading of the state's 'withering away', State and Revolution ch.1, 1917
findings.evidence: To prune Marxism to such an extent means reducing it to opportunism, for this "interpretation" only leaves a vague notion of a slow, even, gradual change
findings.implication: Names the exact mechanism of Reformist-trunk drift: retaining the correct-sounding vocabulary ('withering away') while stripping out the revolutionary content (violent rupture) -- a doctrine node can be worded identically to a Scientific-trunk node while functionally being Reformist if the tag vector's rupture-affirming component is dropped.

findings.case: Lenin's definition of self-criticism as a party's rectification sequence
findings.evidence: Frankly acknowledging a mistake, ascertaining the reasons for it, analyzing the conditions that have led up to it, and thrashing out the means of its rectification—that is the hallmark of a serious party.
findings.implication: Lenin's own definition is a diagnostic-then-corrective sequence (acknowledge -> analyze conditions -> fix) — the direct template for the self_criticism Party Congress action's internal steps, distinct from trunk_shift (changes doctrine) or rectification (purges cadre).

findings.case: Lenin's letter to A. V. Lunacharsky, March 1917, declining a unity conference with the Vperyod group and Martov
findings.evidence: Independence and separateness of our Party, no rapprochement with other parties, are indispensable conditions for me.
findings.implication: A private, low-stakes moment showing the discipline behind the 'no optimal path' law in practice: even a low-cost, friendly conference proposal is refused because any rapprochement is read as trunk-compromising. Doctrine Tree factions should be able to mechanically refuse cheap unity offers when it would blur their trunk identity.

findings.case: Lenin's remarks at the Party Council session on co-optation offers during the Bolshevik/Menshevik split, January 1904
findings.evidence: The Central Committee on an earlier occasion made an offer of two to nine
findings.implication: Concrete proportional-representation math used to try to buy off a faction mid-split (2 minority seats added to a 9-member body) -- usable as a real historical ratio for a trunk_shift/co-optation mechanic that adjusts faction composition on the central committee-equivalent.
findings.parameter: Central Committee (9 named members) offered to co-opt 2 minority members (2:9 ratio, Nov 12/25 1903 ultimatum); Central Organ editorial board composition cited as 1:4 -- candidate co-optation ratios for a splits/rectification mechanic.

findings.case: Lenin's speech against Bund federalism, 2nd RSDLP Congress, 20 July (2 Aug) 1903
findings.evidence: Federation is harmful because it sanctions segregation and alienation, elevates them to a principle, to a law.
findings.implication: The historical Bund/RSDLP fight over federation-vs-centralism is a direct real-world instance of the trunk axis: an autonomist-leaning national organization demanding structural separateness, refused on the grounds that principled fragmentation forecloses common discipline -- template material for the Autonomist trunk's tension with Scientific/centralist doctrine.
findings.parameter: Lenin cites a '90 per cent... majority' at the 2nd Congress as the aspirational supermajority for party unity -- candidate threshold for a trunk-shift/unity vote requirement.

findings.case: Lenin, 'One Step Forward, Two Steps Back,' Ch. K — retrospective on the Council-composition fight, 2nd RSDLP Congress 1903
findings.evidence: the minutes of the Congress clearly show that, in comparison with Paragraph 1, this question was indeed only a detail
findings.implication: Confirms the true load-bearing fork of the 1903 split was the membership clause (Paragraph 1 — who counts as a Party member), while the Council/Central-Organ-vs-Central-Committee fight that consumed the most floor time (24th-25th sittings) was secondary. The Doctrine Tree's 'no optimal path' law should let a single narrow parameter (like a membership-definition node) be more trunk-determining than large visible structural fights.

findings.case: Lovestone (majority) vs. Foster-Bittelman (minority) six-year CPUSA factional war, 1923-1929, per ECCI's 'On the Road to Bolshevization'
findings.evidence: principles served chiefly to camouflage the struggle for supremacy in the Party
findings.implication: The Comintern's own ruling: a six-year 'line struggle' was substantially a power struggle wearing doctrinal costume. The doctrine tree/Congress mechanic needs a way to represent and detect cosmetic trunk-divergence — factions that manufacture ideological distance to justify a supremacy fight, which a Congress action should be able to expose as 'unprincipled.'
findings.parameter: Six years of near-continuous factional struggle, 1923-1929.

findings.case: Lovestone-Foster CPUSA faction fight and CI-directed correction, 1928-29
findings.evidence: The letter also called for the postponement of the Party Convention until February 1929.
findings.implication: A real staged Party-Congress correction sequence (soft letter -> sharp letter -> emissaries with plenipotentiary powers -> floor vote -> expulsion) spanning about seven months from the triggering Sixth Comintern Congress to the convention showdown — grounds a multi-month, multi-stage escalation structure for the trunk_shift/rectification action rather than a single instant event.
findings.parameter: ~7 months from Sixth Congress (Aug 1928) to CPUSA Sixth Convention showdown (Mar 1 1929); ended with Lovestone/Bittelman withdrawn and Foster installed as general secretary.

findings.case: Mao's 1940 definition of the New Democracy doctrine trunk (class alliance with the national bourgeoisie), explicitly time-bounded
findings.evidence: What is new-democratic constitutional government? It is the joint dictatorship of several revolutionary classes over the traitors and reactionaries.
findings.implication: The trunk node itself declares its own expiry ('not yet the Soviet type... we must therefore do without it for the time being') -- a template for a Reformist/class-alliance doctrine node whose validity is explicitly time-bounded rather than a permanent optimum, reinforcing 'no optimal path.'

findings.case: Mao's Two Talks on Mutual Aid and Co-operation, naming Right vs Left deviation, 1953
findings.evidence: To mark time and make no advance is a deviation to the Right; to go beyond what is practicable is a deviation to the 'Left'. Both are manifestations of subjectivism
findings.implication: An explicit two-sided doctrine-trap map from a Party leader's own mouth, three years before the Great Leap: excessive caution (Reformist-trunk liquidationism analog) and rash advance (Insurrectionist-trunk adventurism analog) are named as symmetric failure modes of the SAME underlying error (subjectivism), directly supporting a DAG with traps on both ends of a trunk rather than a single 'wrong' direction.

findings.case: Mao's own acknowledgment of a recurring 'zigzag' pattern of over-correction and under-correction, 1961-62 retrospective on the Great Leap
findings.evidence: But "things come in threes," so perhaps we have to prepare for a third period of zigzags.
findings.implication: The architect of the line himself models correction as cyclical and recurring, not a one-time fix — direct warrant for making Congress/rectification a repeatable, expected mechanic (with the possibility of over-correcting into the opposite trap) rather than a single permanent cure.
findings.parameter: 2 zigzag/correction cycles observed by 1960 (the 1956-57 anti-'daring-advance' swing and the 1958-59 Leap-then-Lushan swing), a 3rd anticipated — candidate ZIGZAG_CYCLE_CADENCE_YEARS ≈ 2-3

findings.case: Mao's rule for classifying 'Left' vs Right deviation during the 1955 agricultural co-operation debate
findings.evidence: it is a "Left" deviation if your judgment is premature, and it is a Right deviation if your judgment lags behind.
findings.implication: A clean, formalizable rule: the SAME action is a Left-trap if taken before conditions are 'ripe' and a Right-trap if withheld after they are. Doctrine-tree traps should be keyed to a readiness-vs-timing mismatch, not to the action in isolation.

findings.case: National Defense Committee convention, back room of a saloon, 228 Oak St. Chicago, January 9-10 1921
findings.evidence: held in a room back of a saloon at 228 Oak Street, Chicago, January 9-10, 1921, with 63 delegates from the United States and 2 from Canada. It lasted 13 hours and none were permitted to leave.
findings.implication: A hard historical data point for an underground-period Party Congress: small delegate count, single marathon session, physical security lockdown. Congress duration/cost should scale down sharply (from multi-day open conventions to single 13-hour lockdown sessions) once a trunk is operating underground.
findings.parameter: 65 delegates total; 13-hour continuous session; zero permitted exits during session.

findings.case: Ninth National Congress of the CPC (April 1969), convened 13 years after the Eighth, its procedure spanning group discussion (from April 2) through plenary adoption (April 14) to Central Committee election (from April 15)
findings.evidence: Starting from April 15, the Ninth National Congress of the Communist Party of China will take up the third item on the agenda, the election of the Central Committee.
findings.implication: Confirms Congress-as-multi-phase-event (discussion -> line-document ratification -> personnel/leadership election), each on its own calendar day, and that full National Congress intervals can stretch past a decade under crisis -- 'trunk_shift'-capable Congress events should be rare/expensive while lower conferences stay frequent/cheap.
findings.parameter: 48 years from founding (1921) to Ninth Congress (1969); Eighth-to-Ninth Congress gap = 13 years (crisis-compressed cadence).

findings.case: Ninth National Congress of the CPC, April 1969 — reconciling rival mass-faction leaders after the Cultural Revolution's trap-cascade
findings.evidence: Didn't the 'August 27th' and the 'Red Headquarters' in the end cooperate?
findings.implication: A real Congress functioning explicitly as a faction-reconciliation mechanism: Mao frames prior mass-faction violence in Nanking and elsewhere as 'a quarrel among the people' (non-antagonistic) and cites their eventual cooperation as the model outcome — a concrete case of a Congress performing exactly the trunk_shift/rectification role the mechanic needs, resolving factional splits rather than purging them.
findings.parameter: Presidium of the reconsolidating Congress numbered 176 members (per the companion roster document), reconstituted roughly 3 years into the Cultural Revolution trap-cascade (1966-1969) — candidate CONGRESS_RECONSOLIDATION_ROSTER_SIZE = 176, TRAP_TO_CONGRESS_LATENCY_YEARS ≈ 3

findings.case: October League's yearlong programless 'temporary leading body,' 1975
findings.evidence: they actually proposed that their party would go for a full year without a political program to unite and guide it
findings.implication: A concrete Autonomist-trunk failure mode: maximal internal democracy marketed as anti-bureaucratic virtue that in practice produces a directionless 'un-center' unable to commit to a line — precedent for the Autonomist trunk's 'Dissociation' trap.
findings.parameter: 1 year proposed with no binding political program (announced Nov 1975).

findings.case: Organising Committee incident, 3rd sitting of the 2nd Congress, 1903
findings.evidence: under the guise of a "reply to the debate", confront the Congress with a new decision of the Organising Committee on the very subject that had been under discussion, was like a stab in the back!
findings.implication: A body can legally spring a new decision after debate closes but before the vote, ambushing the floor. Model a hard 'debate-closed' state in the Party Congress mechanic with a rare, high-legitimacy-cost 'procedural coup' move available to any represented body.

findings.case: Otto Rühle (KAPD) vs Bolshevik centralism at the Second Comintern Congress, Moscow 1920
findings.evidence: Centralism is the principle of organization proper to the capitalist-bourgeois era. Following this principle, one can construct the Bourgeois State and the capitalist economy.
findings.implication: This is a direct historical statement of the Autonomist trunk's founding axiom against the Scientific/vanguard trunk: centralized party-form itself is coded as bourgeois-inherited, not neutral. Grounds the DT thesis that ideology 'determines what you WILL BECOME' — Rühle predicts hypercentralism degenerates into bureaucracy regardless of stated goals, which is exactly the Scientific trunk's mechanically-triggered trap (Bureaucratic degeneration).

findings.case: PRRWO (Puerto Rican Revolutionary Workers Organization) split ahead of its own founding Congress, 1972
findings.evidence: It resulted in a split in the organization in May of 1972, 2 months before the Congress was to take place
findings.implication: A scheduled Party Congress creates a forcing function that precipitates splits in the run-up rather than resolving them at the event itself — Congress mechanics should model a pre-congress volatility spike, not just a post-congress correction.
findings.parameter: Split-to-Congress lead time: 2 months (May split → July 1972 Congress).

findings.case: PRRWO's own polemic citing Stalin on the danger of one-sided anti-leftism, quoted 1976
findings.evidence: while fighting to root out and defeat the main danger, communists must not be one-sided and totally ignore the secondary danger– for this will then create favorable conditions for this secondary danger to grow
findings.implication: A doctrinal citation (not just an observed pattern) of the 'no optimal path' law used inside the actual movement's own theory — directly supports the design law with an in-period source the movement itself considered authoritative.

findings.case: Prague Conference definition of Liquidationism (the 'fight for right')
findings.evidence: the liquidators actually preached the so-called "fight for right", that is to say, a liberal fight for the "renovation" of the June Third regime by partial improvements
findings.implication: Gives the mechanically-checkable trigger for the Reformist trunk's Liquidationism trap: isolating one partial/legal demand from 'the sum total of all the revolutionary demands' — a concrete condition a trap-detector could test for (single-issue legalism replacing the whole programme).

findings.case: Public criticism of Liang Shu-ming, September 1953 (rural-culturalist Reformist deviation)
findings.evidence: reactionary elements, enemy gendarmes and agents, heads of reactionary secret societies, hooligans and ruffians, landlords and rich peasants have wormed their way in, become cadres
findings.implication: A documented Reformist-trunk deviation handled not by expulsion but by public shaming plus continued token committee membership as 'live teaching material' — a real middle path between full trap-ending and full escape, useful for a partial-consequence rectification outcome.
findings.parameter: In the 'backward' 10% of townships nationally, 80-90% of cadre law/discipline violations were attributed to infiltrators vs. the remainder to organic 'degenerate cadres.'

findings.case: RCP May Day 1980 mobilization failure
findings.evidence: the RCP got far less than the 50,000 people it predicted would turn out for their events.
findings.implication: A hard numeric Adventurist-trunk overreach penalty: predicted 50,000, delivered ~600 actual marchers nationwide. Candidate coefficient for how catastrophically an Insurrectionist/Adventurist trunk's mobilization forecasts miss when propaganda substitutes for real mass-line work.
findings.parameter: predicted 50,000 vs. actual ~600 (most local marches only 35-50 people) ≈ 1.2% realization rate — candidate Adventurist-trunk overreach penalty.

findings.case: RCP's own general-lessons summation on left vs. right deviation cycling, 1978
findings.evidence: 'left' errors have more arisen mainly under conditions when the communists were more separated from the working class struggle, and right errors have more arisen as our ties have deepened with the workers' daily struggles
findings.implication: A direct historical-actor statement of the 'no optimal path' design law: correcting for isolation (a left/adventurist trap) increases exposure to co-optation (a right/liquidationist trap), and vice versa — the vulnerability profile is structurally coupled to which trap you most recently avoided, not eliminable.

findings.case: RSDLP Council resolves to convene the Third Party Congress, January 1904
findings.evidence: Being convinced that the central Party institutions are powerless to terminate the absolutely abnormal and disorganising relations which have developed in the Party since the Second Congress and have continued for over five months
findings.implication: The trigger condition for a corrective congress is explicit institutional deadlock persisting past a stated duration, not a single dramatic event -- gives a design template for an automatic 'call congress' trigger keyed to sustained faction gridlock rather than a one-shot threshold.
findings.parameter: 5+ months of unresolved central-institution deadlock cited as the threshold that forced convening a corrective Congress (though the actual 3rd Congress didn't convene until April 1905, ~14 months later) -- candidate range for a congress-trigger cooldown/deadlock-duration coefficient.

findings.case: RSDLP Party Rules on Congress cadence (adopted 1903, cited 1904 Council)
findings.evidence: The congress is convened (if possible not less than once in two years) by the Party Council.
findings.implication: Direct textual source for the Party Congress cadence constant in the Doctrine Tree correction mechanic.
findings.parameter: Mandated minimum congress cadence = 2 years (RSDLP Party Rules, 1903).

findings.case: Revolutionary Union's 1972-73 National Liaison Committee proposal to build 'the Party' via merged secretariats and 'flying squadron' work-teams
findings.evidence: All of this would be topped off in about a year at a Congress where the party would be called into being with a program and permanent leading body elected
findings.implication: An aggressive fixed-timeline party-declaration plan, decoupled from actual base-building, is a recognizable real-world marker of the Adventurist trunk's trap -- a 'declare the party in N ticks' action pattern is a good in-game signal to weight toward Adventurism.
findings.parameter: ~1-year fixed timeline targeted for full party formation (proposed, later collapsed into the 'Broken Alliance' split)

findings.case: Robert Knight (trade-union secretary) vs Wilhelm Liebknecht as personified trunk archetypes (WITBD Ch.3 sec. E)
findings.evidence: The Social-Democrat's ideal should not be the trade union secretary, but the tribune of the people
findings.implication: Lenin picks two named, real contemporaries as living embodiments of the Reformist trunk (sectoral bargaining) vs the Scientific/vanguard trunk (addressing all classes) — a ready-made pair of NPC archetypes for doctrine-tree flavor text.

findings.case: Ruthenberg's fork over centralized-party vs. federated-language organizing, driving his move from CPA to CLP/UCP
findings.evidence: Ruthenberg did not want segregated language federations in the Party — he stood for a centralized Party. This is why he went, along with Jay Lovestone, from the CPA to the CLP to found the United Communist Party.
findings.implication: A real historical fork over organizational architecture (federalist/autonomist organizing vs. centralized vanguard) drove an actual faction split, with named cadre literally walking to a new party over this exact axis -- a clean Autonomist-vs-Scientific trunk case.

findings.case: SPD secretary Ignaz Auer to Bernstein, 1899, on revisionism
findings.evidence: one does not formally make a decision to do the things you suggest, one doesn't say such things, one simply does them
findings.implication: Real trunk drift often happens BELOW the level of a formal Congress vote — an apparatus can silently practice revisionism/liquidationism while its formal doctrine still condemns it. The doctrine tree should allow a 'shadow' tag-vector drift (practice diverging from declared trunk) that only a rectification/self_criticism event can expose and reconcile.

findings.case: Same Bund speech — what non-trapped autonomy looks like
findings.evidence: autonomy in the sense of freedom from petty interference by the central body, although the duty of obeying that body will, of course, remain
findings.implication: Distinguishes safe autonomy (operational independence plus retained duty of obedience) from the Dissociation-trap version (full federation, no accountability) — a two-tier design for the Autonomist trunk: a stable 'autonomous-but-loyal' plateau versus the 'federated-and-exited' trap state.

findings.case: Same Congress, on adopting an annual Party delegates'-congress cadence as an explicit unity-maintenance mechanism, distinct from the (13-years-apart) National Congresses
findings.evidence: In the past we did not have the systems of holding a delegates' congress every year; we held other kinds of meetings. Now we hold a congress every year.
findings.implication: Suggests a two-tier Congress design: frequent, cheap delegate conferences that maintain unity and absorb small line disagreements, versus rare, expensive National Congresses that actually shift the trunk -- matching the 13-year gap between the Eighth (1956/58) and Ninth (1969) National Congresses despite constant lower-tier meetings in between.
findings.parameter: annual cadence for lower-tier unity conferences (post-1956) vs. ~13-year gap between National Congresses under crisis compression.

findings.case: Same chapter — Popov vs. Akimov exchange over Central Organ predominance
findings.evidence: I am prepared to combat it with every means in my power, because it is the banner of opportunism.
findings.implication: Shows the mechanism of factional accusation itself: a procedural preference (decentralized vs. centralized leadership) gets tagged 'opportunism' and becomes a trunk marker, later claimed by both sides against each other. Models how DT tag vectors (e.g., CLASS_ANALYSIS vs NATIONALISM axis) get attached retroactively to organizational choices that were originally about mundane structure.

findings.case: Same chapter — vote on Paragraph 3 (congress representation rules), 2nd Congress 1903
findings.evidence: Paragraph 3 of the Rules, which defines the conditions of representation at congresses, was adopted by a majority with seven abstentions
findings.implication: A concrete abstention count on a procedural rules vote at the same congress that produced the Bolshevik/Menshevik split — usable as a texture data point for how much of a fracturing congress's votes are near-unanimous on secondary rules versus knife-edge on the trunk-defining clause.
findings.parameter: 7 abstentions on a secondary Rules vote (Paragraph 3, 2nd RSDLP Congress, 1903) — candidate baseline for 'normal' procedural dissent versus the sharp split on the trunk-defining clause

findings.case: Second Congress of the Comintern — amendment of the National and Colonial Question theses, replacing 'bourgeois-democratic' with 'national-revolutionary'
findings.evidence: we came to the unanimous decision to talk not about the 'bourgeois-democratic' movement but only about the national-revolutionary movement
findings.implication: A real, procedurally-recorded case of a Congress adjusting the exact wording that separates two adjacent doctrine tags (uncritical support for any nationalist movement vs. class-conditioned support) — direct precedent for how a CLASS_ANALYSIS vs NATIONALISM tag boundary should be something a Congress can redraw by amendment, not just a fixed constant.

findings.case: Second Plenary Session, Seventh CC — village-to-city trunk shift, March 1949
findings.evidence: The centre of gravity of the Party's work has shifted from the village to the city.
findings.implication: A literal, congress-declared trunk shift delivered as a formal report to a specific quorum — a clean template for the Party Congress as trunk_shift correction mechanism, distinct from rectification/self-criticism.
findings.parameter: Second Plenary Session, Seventh CC: 8-day session (March 5-13, 1949), 34 full + 19 alternate members present (53 total) — candidate quorum/cadence for a trunk_shift congress event.

findings.case: Second Session of the Eighth Party Congress (May 1958) ratifying the Great Leap Forward's 'go all out, aim high' general line, explicitly repudiating the cautious 1956 'anti-adventurist' line
findings.evidence: Imbalance, sudden changes and disunity are absolute and permanent.
findings.implication: The direct historical instance of a Party Congress mechanically ratifying a trunk shift toward the high-risk/Insurrectionist pole, with the Congress itself supplying the ideological justification (permanence of imbalance over balance) for abandoning caution -- a template for a trunk_shift event carrying its own doctrinal rationale, not just a numeric coefficient change.

findings.case: Session of the Council of the R.S.D.L.P., resolution 4-II, January 1904
findings.evidence: The Party Council considers it improper for the editorial board of the Central Organ to dispatch representatives to the committees without the knowledge and consent of the Central Committee
findings.implication: A real post-split correction mechanic: the Party Council (an inter-factional body) formally polices boundary violations between the Central Organ (theory/press) and Central Committee (organization) per the prior Congress's division of functions. Maps directly onto the Party Congress's 'rectification' correction type — a standing arbitration body enforcing the last congress's settlement between eruptions.

findings.case: Socialist Party 1919 split producing the Communist Party and subsequent membership collapse, USA
findings.evidence: the membership dropped swiftly from 104,822 in 1919 to but 26,766 in 1920. The decline continued, until it had sunk to 7,425 in 1927.
findings.source: /media/user/data/old-hdd/old-hdd/prolewiki/Exports/Library/Library_History of the Communist Party of the United States.txt
findings.implication: An unusually clean decay curve for a doctrinal/organizational fork - a strong candidate defection-fraction coefficient for what a real trunk split costs an organization over time, distinct from a single purge event.
findings.parameter: Post-split membership retention: ~25% after 1 year, ~7% after 8 years (104,822 -> 26,766 -> 7,425).

findings.case: Socialist Party National Executive Committee's mass expulsion of the left wing, May 24-30 1919, USA
findings.evidence: a total of at least 55,000 members had been dictatorially driven out of the Party
findings.source: /media/user/data/old-hdd/old-hdd/prolewiki/Exports/Library/Library_History of the Communist Party of the United States.txt
findings.implication: A single top-down administrative purge removing over a third of a party's membership in one week - a distinct 'fast fracture' fork event type versus the multi-year attrition case, useful for modeling a sudden trunk_shift cost.
findings.parameter: 55,000 members expelled in a single week-long series of NEC actions (May 24-30, 1919).

findings.case: Sojourner Truth Organization's break from the NCM's cadre-vanguard model toward workplace autonomism, early 1970s
findings.evidence: self-motivated actions by workers such as wildcat strikes and plant takeovers, and alternative-union formations
findings.implication: Grounds the Autonomist trunk in a real, named 1970s organizational lineage distinct from the vanguard-party trunks (Reformist/Insurrectionist/Scientific), useful for historically-faithful node/flavor-text naming.

findings.case: Speech to Regional Secretaries — work-teams obstructing their own rectification, July 1966
findings.evidence: We must rely on internal forces in the schools. The work teams won't do. I won't do, you won't do.
findings.implication: A real case where the externally-imposed correction apparatus itself became the obstruction and had to be discarded for ground-level actors — directly parallel to the EH design law: you cannot rectify a line from outside any more than you can SCOUT your way to intelligence.

findings.case: Stalin's "Trotskyism or Leninism" (1924) definition of trunk divergence
findings.evidence: Trotskyism possesses three specific features which bring it into irreconcilable contradiction with Leninism.
findings.implication: A primary-source three-axis definition of trunk divergence (stance on revolution/peasantry, stance on party organization, stance on leadership legitimacy) — a template for structuring the tag vector as multiple load-bearing axes rather than one left-right scalar.

findings.case: Stalin's 1905 'Armed Insurrection and Our Tactics' vs. spontaneist ('khvostist') tendency
findings.evidence: For those who worship "spontaneity," who degrade the Party's objects to the level of simply following in the wake of life, who drag at the tail and do not march at the head
findings.implication: A primary internal-debate document (spontaneist tailism vs. organized vanguard insurrection) that grounds the Insurrectionist trunk's internal variance, not merely its trap — useful for modeling sub-trunk factional pull before a hard trap trigger.

findings.case: Stalin's Nov. 1924 response to Trotsky's 'Lessons of October'
findings.evidence: As for reprisals, I am distinctly opposed to them. We need no reprisals now, but a developed battle of ideas against the resurrection of Trotskyism.
findings.implication: A documented instance of the ruling faction explicitly framing line-correction as ideological ('theoretical_offensive') rather than punitive, even though historical reprisals followed within years — useful for scripting how a Party Congress publicly claims a rectification path before it can later escalate into an actual purge/trap.

findings.case: Stalinist line reversals catalogued 1923-1939
findings.evidence: In 1933 they denounced the reformists as a 'wing of fascism' in 1939 they signed a pact with Hitler 'in the interests of world peace'
findings.implication: Three documented 180-degree doctrine reversals in a single organization within 6-16 year windows (red-unions→popular-front 1934; snail's-pace→catch-up 1928; social-fascism→Hitler-pact 1933-39) show trunk position CAN flip entirely without an honest theoretical_offensive — driven by bureaucratic-apparatus interest, not study. Model rapid, low-TL-cost trunk flips as a signature of Bureaucratic-degeneration specifically, distinct from an honest, TL-expensive Party Congress rectification.
findings.parameter: documented reversal cadence: 3 full doctrinal 180s across a ~16-year span (1923-1939), each taking 5-11 years to fully invert

findings.case: Structural non-transferability of the Bolshevik party-form to Germany
findings.evidence: 90% peasants in Russia, 35% in Germany
findings.implication: The authors use this ratio to argue no organizational form is portable across differing material bases — direct textual support for 'the tree must have no optimal path': trunk fitness is a function of the material substrate (class composition), not an intrinsic doctrine quality.
findings.parameter: Peasant share of population: ~90% (Russia, 1917) vs ~35% (Germany, 1918) — proposed as the structural reason the Bolshevik party-form could not simply be replicated in Germany.

findings.case: Tenth ECCI Plenum, July 1929 — Lozovsky/Thälmann ultra-left ascendancy
findings.evidence: it was not the fascists who were going to drive the Communist parties underground, but the Social Democrats
findings.implication: A Congress/Plenum meant as a correction mechanism instead ratified and hardened the sectarian line just before catastrophe (KPD banned by 1933). Party Congresses in the doctrine tree should be modeled as capable of making things WORSE (a bad trunk_shift), not a guaranteed rectification — the correction mechanism itself needs a failure mode.

findings.case: The 'Marsh' (Bolото) — the vacillating centrist bloc at the 2nd Congress
findings.evidence: the unstable elements who vacillate between the contending sides have always been designated
findings.implication: An explicit swing/undecided bloc, not aligned to any trunk tag, whose members flip per-vote under momentary pressure rather than fixed loyalty — this is the historical mechanism behind the 'no optimal path' design law: courting the Marsh is unreliable and never permanently secured.

findings.case: The 'New Course' resolution, Politburo, December 1923
findings.evidence: In fact, only Trotsky fought for the implementation of the policy. The majority of the Party leadership adapted itself instead to pressures coming from below.
findings.implication: A unanimously-adopted Party Congress rectification resolution failed anyway because the apparatus that was supposed to execute it had already been captured by the material forces (bureaucracy) it targeted — grounds the design rule that a 'rectification' action must actually reduce the trap's driving stat, not just fire a scripted event; passing a vote is necessary but not sufficient.

findings.case: The 'Right' Socialists' boring-from-within success and later collapse inside the AFL, per Foster 1921
findings.evidence: They came to control over 1/3rd of the votes in that body. But the waves of dualism submerged the movement and it has practically dissolved.
findings.implication: A dateable, quantified boring-from-within peak (roughly a third of convention votes) followed by total dissolution once dual unionism resurfaced -- a real historical curve for a Reformist-trunk gain that was reversible and was in fact reversed, useful for calibrating how fast Reformist-trunk influence can both accumulate and evaporate.
findings.parameter: Peak internal-infiltration share before collapse: ~1/3 (33%) of AFL convention votes

findings.case: The 11th Congress (Aug 1977) fails to correct the Cultural Revolution's errors; the Third Plenary Session (Dec 1978) succeeds where it failed
findings.evidence: owing to the limitations imposed by the prevailing historical conditions and the influence of Comrade Hua Guofeng's mistakes, it reaffirmed the erroneous theories, policies and slogans of the 'cultural revolution' instead of correcting them.
findings.implication: Even a full, formally-convened Party Congress can fail to correct a trap and instead RATIFY the status quo. The correction mechanic must allow a Congress/rectification event to resolve as a non-correction, with a distinct later plenum required to actually flip the trunk.
findings.parameter: 16 months elapsed between the failed 11th Congress (Aug 1977) and the successful corrective Third Plenary Session (Dec 1978).

findings.case: The 1972 National Liaison Committee (PRRWO, RU, IWK, BWC) attempted four-way party-building merger, collapsed
findings.evidence: the RU thought that they were the party and wanted both the BWC and the PRRWO to 'submerge' ourselves into the RU
findings.implication: A failed multi-org trunk-merger case: one org tried to force premature unification ('flying squadrons' + a rush Congress within about a year) rather than genuine line-struggle-to-unity, producing the 'Broken Alliance' instead of a merged trunk.

findings.case: The 25%-income classification rule for peasant class status, 'On Some Important Problems of the Party's Present Policy', 1948
findings.evidence: classified as middle peasants if such income is less than 25 per cent of their total income, and classified as rich peasants if it is more.
findings.implication: A precise, numeric, materially-grounded classification threshold in the Aleksandrov-Test spirit — a template for defining doctrine-tree tag-vector cutoffs (e.g., a CLASS_ANALYSIS/NATIONALISM ratio) as explicit numeric thresholds rather than fuzzy judgment calls.
findings.parameter: 25% exploitation-income threshold distinguishing middle from rich peasant classification, 1948

findings.case: The 7,000-Cadre Conference, January 1962, summing up the Great Leap Forward
findings.evidence: the enlarged Central Work Conference attended by 7,000 people made a preliminary summing-up of the positive and negative experience of the Great Leap Forward and unfolded criticism and self-criticism.
findings.implication: A named, sized rectification congress (7,000 cadres) convened roughly 3 years after the trap's onset (1958-1962) — gives a concrete headcount and lag for what a 'theoretical_offensive'/'self_criticism' correction-congress event looks like at scale.
findings.parameter: 7,000 attendees; ~3-4 year lag between trap onset (1958) and major correction congress (1962)

findings.case: The Bremerlinke -- a fixed doctrinal position converted into heresy purely by the Party's own line-shift
findings.evidence: The Bremerlinke appeared as the first and disappeared as the last expression of Leninism in Germany.
findings.implication: A faction that never changed its own position was first celebrated as orthodox Leninism, then expelled as 'rightist' in 1928 once the Congress line moved leftward -- trap/trunk classification must be computed relative to the CURRENT Congress line, not a static absolute doctrine threshold.

findings.case: The Lovestoneite faction's independent post-expulsion trajectory as the 'International Communist Opposition,' 1937
findings.evidence: unlike the official Communist parties or the Trotsky groups the world over, we have never been, nor are we now, associated with any tendency or faction
findings.implication: Not every expelled faction cascades toward Strasserism/PatSoc or dissolves -- an expelled trunk can persist as a stable, independent third-position node. The DT design should include a non-terminal 'principled minority holdout' outcome alongside the four trunk traps.

findings.case: The September 1-2, 1919 founding split of American communism into the Communist Party of America and Communist Labor Party, Chicago
findings.evidence: which leaves the Socialist Party not only without a single member, but even owing something like 7,000 members to the other parties
findings.implication: At the exact moment of a trunk split, both successor factions' self-reported membership summed to MORE than the parent body had. A trunk_shift/split event should not trust either faction's self-reported cadre count at the moment of divergence -- both inflate simultaneously.
findings.parameter: Parent Socialist Party membership ceiling in 1919: 109,000; combined claims of the two Sept. 1919 splinters: 58,000 (CP, 128 delegates) plus a rival claim of "at least equal strength" (CLP) -- over 100% overcounting within 24 hours.

findings.case: The Shanghai People's Commune naming dispute, February 1967: whether a mass 'commune' form should replace the vanguard Party
findings.evidence: The commune must have a party, but can the commune replace the party?
findings.implication: A live, in-the-room fork between an Autonomist path (commune replaces Party) and continued vanguard-Leninist organization, resolved by Mao rejecting the Autonomist/Dissociation direction and insisting a Party 'nucleus' remain. NOTE: this document is dated February 1967 (Shanghai Commune episode) and concerns the Paris-Commune/soviet-form debate, not the 1975 'new bourgeois class' material the assignment description described; no document matching that description was found at this path or elsewhere in the assigned set, so this finding uses the actual 1967 content instead.

findings.case: The Tenth Plenum of the Eighth Central Committee (Sept 24, 1962), reasserting the 'never forget class struggle' hardline trunk after the Great Leap's partial retreat
findings.evidence: Therefore, from now on we must talk about this every year, every month, every day. We will talk about it at congresses, at Party delegate conferences, at plenums, at every meeting we hold.
findings.implication: A real trunk_shift-back-again event: rather than a single corrective action, Mao mandates continuous, layered reinforcement across every organizational tier -- doctrine 'reversion' modeled as a saturation campaign rather than a one-shot fix, supporting a decay-resistant reinforcement mechanic for trunk_shift.

findings.case: The two rectification/anti-rightist campaigns bracketing the Great Leap: 1957 and the 1959 Lushan Conference
findings.evidence: The two rectification and antirightist campaigns, one in 1957, one at Lushan, subjected the effects of bourgeois ideology and remaining bourgeois influences to comparatively thoroughgoing criticism
findings.implication: A real, named cadence for repeated rectification events (roughly two years apart) bracketing a period of doctrinal crisis — usable directly as a tunable interval for how often a Party Congress/rectification event can plausibly recur under stress.
findings.parameter: RECTIFICATION_CAMPAIGN_CADENCE_YEARS ≈ 2 (1957 anti-Rightist campaign to 1959 Lushan Conference)

findings.case: Theoretical Review's 1980 retrospective check on whether the post-1976 party-building movement broke with NCM dogmatism
findings.evidence: In 1976, the anti-dogmatist, anti-revisionist movement stood at the crossroads of either developing new bases for a genuine communist party or else replicating the essential course of the NCM
findings.implication: A useful in-game 'generational audit' cadence: roughly every 4 years, check whether an organization's trunk has genuinely progressed past its founding-era errors or is replaying them under new labels -- could gate a periodic trap-recurrence check distinct from the Party Congress's shorter correction cycle.
findings.parameter: ~4-year generational audit window (1976 crossroads -> 1980 retrospective verdict of failure-to-break-with-predecessors)

findings.case: Third Comintern Congress (1921) as the correction mechanism after the KPD's ultra-left March Action
findings.evidence: The Third Congress resolved nothing, since it supported the Central Committee and was in favor of the strengthening of the KPD through its penetration of the masses.
findings.implication: A real historical Party Congress convened explicitly to correct a failed adventurist action but produced an ambiguous, contested outcome rather than a clean fix — supports modeling trunk_shift/rectification as probabilistic and partial rather than a guaranteed full reset, with real cost (a whole congress cycle) for an uncertain payoff.

findings.case: Three-decade decline of radical influence in American unions after the dual-unionism turn (early 1890s to 1921), per Foster
findings.evidence: from then on radical influence among the unions waned and waned until now it is next to zero.
findings.implication: A long, slow doctrinal-drift decay curve (not a sudden collapse): once radicals abandoned mass unions for pure/separatist organizing, influence didn't crash but eroded steadily over a generation to near-zero -- a good shape reference for a slow Autonomist-drift decay rate rather than an instant trap trigger.
findings.parameter: Decay timescale from peak radical influence to 'next to zero': ~30 years (early 1890s -> 1921)

findings.case: Trotsky-Stalin split: Permanent Revolution vs. Socialism in One Country
findings.evidence: Trotsky occupied a middle position between the Bolsheviks and Mensheviks but in essence was closer to the Mensheviks.
findings.implication: This is the canonical historical trunk-fork the DAG's core divergence should be checkable against: both factions agreed on proletarian revolution and rejected liberal-bourgeois alliance, but split irreconcilably over whether the peasantry counts as a dependable ally and whether revolution can consolidate nationally before global victory — a load-bearing tag-vector axis, not a personality dispute.

findings.case: Trotsky-Stalin struggle inside the CPSU and Comintern, 1922-1927
findings.evidence: The struggle raged over a period of five years (1922-27) during which time the Trotsky bloc had access to the press and Trotsky's works were widely circulated for everyone to read.
findings.implication: A trunk-line struggle ran five years with the losing faction retaining publication/circulation rights throughout, supporting a slow-burn line-struggle model where expulsion is the terminal, not initial, correction step.
findings.parameter: 5-year struggle (1922-27); Trotsky's position received only "two votes out of the whole body" at the Nov 1926 Seventh Plenum of the ECCI; 74 supporters expelled alongside Trotsky/Zinoviev/Kamenev in Nov-Dec 1927 — candidate defection-fraction figures.

findings.case: Tsunyi Conference, enlarged Political Bureau meeting, January 1935
findings.evidence: This error was corrected at the enlarged meeting of the Political Bureau of the Central Committee at Tsunyi in January 1935.
findings.implication: A real Party Congress/emergency conclave is the historical original of the 'trunk_shift' correction mechanic — it fires once, discretely, after a multi-year error, not continuously; the engine's Congress event should likewise be a rare, discrete, line-reversing event rather than a smooth dial.
findings.parameter: Error incubated 1931-34 (~3 years) before a single emergency Congress corrected it — candidate CONGRESS_CORRECTION_LATENCY ≈ 3 years

findings.case: Union of Russian Social-Democrats Abroad: economist takeover (1898) to final dissolution (1903)
findings.evidence: The Union was dissolved by a decision of the Second Congress
findings.implication: One organizational vessel drifted from founding orthodoxy (1894) to an opportunist majority (1898) to formal expulsion (1903) — line-struggles inside one org can run most of a decade before a congress finally excises the losing faction.
findings.parameter: line-struggle-to-expulsion duration ~= 9 years (1894 founding -> 1898 opportunist capture -> 1903 dissolution)

findings.case: United Communist Party of America's 'Boycott the Election!' proclamation, Oct. 1920
findings.evidence: You must organize and be prepared so that when the revolutionary crisis comes you can turn a general strike against the government into an armed uprising to overthrow the government.
findings.implication: A clean, dated, hard-line Insurrectionist-trunk manifesto (explicit rejection of electoral participation as inherently corrupting) from the same organizational lineage that, within about sixteen years, would run an electoral Popular Front platform -- documents that a real trunk_shift of this magnitude did occur historically, just not instantaneously.

findings.case: Vote-count arithmetic on co-optation to the central bodies, 2nd Congress
findings.evidence: The total votes were 51, without the Bundists 46, without the Rabocheye Dyelo-ists 43.
findings.implication: In a ~51-seat congress the decisive swing bloc was worth only 3-5 votes — small minority factions can flip control of the central bodies without ever forming a majority themselves, purely by tipping whichever side they join.
findings.parameter: Total Congress votes = 51; Bund delegation = 5 votes (~10%); Rabocheye Dyelo delegation = 3 votes (~6%) — both sufficient to flip composition-of-central-bodies votes decided by margins of 2-6.

findings.case: Wayaobao Conference line-struggle: united front vs closed-doorism vs adventurism, Dec 27 1935
findings.evidence: The present situation demands that we boldly discard all closed-doorism, form a broad united front and guard against adventurism
findings.implication: A three-way tactical fork stated in one sentence: sectarian purity (closed-doorism, an Autonomist/Dissociation-trap analog), adventurism (an Insurrectionist-trap analog), and the correct united-front trunk. Historically resolved the 1927-35 closed-doorism that Mao blames for the Party's setbacks — good primary text for the Reformist/Insurrectionist/Autonomist trunk split itself.

findings.case: William Z. Foster's 'boring-from-within' thesis against dual unionism, 1921
findings.evidence: In the course of many years, tens of thousands of these precious militants have been sucked out of the old unions.
findings.implication: This is the founding logic that separates the Reformist trunk (work inside existing mass organizations) from a dual/Autonomist path (build ideologically pure separate unions): Foster's central charge is that purity-seeking splits bleed off exactly the militant vanguard fraction that mass organizations need to be worth anything.
findings.parameter: Vanguard/militant fraction of union membership: 'not over 1 percent of the whole membership'

findings.case: Yan'an Rectification Movement as the forge of Mao Zedong Thought's party-building theory, 1942-44
findings.evidence: his comprehensive theory on the subject took shape, on the basis of practice, in the Yan'an rectification movement.
findings.source: /media/user/data/old-hdd/old-hdd/prolewiki/Exports/Library/Library_Mao Zedong Thought Must Be Correctly Understood as an Integral Whole.txt
findings.implication: Names a designed rectification campaign as the historical archetype where a full party-building doctrine — not just personnel discipline — was forged, supporting a rectification Congress action that can also mint new doctrine-tree structure, not only punish individual cadre.

findings.case: Zimmerwald Conference, September 1915 — the founding internationalist/social-chauvinist fork
findings.evidence: we who stand, not on the ground of national solidarity with the exploiting class, but on the ground of the international solidarity of the proletariat
findings.implication: This is the archetypal historical trunk fork the whole tree should be checked against: a minority (the Zimmerwald Left) splits from a much larger, resourced Reformist apparatus purely over the CLASS_ANALYSIS-vs-NATIONALISM axis, at real cost (isolation, illegality) and with no guaranteed payoff at the time. Confirms 'no optimal path' is historically true even for the split that later 'won'.

findings.case: National Liaison Committee (RU + Black Workers Congress + PRRWO) formed 1972, fractured over 'Bundism' late 1973
findings.evidence: the majority of the proletariat has not yet developed the necessary class-consciousness to play the leading role in the national struggle or the United Front as a whole
findings.implication: A concrete, dated case of a coalition collapsing along the CLASS_ANALYSIS-vs-NATIONALISM axis when a faction demanded 'special place' for nationality-based vanguard organizations — directly grounds the load-bearing tag-vector design and gives a real timescale for how fast this drift can rupture a coalition.
findings.parameter: NLC formed summer 1972, ruptured 'latter part of 1973' — roughly 12-18 months from coalition founding to nationalist-drift rupture.

findings.case: BWC Interim Committee's un-summarized ideological about-face at an emergency CC meeting, February 1975
findings.evidence: The IC led the CC in changing the line of the organization with no summary at all of the debate and absolutely no written analysis of our so-called 'leftism.'
findings.implication: A corrective 'congress' event captured by factional power struggle rather than genuine rectification — both rival blocs agreed 'leftism' was the danger only to secure Central Committee seats, not from real line analysis. Models a failure mode where the Party Congress mechanic fires but produces no real trunk_shift, only elite reshuffling.

findings.case: RCP's 'Friday night massacre' purge of the July 4th 1976 campaign's Central Committee leadership, 1977-78
findings.evidence: The five CC members who were responsible for politically developing the campaign, leading the four days of activities and providing political summation were all removed from their positions in the Friday night massacre.
findings.implication: Once a rival faction (Avakian's) consolidated Central Committee control, it could remove an entire rival leadership cohort in a single named event — concrete count and timescale for how fast top-leadership purges execute once a trunk faction wins internal control, useful for calibrating a 'purge tick' cost.
findings.parameter: 5 Central Committee members purged in one night ('Friday night massacre') — concrete headcount+timescale for rapid top-leadership removal.

findings.case: 1950-51 'Strike Surely, Accurately and Relentlessly' counter-revolutionary suppression campaign's explicit triage ratio
findings.evidence: roughly 10 to 20 per cent, while those to be sentenced to death but to be granted a reprieve probably account for 80 to 90 per cent, that is to say, 80 to 90 per cent may be saved.
findings.implication: A named, numeric triage ratio for how a state security sweep discriminates hard-core vs. reprievable targets when suppressing infiltrators inside Party/government/army ranks -- directly usable as a coefficient for a state 'purge severity' parameter.
findings.parameter: ~10-20% terminal removal, ~80-90% conditional/reversible (death-with-reprieve, forced labor) when a state security action processes suspected internal infiltrators.

findings.case: 1978 CPUSA/ML split led by an internal infiltrator faction (Thrasher/Suber)
findings.evidence: the emergence and subsequent purging of the ultra-left, anti-Party faction headed by Albert Thrasher of Birmingham, Malcolm Suber of New Orleans and a group of lawyers from New Orleans
findings.implication: Shows infiltration operating as ideological sabotage (steering a party toward self-isolating ultra-left tactics) rather than only external espionage — a second attack surface the EH mechanic should model: adversaries can suppress Mass Receptivity indirectly by driving a faction toward tactics that sever mass ties.

findings.case: 1990 Stanford Daily red-baiting exposé of the League of Revolutionary Struggle, rebutted by two independent letter-writers
findings.evidence: It insinuates a great deal without producing a shred of evidence
findings.implication: N/A — see next entry for the correct source; this quote is drawn from the companion piece and demonstrates the Desert-state pattern: hostile narratives about an org's 'secretive' infiltration can be manufactured and printed with zero underlying relationship-based intelligence.

findings.case: A. Mitchell Palmer's Nov. 1919 Senate Report — Radical Division dossier program
findings.evidence: Since the organization of the Radical Division, a more or less complete history of over 60,000 radically-inclined individuals has been gathered together and classified
findings.implication: The state's own self-assessment shows breadth-without-depth surveillance: 60,000 dossiers and 471 monitored publications (40 translators employed) still could not secure convictions or predict action. Model Desert/Mud as high-volume, low-fidelity intelligence, distinct from cadre-level Water-state understanding that only comes from relationship.
findings.parameter: 60,000 catalogued individuals; 471 monitored radical publications (222 domestic + 144 foreign-received, 106 English + 116 foreign-language domestic); 40 translators/readers on staff — candidate baseline for a large, low-precision 'public-baseline' surveillance term in I_c.

findings.case: A. Mitchell Palmer's public justification of the January 1920 Palmer Raids (Forum magazine, Feb. 1920)
findings.evidence: it is the highest testimony to the services of these men that the Department of Justice of the United States is today a human net that no outlaw can escape
findings.implication: The State's claim of omniscience was made with a Bureau of only ~500 agents nationwide (vs. NYC's own 12,000 police) covering an alleged 60,000 radicals -- the 'sees everywhere but cannot understand' thesis should be tuned so state 'presence' coverage is cheap/wide but its actual per-node Intel Confidence stays low without cultivated informants.
findings.parameter: DOJ investigative force ~500 agents for the entire country (1920) vs. claimed 60,000 organized agitators under suspicion -- a ~1:120 nominal coverage ratio.

findings.case: Agent provocateur N. N. Mikhailov inside the St. Petersburg League of Struggle, 1896-97
findings.evidence: the discovery and arrest were facilitated by an agent provocateur, N. N. Mikhailov, a dentist, who had been in contact with a group associated with the "Decembrists"
findings.implication: A named, professionally-embedded informant (a dentist with legitimate social access) precipitated a whole-group rollup -- models a discrete 'informant' event type that should crater local I_c/M_r sharply rather than merely decaying it, and should be discoverable only after the fact.

findings.case: Agent provocateur N.N. Mikhailov infiltrates the St. Petersburg 'Decembrists' circle, December 1895
findings.evidence: the discovery and arrest were facilitated by an agent provocateur, N. N. Mikhailov, a dentist... the workers were so enraged that they decided to kill him
findings.implication: A single embedded informant rolled up an entire founding cell overnight (raid night of Dec 8 1895) — infiltration should be modeled as a discrete catastrophic event that zeroes cadre-presence for a locale, not gradual decay; discovery of the mole itself becomes a mobilizing/violent event, not just an intel loss.
findings.parameter: 1 informant -> whole cell (dozen-person circle) rolled up in 1 night raid; candidate for infiltration-event severity coefficient.

findings.case: Agent-provocateur false-flags cited by Dimitrov at the Reichstag Fire Trial (Sofia cathedral bombing 1925, Juterbog/Matushka, Gorgulov assassination)
findings.evidence: the Bulgarian Communist Party was persecuted on account of it. That act of provocation, the blowing up of Sofia cathedral, was actually organised by the Bulgarian police
findings.implication: Grounds a 'provocation' event type: the state fabricates a high-tension incident and blames the revolutionary org, which should read in-game as a Desert-state false positive that damages M_r/I_c without any real cadre action having occurred.

findings.case: Anglo-French governmental blockade of returning emigrant revolutionaries, forcing the sealed-train deal (April 1917)
findings.evidence: After waiting two weeks for an answer from Russia, we decided to carry out the above-mentioned plan by ourselves
findings.implication: Intelligence/coordination channels between an underground organization and its own leadership can be physically severed by hostile states (border control, mail interception), forcing costly workarounds -- a channel-interdiction mechanic distinct from pure informant infiltration.
findings.parameter: 2-week wait threshold before the emigrants acted unilaterally rather than continuing to rely on the blocked channel

findings.case: BATF agent Bernard Butkovich's infiltration of the Nazi party that carried out the Greensboro Massacre of the CWP 5, Nov 3 1979
findings.evidence: he taught the Nazis to turn semi-automatic weapons into automatics, and how to get hand grenades. Bernard Butkovich even offered to hide the killers after the murder had been carried out
findings.implication: State infiltration in this case was not passive observation but active enablement of the violence that damaged the revolutionary org -- the Epistemic Horizon's 'Desert' state (falsified data that looks normal) should model infiltration as capable of manufacturing the very events it later 'reveals,' not just concealing truth.

findings.case: BWC's own use of unproven police-agent accusations as a factional weapon against a dissident member
findings.evidence: he started to rumour monger to various cadre that one of the members of the CC was a police agent
findings.implication: Shows infiltration paranoia becoming an internal weapon independent of actual state penetration — a Desert-state effect can be self-inflicted: false 'informant' accusations degrade internal trust/M_r just as effectively as real infiltration.

findings.case: Base-area reversal under leadership error or enemy pressure
findings.evidence: a guerrilla base may turn into a guerrilla zone, and a guerrilla zone may turn into an area under relatively stable enemy occupation
findings.implication: Vision-state decay is explicitly bidirectional and can cascade two full tiers from a single leadership mistake — the M_r decay function should allow multi-tier drops per tick, not only single-step degradation.

findings.case: Bill Dunne (United Communist Party) impersonating a DoJ agent to seize a rival faction's print shop, New York, c. 1920
findings.evidence: Bill Dunne and his companion had phony badges. A crowd from C.E.'s faction followed them in and threw the 'capturers' out!
findings.implication: Falsified state authority was weaponized INTRA-movement, not only by the state against the movement -- broadens Desert-state design: faction-vs-faction deception using the state's own credibility as a prop. Worth modeling faction-on-faction falsified data, not only State-on-Revolution.

findings.case: Bridgman Convention informant Francis A. Morrow at the Foster criminal-syndicalism trial, St. Joseph MI, March 1923
findings.evidence: I must have been a little confused yesterday
findings.implication: The state's 'star witness' was a paid infiltrator whose account visibly disintegrates under cross-examination — the American analog of the Malinovsky-type agent provocateur. Infiltrator-derived intelligence should be modeled as inherently unstable/falsifiable data that looks solid until directly contested, not as reliable Water-tier information.

findings.case: Bureau of Investigation informant staffing memo, Philadelphia, Sept. 1921 (Foster to Hoover)
findings.evidence: In Philadelphia alone, we have more than 28 different organizations affiliated with the COMMUNIST PARTY OF AMERICA - UNIFIED, and with but one paid confidential informant, who only speaks the English language
findings.implication: A concrete informant-to-organization ratio (1:28) and an explicit language/ethnic blind spot the Bureau itself flagged — I_c should not be a single scalar per organization but should vary by language/ethnic subgraph, since state coverage is structurally uneven across factions.
findings.parameter: 1 confidential informant per 28+ organizations in Philadelphia, 1921; proposed remedy was 2 informants x 8 major metro centers (Philadelphia, NY, Boston, Chicago, Detroit, SF, Seattle, New Orleans) = 16 informants nationwide.

findings.case: Bureau of Investigation surveillance and raid on Edward Lindgren ('Flynn'/'Seibert'), NYC, April 27-30 1921
findings.evidence: It will take at least a week or ten days to have detailed report submitted as to the contents of the data seized.
findings.implication: Real infiltration-to-rollup was fast (tip-off to raid in ~3 days) but intelligence PROCESSING lagged a week-plus -- Intel Confidence gains from a successful raid should not resolve instantly; model a 7-10 day processing lag between 'data seized' and usable Intel Confidence.
findings.parameter: Surveillance-to-raid: ~3-4 days (Apr 27 tip to Apr 30 arrests); captured-document processing: 7-10 days; bail set at $50,000 per defendant under the State Anarchy Act.

findings.case: Burns's dictagraph wiretap of the McNamara brothers' privileged conversation with attorney Clarence Darrow, 1911
findings.evidence: obtaining a record of the conversation between the McNamaras and their attorney, Clarence Darrow, by means of the dictagraph
findings.implication: Technological escalation let the state penetrate even privileged, supposedly-protected communication -- a rare, high-cost state action that can puncture even 'cadre-protected' true-state visibility; useful as an expensive Desert-breaking special action for the state AI.

findings.case: CCP demands on the Chiang Kai-shek/Hurley regime for minimum democratic reforms, July 1945
findings.evidence: the release of political prisoners, the abolition of the secret service, and the granting of freedoms to the people
findings.implication: Names the state's secret-service apparatus itself as the primary object of revolutionary demand — confirming that in the EH framing the State's problem is never lack of surveillance reach (it has a secret service) but its disconnection from mass consent, exactly the 'sees everywhere but cannot understand' thesis.

findings.case: CEC Executive Secretary Alfred Wagenknecht's January 1921 correspondence-security directive to District Organizers
findings.evidence: Never write legal names of persons belonging to the party in your correspondence. Several DOs have done this and mail recently opened by the authorities connected these persons with the party.
findings.implication: Concrete tradecraft failure modes existed and were explicitly named by leadership as the cause of compromise -- ESTABLISH_CONTACT_NETWORK should carry a 'discipline' sub-roll (real code use, no real names) whose failure directly feeds the State's Intel Confidence, independent of raw cadre-presence.
findings.parameter: The party maintained two numerical correspondence codes on file at the time (1921).

findings.case: CPA Australia internal security culture, 1950s — paranoia about informants actively degrades counter-intelligence
findings.evidence: Quick stifling of criticism provides the atmosphere in which police agents can flourish.
findings.implication: Over-securitized cadre culture is self-defeating: suppressing internal dissent to guard against provocateurs actually prevents the criticism that would expose a real agent. A game mechanic that rewards blanket suspicion (raising 'security' at the cost of internal Intel/coherence) should carry a hidden penalty to genuine Intel Confidence, mirroring this paradox.

findings.case: CPA Central Executive Committee's 'Rules for Underground Party Work' leaflet, circa March 1920
findings.evidence: they will open up our letters, photograph or copy them, and then send them through to us nicely closed again
findings.implication: The party itself documented that the State's advantage came from CHOOSING not to act on intercepted intelligence, preserving the relationship for later use -- this models an inverse fog: the State's true Water-level knowledge can be deliberately hidden from the Revolution player as a bluff, not merely absent.

findings.case: CPA underground-security leaflet's explicit citation of Tsarist-era Russian underground practice as its source doctrine
findings.evidence: This course of action has been proved best by the experiences of our Russian comrades in their long years of difficult underground struggle against tsarism.
findings.implication: American CP tradecraft (compartmentalization, code use, refuse-to-answer-when-arrested) was a direct, named inheritance from the Okhrana-era Russian underground -- validates modeling a single, historically-continuous 'underground discipline' skill/tech rather than inventing a US-specific one from scratch.

findings.case: CPML polemic citing FBI COINTELPRO and provocateurs against the U.S. left, 1978
findings.evidence: From the use of such FBI stunts as 'Operation Cointelpro' to the activities of provocateurs and hidden opportunist agents, the bourgeoisie's war against working class unity has...become well-known.
findings.implication: Named, era-specific confirmation that COINTELPRO-style infiltration and provocateurs were a live, widely-acknowledged threat model inside the actual New Communist Movement — corroborates using historical infiltration dynamics (not just Okhrana/Malinovsky) as source material for the Desert-state falsification mechanic.

findings.case: CPUSA Organizer's Manual (1935) cell-size rule for factory Shop Units
findings.evidence: The Shop Unit which grows to over 10-12 members should be divided into two independent working groups as quickly as possible.
findings.implication: A hard, numeric security-vs-reach trade-off: cell size is deliberately capped to bound infiltration/exposure risk, at the direct cost of how many masses one cell can personally organize. Good candidate coefficient for a cadre-network node-size cap that trades I_c growth rate against infiltration exposure.
findings.parameter: Max secure cell size before mandatory split: 10-12 members

findings.case: CPUSA/ML split by an infiltrator faction, 1978-79
findings.evidence: Only nine months after the founding of the CPUSA/ML in December 1978, a split was engineered in our Party by a faction of infiltrators.
findings.implication: A concrete rollup timescale for how fast a newly-founded underground org can be compromised: 9 months from founding to an infiltrator-driven split. Also frames the Desert/Water tradeoff as explicit doctrine — Lenin's 'illegal nuclei surrounded by legal strong-points' — with excess secrecy ('left liquidationism') and excess openness ('right liquidationism') both named as fatal.
findings.parameter: 9 months — founding-to-infiltrator-driven-split window for a young underground party; candidate 'young-org vulnerability period' for Desert-state exploitation.

findings.case: California Criminal Syndicalism prosecutions of IWW members, 1919-c.1924
findings.evidence: Five hundred and thirty-one persons were accused by indictment; 292 dismissed without trial; 264 were actually tried; 164 actually convicted.
findings.implication: A small cadre of just three recurring professional informant-witnesses (testifying to their own past IWW membership) drove convictions across 14 counties over 5+ years — a numbered example of how disproportionate a few embedded informants' leverage can be on outcomes, useful for calibrating an 'informant leverage multiplier' distinct from raw informant headcount.
findings.parameter: 531 indicted, 292 dismissed (55%), 264 tried, 164 convicted (31% of indicted, 62% of tried) across 14 of 20 counties over 5+ years; only 3 recurring professional-witness informants used at ALL trials.

findings.case: Chiang-clique infiltration of the Shensi-Kansu-Ningsia Border Region, 1938
findings.evidence: Some of them are even acting as spies, conspiring with bandits, inciting our soldiers to mutiny, making surveys and maps of our region, secretly collecting information
findings.implication: A documented infiltration/agent-provocateur episode bundling propaganda, reconnaissance, and mutiny incitement; the response was a written-authorization entry requirement — a relationship-gate (ESTABLISH_CONTACT_NETWORK-style vetting) rather than pure military hardening.
findings.parameter: 23 counties comprised the Border Region under this counter-infiltration regime — candidate scale reference for a base-area territory count.

findings.case: Cold War police infiltration of the Daughters of Bilitis (DOB)
findings.evidence: police informants had infiltrated DOB during the Cold War and were channeling the names of the group's membership to the FBI and CIA.
findings.implication: Membership-list exfiltration via informant is a distinct, cheaper attack than active provocation — pure intelligence extraction with no disruption. The design should allow a low-cost state action that harvests contact-network data without raising suspicion, degrading the rebel's relational advantage silently.

findings.case: Communist Collective of the Chicano Nation on the Watergate-era repression toolkit against the antiwar/national movements, 1973
findings.evidence: subjected to murder, illegal arrest, break-ins, bugging, physical beating, agents-provocateurs, etc
findings.implication: A period catalog of the full state-repression toolkit (COINTELPRO-adjacent) applied against a movement that lacked its own independent class organization and had 'fallen under the hegemony' of a bourgeois party — the vulnerability was organizational, not merely tactical.

findings.case: Counter-espionage order inside the besieged Kronstadt garrison (Provisional Revolutionary Committee Order No. 6, March 13 1921)
findings.evidence: Communists are abusing the trust which the Prov. Rev. Com. has shown them. They have been discovered attempting to send light signals to the enemy.
findings.implication: A real-time counter-infiltration bulletin: trusted insiders given liberty on parole used covert signals to aid the besieging state -- informant risk here arises from the organization's own leniency toward existing members, not just external plants.

findings.case: Counter-informant killings spreading from prison to the streets, Occupied Palestinian Territories, Intifada era
findings.evidence: some of these groups went as far as killing these collaborators and dumping their bodies in garbage heaps or public squares
findings.implication: Shows the second-order cost of successful counter-infiltration: once a movement starts hunting informants the practice escalates past organizational control and becomes a legitimacy liability, a realistic downside branch for an ESTABLISH_CONTACT_NETWORK counter-intel action.

findings.case: Countering the 'German agent' slander campaign, Petrograd Soviet Executive Committee, 4 April 1917
findings.evidence: In order to put an end to the lies being spread by the bourgeois press, it is essential to adopt the resolution proposed by Comrade Zinoviev.
findings.implication: A real deception episode in class struggle: an informational vacuum let the bourgeois press fabricate a hostile narrative; the Bolshevik countermove was proactive disclosure. Models the Desert state's masked-and-falsified-data risk — if the true state isn't asserted, an adversary narrative fills the gap.

findings.case: DOJ internal memo on the British Secret Service's parallel espionage network in the US, Feb. 1921
findings.evidence: the English were much better informed on radical activities in this country, at least in New York, than the United States Government
findings.implication: Presence does not equal understanding even BETWEEN rival state intelligence services -- a foreign power with cultivated informant relationships (Jonas, Mellin, Finch) out-performed the domestic Bureau. Reinforces that Intel Confidence should be relationship-driven and non-transferable between actors, not a shared 'fog of war' pool.

findings.case: DoJ Bureau of Investigation instructions preceding the Jan. 2, 1920 nationwide Communist Party raids (33 cities)
findings.evidence: If possible, you should arrange with your undercover informants to have meetings of the Communist Party and the Communist Labor Party held on the night set.
findings.implication: The state doesn't just observe meetings, it engineers them through embedded agents to make simultaneous mass arrests possible -- grounds the Desert vision-state's 'data is not hidden but FALSIFIED' design: informants actively shape the object being surveilled, not merely report on it.

findings.case: Exposure of GM informants during the 1936-37 Flint Sit-Down Strike
findings.evidence: Their supporters, working for a U.S. Senate committee investigating industrial espionage, uncovered the names of GM stool pigeons and leaked them to union leaders.
findings.implication: Counter-intelligence came from cadre embedded in a STATE investigative body, not from local mass contact — suggests a secondary, rarer EH channel (infiltrating the state's own apparatus) distinct from AGITATE_LOCALLY/SOCIAL_INVESTIGATION/ESTABLISH_CONTACT_NETWORK.

findings.case: FBI COINTELPRO fomenting the Black Panther Party/US Organization feud, 1969
findings.evidence: tensions largely instigated by the FBI's counterintelligence program), it erected an insurmountable divide between the two organizations
findings.source: /media/user/data/old-hdd/old-hdd/prolewiki/Exports/Library/Library_A Black Women's History of the United States.txt
findings.implication: Confirms the state doesn't just observe under a Desert/falsified-data condition, it actively degrades SOLIDARITY edges between allied orgs — a candidate 'sow discord' state action that damages inter-org edges rather than nodes.

findings.case: FBI COINTELPRO forged-letter split of the Black Panther Party, March 1971
findings.evidence: caused a split between the Newton and Cleaver branches of the party by sending them forged letters accusing one side of working with the police and plotting to kill the other side
findings.implication: This is the literal historical template for Desert-state 'masked data looks normal': the state doesn't need real intelligence on a target, it manufactures internal distrust via forged communications to sever SOLIDARITY edges between allied factions.

findings.case: FBI Key Agitator Index vs. Chicago PD relational harassment of young Fred Hampton
findings.evidence: he was put on the FBI's Key Agitator Index, a list of activists that FBI director J. Edgar Hoover ordered FBI agents to monitor closely.
findings.implication: The state's presence-based surveillance (a name on a list, monitored since before Hampton even joined the Panthers) never converted into actionable understanding until a relational asset (O'Neal, embedded for years) was inserted. This is the 'sees everywhere but cannot understand' thesis almost verbatim: the list alone bought the state nothing until relationship replaced presence.

findings.case: FBI assassination of Fred Hampton, Black Panther Party, Chicago, Dec 4 1969
findings.evidence: The FBI drugged and assassinated him at the age of 21.
findings.implication: Anchors near-zero rollup latency once an informant is embedded at leadership level: the historical case is a single-night operation ending a key-figure's arc instantly, useful as a worst-case severity marker for a fully-realized Water-level state intelligence hit.
findings.parameter: Cadre age at assassination: 21; latency from informant placement to lethal action: effectively same-night.

findings.case: Ferdinand Petersen (alias 'Nosovitsky'), DoJ confidential employee tasked against Soviet Bureau contacts, Sept-Nov 1919
findings.evidence: Petersen's work, however, was not entirely satisfactory, in that he was obtaining no information of great value and he withdrew from the service in November 1919.
findings.implication: Even a well-placed informant with real access can have a short, low-yield tenure -- Intel Confidence gains from a single agent should decay/cap quickly rather than compound indefinitely; agents can be duds.
findings.parameter: Low-value informant tenure: ~3 months (Sept-Nov 1919)

findings.case: Four Clean-ups Movement, Mao on relying on the masses despite bad elements, 1965
findings.evidence: even though there may be a bad one in 28, you will still have 27 good people
findings.implication: An explicit tolerated-informant-density figure used to justify still trusting a mass base despite known infiltration risk. Gives EH a baseline 'noise floor' for informant/bad-actor fraction below which mass-line intelligence remains net-positive.
findings.parameter: ~3.6% (1-in-28) baseline tolerated bad-actor/informant density within an otherwise reliable mass base

findings.case: Frank Martinez, ATF informant who rose to Chairman of the Chicano Moratorium Committee
findings.evidence: while you were infiltrating the Brown Berets, at that point were you under instructions to create incidents? ... Yeah, that's true.
findings.implication: The informant did not merely observe — he captured the chairmanship of a mass organization while under orders to manufacture incidents, showing infiltration depth scales with an org's own success and reach, an inverse counterpart to Mass Receptivity.
