This should ground the design principle that Desert-state falsification can be MORE destructive than the underlying threat, and that only relational trust (not more surveillance) resolves it.
findings.parameter: Ratio of real infiltrators to purge victims: 7-8 actual vs. ~2,000 executed; duration of the self-destructive purge cycle: ~3 years (1932-1935).

findings.case: Mutual falsification of the Kronstadt rebellion's character by both sides
findings.evidence: Our enemies are deceiving you. They say that the Kronstadt uprising was organized by Mensheviks, SR's, spies of the Entente and tsarist generals... It is all a blatant lie.
findings.implication: Real class struggle produces symmetric, two-sided narrative falsification (Bolshevik press pinned 'White Guardism' on Kronstadt; Kronstadt accused the Bolsheviks of lying in turn) — supports making Desert-state falsification bidirectional between State and Revolution rather than a State-only mechanic.

findings.case: National township-quality baseline cited at the Liang Shu-ming session, 1953
findings.evidence: What is the proportion of the good and fairly good townships in the country as a whole? Ninety per cent.
findings.implication: A dated national baseline for how much of a territory-network was 'sound' (Water/Mud) vs. 'backward'/infiltrated (Desert) at a specific historical moment — a candidate default split for how much of a map starts compromised before player action shifts it.
findings.parameter: 90% good/fairly-good townships vs. 10% backward (infiltrated) nationally, 1953.

findings.case: North Kalimantan People's Forces establishing liberated base areas against Indonesian/Malaysian 'mopping-up' campaigns, 1968
findings.evidence: they wiped out 73 enemy troops
findings.implication: Comparative color confirming the base-area/liberated-zone pattern generalizes outside China: League organizations 'mushroomed' specifically where peasant-mass support existed, and base areas were built by relying on that support rather than by military strength alone — reinforcing that M_r-building (relationship) precedes and enables territorial control, not the reverse.
findings.parameter: 73 enemy troops annihilated in the single reported base-area-defense engagement (1968) — minor scale reference only

findings.case: POUM 'No Pasaran!' 1936 -- broadcast appeal vs. relationship-built intelligence
findings.evidence: Workers of the world, you have obligations towards us. Every hour, every minute you must demonstrate calling for the most decisive support for the Spanish Revolution.
findings.implication: A mass appeal published in a newspaper is a public-baseline broadcast, not cadre-verified contact -- illustrates that I_c raised this way should cap at 'Mud' at best, since it lacks the local relationship verification that ESTABLISH_CONTACT_NETWORK/AGITATE_LOCALLY require to reach 'Water.'

findings.case: Pan-African transition from pacific to armed contested-zone action, post-1960
findings.evidence: In less than three years, from 1960, the armed form of struggle became a necessity of the African anti-colonial liberation movement.
findings.source: /media/user/data/old-hdd/old-hdd/prolewiki/Exports/Library/Library_Handbook of Revolutionary Warfare_ A Guide to the Armed Phase of the African Revolution.txt
findings.implication: Gives a concrete real-world timescale for a zone's escalation from contested/Mud to active Water-building armed struggle, usable as a tick-to-phase-transition tuning reference.
findings.parameter: ~3 years (1960-1963 decolonization wave) for contested-zone escalation to armed struggle.

findings.case: Peasant-association manhunts for fled local tyrants, Hunan 1927
findings.evidence: the peasantry, having many eyes and ears, can easily track them down
findings.implication: Intel Confidence in a Water-state territory is literalized here as a function of a large diffuse population of informal observers, not any scout/spy headcount — supports I_c scaling with cadre-presence x population rather than agent count.

findings.case: Profintern (RILU) International Conference on Strike Strategy, Strassburg, January 1929 -- pre-strike mass-investigation doctrine
findings.evidence: Leaders are required to possess complete information not only about the condition of a, given branch of industry, but also about what is going on among the masses.
findings.implication: Grounds SOCIAL_INVESTIGATION as a literal historical organizing practice: intel confidence must be built from ground-level sentiment reads, not industry/economic data alone -- a real-world statement of 'you cannot SCOUT your way to intelligence.'

findings.case: Profintern (RILU) strike-committee doctrine on bureaucrat vigilance, Strasbourg conference, Jan 1929
findings.evidence: The strike committee must be constantly alert to every move of the trade union bureaucrats. When they enter into machinations with the bosses, demonstrations should be organized
findings.implication: This is a formal doctrine for maintaining Intel Confidence against a specific corruption vector (reformist bureaucrats colluding with employers) — grounds an EH sub-mechanic where I_c can be locally undermined by a trusted intermediary defecting, not just by state surveillance.

findings.case: Profintern Strike Strategy Conference, Strasbourg, January 1929
findings.evidence: the revolutionary organizations must base themselves on elected strike committees and establish the closest cooperation with them
findings.implication: Official Comintern-adjacent doctrine explicitly ties trade-union intelligence/authority to standing relationships with elected local committees, not top-down proclamation — a real-world statement of 'you cannot SCOUT your way to intelligence,' since the whole document is about winning local trust away from rival reformist unions member by member.

findings.case: Profintern strike-committee representation density formula, 1929
findings.evidence: in small shops the strike committee may be elected on the basis of one for every 25 to 50 workers; in larger shops, one out of every 100 to 200
findings.implication: A concrete historical cadre-density ratio for relationship coverage of a mass base — usable to calibrate cadre-presence-per-population in the M_r formula (dense small-shop coverage vs. sparser mega-factory coverage of 200-300 delegates for 'tens of thousands').
findings.parameter: Representation ratio: 1:25-50 (small shop), 1:100-200 (large shop), 200-300 delegates total for factories employing 'tens of thousands'.

findings.case: Reorganization of the CPA's illegal Russian-language print shop after a rival faction physically seized it, c. 1922
findings.evidence: The Russian paper was organized in two weeks.
findings.implication: Propaganda/communication infrastructure (the concrete channel that carries mass-line intelligence back to the leadership) can be reconstituted very fast once cadre and equipment exist -- a much shorter recovery constant than full underground-apparatus rebuilding.
findings.parameter: Print-shop/propaganda-channel rebuild time: ~2 weeks

findings.case: Revolutionary Union cultural workers protected by masses at an unemployment-office action (mid-1970s)
findings.evidence: when the police came in looking for the 'guy with the bullhorn,' the workers in the office pulled these people into the lines with them, and denied that there had been a bullhorn.
findings.implication: A concrete real-world 'Water' vision-state case: once genuine relationship exists, the masses actively conceal cadre from state observation. This should be a mechanical trigger where high M_r produces mass-initiated data falsification protecting the Revolution, mirroring the Desert state's falsification against it.

findings.case: Russian Federation cadre base vs. the Jan. 2, 1920 raid dragnet, per M. Golos's 1940 oral history
findings.evidence: There were about 600 or 700 held at Ellis Island.
findings.implication: The Federation had 12,000 dues-paying members (per the same interview) against which the state's dragnet netted a few thousand arrests and 600-700 detentions -- a coarse, presence-based sweep that is wide but shallow, exactly the 'sees everywhere, understands nothing' State thesis.
findings.parameter: Cadre base 12,000 vs. raid detentions ~600-700 (a ~5-10% seizure rate of a named organization's membership)

findings.case: SPD capture of the November 1918 council movement as a pacification mechanism
findings.evidence: The council was the form chosen to liquidate the subversive movement, from the very moment of its appearance
findings.implication: High mass receptivity can be captured and redirected toward pacification by a rival organization operating through the very channel (councils) that generated it — Water-state visibility must be paired with independent cadre/organizational capacity, or an opposing faction converts the readout into inertia instead of action.

findings.case: Same 1902 piece — critique of the SRs' classless, all-things-to-all-people program
findings.evidence: Lacking a social basis and any links with a definite social class, it is endeavouring to cover up its inner impotence by the sweep of its emotion
findings.implication: A party without real cadre-presence in a specific class compensates with rhetorical breadth ('sweep of emotion') rather than earned intelligence — a warning pattern for the game: factions that skip ESTABLISH_CONTACT_NETWORK-type investment can look active (loud agitation) while I_c stays at public-baseline.

findings.case: Same chapter — mechanism of bourgeois deception of the masses on the war's class character
findings.evidence: The bourgeoisie deceives the people by working on their noble pride in the revolution and by pretending that the social and political character of the war, as far as Russia is concerned, underwent a change
findings.implication: Deception targets pride and apparent regime change (tsar to republic), not raw facts — the state doesn't need to hide events, only reframe their meaning. Reinforces that Desert-state falsification should distort interpretation/context around visible events, not merely blank out data.

findings.case: Same correspondence: Mao requires the accounting-unit policy to be validated by direct canvassing of four-level county cadre conferences before implementation
findings.evidence: In short, work must be done in accordance with the wishes of the masses. No matter what the method is, it will be workable only if it meets with the requirements of the masses.
findings.implication: Mass receptivity was verified empirically by direct bottom-up canvassing, never inferred from aggregate reports alone -- supports designing AGITATE_LOCALLY/SOCIAL_INVESTIGATION as literal queries against mass-level graph nodes rather than a formula applied to summary statistics.

findings.case: Self-reinforcing small controlled-organization bias flagged in the 1930 CPUSA language-work critique
findings.evidence: Work in small, Party controlled organizations, in which in some cases the Party members are the majority of those present at the meetings, develop a tendency of giving to these organizations almost the role of the Party.
findings.implication: A concrete historical description of the Mud vision-state's failure mode: cadre over-invest in easy, already-friendly small cells and mistake the resulting echo of their own voice for genuine mass sentiment. Mud readings drawn mostly from captured/controlled nodes should be biased optimistic, not merely noisy.

findings.case: Sinn Fein counter-intelligence operatives buying leaked Bureau files on British agents in the US, early 1921
findings.evidence: These individuals have in fact gone so far as to pay for certain information which has been given them.
findings.implication: A stateless liberation movement built relationship-based intelligence by penetrating a rival power's own bureaucracy -- ESTABLISH_CONTACT_NETWORK should be modeled as bidirectional: the masses/movement can raise their OWN Intel Confidence about the State, symmetric to the State's intelligence on them.

findings.case: Spartacist League's organizational weakness relative to the November 1918 council movement
findings.evidence: they had no one on the executive of the councils in Berlin
findings.implication: A vanguard can have wide diffuse sympathy (high raw M_r) yet zero institutional presence in the very bodies that channel that receptivity into decisions — cadre-presence (I_c's second term) is a distinct, independently-tracked variable from mass mood, not a derivative of it.
findings.parameter: KPD founded 29 December 1918 with 127 delegates / roughly 3,000 members at the exact moment ~10,000 councils and millions of workers were mobilized — an extreme cadre-to-mass-movement ratio.

findings.case: Stalin's total public silence on the rise of German fascism, September 1930 to early 1931
findings.evidence: nowhere would we be able to unearth a single reference to the German fascists
findings.implication: Intel confidence is not monotonic with organizational seniority: the top leadership's effective vision state was worse (total blank) than mid-level cadres who at least engaged with the new threat category, arguing against any design assumption that I_c automatically improves with rank/centrality.

findings.case: State-side battlefield disinformation during the siege of Kronstadt (March 1921, satirical bulletin)
findings.evidence: We've succeeded in duping a whole herd of soldiers to believe that Kronstadt is destroyed
findings.implication: Even framed as satire, the named mechanism is deliberate falsification of battlefield status fed to one's own rank-and-file to keep them compliant -- a clean narrative case for the Desert state's 'falsified, not just hidden' property.

findings.case: Sustained defense of the Hunan-Jiangxi border area (Jinggangshan), April-July 1928, against repeated numerically superior KMT expeditions
findings.evidence: with a force of less than four regiments we fought the enemy for four long months
findings.implication: Quantifies a real floor for Water-state survivability: a base area with strong local Party organization held against forces 2-4.5x its size for four months. Candidate lower bound for how much cadre-presence/organization can offset raw State reach in the Water vision-state.
findings.parameter: Held for 4 months against forces 2x to 4.5x larger (4 regiments vs 8-18), Hunan-Jiangxi border, 1928

findings.case: The 'Three Check-ups and Three Improvements' internal army-vetting campaign, 1948
findings.evidence: the soldiers should have the right, when necessary, to nominate those whom they trust from their own ranks for lower-level cadre posts
findings.implication: A grassroots-legitimacy mechanic where rank-and-file nominate trusted cadre from within their own ranks — models cadre-presence trust as bidirectionally earned rather than unilaterally assigned, relevant to how ESTABLISH_CONTACT_NETWORK should be scored.

findings.case: The Bolshevik Decree on Land, 2nd All-Russia Congress of Soviets, 26 October 1917
findings.evidence: compiled on the basis of 242 mandates from local Soviets of Peasants' Deputies
findings.implication: The regime's flagship policy document was assembled from a large, itemized corpus of aggregated local reports rather than authored top-down -- a concrete historical model for how a SOCIAL_INVESTIGATION/mass-line data-gathering mechanic should scale (many discrete local inputs -> one synthesized Water-state document).
findings.parameter: 242 discrete local mandates aggregated into a single Peasant Mandate (published Aug 19, 1917, adopted as law Oct 26, 1917) -- candidate scale for the number of local AGITATE/INVESTIGATE actions needed to synthesize a high-confidence regional intel picture.

findings.case: The Call worker-correspondent distribution network, 1972-1977
findings.evidence: It couldn't have been accomplished without the networks of factory workers, which each took responsibility for distributing hundreds of copies.
findings.implication: Concrete real-world description of relationship-based reach: mass penetration (into 'every major prison' and the biggest plants) was achieved only through embedded worker-correspondent networks, not organizational visibility — directly models Intel Confidence rising through ESTABLISH_CONTACT_NETWORK-type actions rather than passive presence.
findings.parameter: 'literally dozens of worker correspondents' writing weekly; each network node distributing 'hundreds of copies.'

findings.case: The Call's canonical statement of Mao's mass-line method, December 1976
findings.evidence: correct leadership is necessarily 'from the masses, to the masses,' This means: take the ideas of the masses (scattered and unsystematic ideas) and concentrate them
findings.implication: The historical mass-line cycle (draw from masses → concentrate → return to masses → test in action) is structurally the same loop that should raise Mass Receptivity/Intel Confidence — textual grounding for why AGITATE_LOCALLY/SOCIAL_INVESTIGATION/ESTABLISH_CONTACT_NETWORK (a two-way relational loop) raise M_r while passive propaganda does not.

findings.case: The Call's critique of 'propaganda only' sects (WVO/PRRWO/MLOC), December 1976
findings.evidence: communists can no more substitute themselves for the actions of the masses than the actions of the masses could by themselves take the place of communist consciousness and leadership.
findings.implication: Direct period-source backing for the design law 'you cannot SCOUT your way to intelligence' — pure vanguard/propaganda activity divorced from mass action is explicitly named as a historical dead end that fails to build real relationship.

findings.case: The RU informant's actual intelligence yield, self-assessed
findings.evidence: at the beginning I could probably do little more than go to RU collective meetings and report who was there
findings.implication: Even a live human informant starts at Mud-tier, not Water-tier — attendance lists, not relationship/motive data. I_c from a fresh informant should be modeled as a slow-ramping asset, not an instant-Water cheat code for the state.

findings.case: UCP National Office financial-statement handling discipline, Dec 1920 convention
findings.evidence: NOTE: Read at meeting, and then destroy.
findings.implication: The movement's own operational-security discipline -- oral-only, never-persisted sensitive data -- is the historical mechanism that kept internal financial/organizational intelligence out of state hands; models why some 'true state' data should have zero storage half-life in the engine.

findings.case: WVO general theory of mass initiative, 1978
findings.evidence: Communist leadership does not create mass initiative. Communist leadership can only promote, organize, and give full play to this initiative which already exists by making it conscious.
findings.implication: Direct primary-source grounding for the M_r thesis: intel/receptivity is not manufactured by cadre action, it is a pre-existing latent quantity that mass-line actions reveal and channel — supports modeling AGITATE/INVESTIGATE as catalysts of a hidden state rather than generators of a new one.

findings.case: Workers Viewpoint Organization, Chairman Mao memorial, NYC Chinatown, September 1976
findings.evidence: This sentiment can be illustrated by the over 2,000 people who turned out for the memorial uptown organized by the patriotic forces.
findings.implication: The Party's own bureaucratic caution (waiting for CC plenary guidance) blinded it to a mass sentiment a rival opportunist org read instantly — a textbook Desert-state failure: the Party had presence in Chinatown but no live relationship-read, so it missed a real-time receptivity spike its rivals capitalized on.
findings.parameter: Rival 'patriotic forces' memorial drew ~2,000 with full lead time; the Party's own catch-up memorial, organized on 4 days' notice once it finally listened, still drew 200+ — M_r can be re-tapped within days once genuine listening resumes, but only from a base already built.

findings.case: Chiang Kai-shek's Double Tenth speech vs. real anti-CCP deployment, 1944
findings.evidence: Chiang Kai-shek has already dispatched 775,000 troops who are now engaged exclusively in encircling or attacking the Eighth Route Army, the New Fourth Army
findings.implication: Nationalist rhetoric was deployed publicly while the same actor secretly negotiated with the actual foreign invader and militarily encircled the anti-imperialist left — a real template for state-apparatus deception dressed in patriotic language.
findings.parameter: 775,000 troops committed to anti-Communist encirclement under cover of anti-Japanese unity rhetoric.

findings.case: 1922 national railroad Shopmen's Strike, mass-solidarity surge, per William Z. Foster, Labor Herald, Oct. 1922
findings.evidence: Never in the history of American railroading was there such a splendid spirit of solidarity developed
findings.implication: Mass receptivity can spike very fast under direct strike agitation across an entire national workforce, independent of any cadre-network build-up — but Foster shows in the same piece that it collapsed the instant several union leaderships (Grable, Fitzgerald, Lee) betrayed the base, arguing for a distinct 'leadership-betrayal' decay event separate from ordinary per-tick decay.
findings.parameter: Strike duration ~3 months (July-Oct. 1922); fragmentation ratio 7 of 16 railroad unions struck (44%) while 9 scabbed.

findings.case: 1942 Shensi-Kansu-Ningsia Border Region under KMT economic blockade; material self-sufficiency built through production campaigns rather than relief
findings.evidence: The Kuomintang tried to strangle us by cutting off the funds due to us and imposing an economic blockade; we were indeed in dire straits. But we pulled through.
findings.implication: Shows the material-deprivation ('desperation') component of Mass Receptivity being met not by charity but by the Party organizing the base area's own production -- suggesting M_r's desperation factor should rise fastest where the Party itself visibly organizes relief/production, not merely where hardship is highest.

findings.case: 1978 San Francisco Bulk Mail Center wildcat strike, mobilization intensity over time
findings.evidence: For the first five days, there were over 100 workers on the picket lines, which the majority of workers at the facility honored
findings.implication: Concrete decay curve for organizational mobilization: full-strength participation held for about five days before the strike's effectiveness began to wane once state/employer reprisals (85+ fired) and lack of union backing compounded -- usable to calibrate a per-tick Mass Receptivity decay rate that is roughly flat for ~5 ticks then falls off sharply absent reinforcing mass-line actions.
findings.parameter: mobilization held at full strength ~5 days before measurable decay; 85+ workers fired as the repression cost that triggered the falloff; League had built the base over a prior 5-year history of postal work

findings.case: AWO/AWIU membership growth continuing through the Sept. 1917 national IWW raids
findings.evidence: The membership of the agricultural workers' organization more than doubled during the year 1917. This increase continued even after the great national raids on I.W.W. halls, September 5
findings.implication: Repression did not automatically crash mass receptivity — membership continued rising through a major nationwide infiltration/raid event before delayed effects set in ('greatly hampered... work of organization' in following months). M_r decay-on-repression should not be a flat, instantaneous universal penalty; prior relationship-capital buys a lag.
findings.parameter: Membership 'more than doubled' in calendar 1917 despite the Sept. 5, 1917 nationwide raid on IWW halls — a documented case of receptivity rising through, not immediately collapsing from, a major repression event.

findings.case: Agricultural-cooperative membership surge, 1955
findings.evidence: I put the number of peasant households in co-operatives at 16,900,000, but in the space of a few months that number has been exceeded by well over 50 million
findings.implication: A second real phase-change mass-mobilization curve, echoing the Hunan 1926-27 pattern — supports modeling M_r growth as bursty/threshold-triggered rather than smoothly exponential.
findings.parameter: ~16.9M to 70M+ peasant households (of 110M total, >60%) joined cooperatives in about 5 months (July-Dec 1955) — a second data point for a ~4x phase-change M_r growth multiplier.

findings.case: Army self-sufficiency policy ('production for its own support') in the Liberated Areas, 1945
findings.evidence: we are able to ease the tax burden on the people who are also in difficulties, thus winning their support
findings.implication: Direct material driver for the 'desperation' term in Mass Receptivity: a policy that lowers extraction from the population (army feeds itself instead of taxing peasants) raises M_r through material conditions, independent of ideological messaging.

findings.case: Batista regime fabricates Moncada casualty reports before Fidel Castro's court (Santiago de Cuba, 1953)
findings.evidence: I asked him how many of our men had been wounded. He answered: 'None. All of them were killed.'
findings.implication: The state's official casualty account (32 dead reported publicly, rising to 80+ within a week, zero wounded ever recorded) is internally coherent and format-correct — it 'looks normal' — while being wholly invented. This is the strongest available grounding for Desert (masked data that looks normal, not just hidden data).

findings.case: Black workers' distrust of Communism as a 'white' movement, per McKay 1922
findings.evidence: The blacks are hostile to Communism because they regard it as a 'white' working-class movement and they consider the white workers their greatest enemy
findings.implication: Low ideological-alignment doesn't read as neutral/absent -- it reads as active hostility that suppresses M_r below baseline. Receptivity can go negative relative to a 'no relationship' default, not just fail to rise.

findings.case: Bulgarian CP illegal survival and regrowth, 1923 suppression to 1933 trial
findings.evidence: The Communist Party there was prohibited after the insurrection of 1923, but has nevertheless continued to exist and to work.
findings.implication: Gives a real recovery timescale for a mass-line network after a state Desert-state shock: roughly a decade (1923-1933) for an illegal party to rebuild and exceed its pre-suppression strength — a candidate floor for M_r recovery rate after Rupture-triggered repression.
findings.parameter: ~10 years (1923 suppression to 1933) for an illegal party's mass base to exceed its pre-suppression strength

findings.case: CPA Australia membership collapse under Menzies' anti-Communist referendum campaign (1949–1952)
findings.evidence: Membership had fallen by half, from twelve to six thousand.
findings.implication: A sustained, multi-front state campaign (legal harassment, media war, banning referendum, economic repression) roughly halved a legal-but-embattled mass party's membership over about two to three years even where the ban itself failed.
findings.parameter: ~50% cadre attrition over ~2-3 years (1949-1952) under sustained but non-lethal state repression — candidate decay-rate ceiling for Mass Receptivity under hostile-but-legal conditions (contrast with faster rollup under outright illegality, see RGO/Blick finding below).

findings.case: CPA underground-security leaflet warning against complacency after the January 1920 Palmer Raids
findings.evidence: Do not beget a false sense of security because for some length of time no arrests are made and no raids; it may be the calm before the storm.
findings.implication: State Intel Confidence should accumulate silently and non-linearly (threshold collapse), independent of any visible Mass Receptivity decay curve on the Revolution side -- an apparent calm is itself evidence the mechanic must NOT expose to the player as safety.

findings.case: Castro's Moncada attack — action precedes/creates mass support rather than growing out of prior relationship-building
findings.evidence: our organizing, training, mobilizing and arming men under this repressive regime with its millions of dollars spent on espionage, bribery and information services
findings.implication: This is the explicit foco boundary-test the brief asked for: the 26th of July Movement built a small, secret, insulated cadre (not a mass base) and only afterward — via the planned five revolutionary laws to be broadcast post-capture — expected mass receptivity to follow the deed. If the mechanics permit any I_c gain from spectacular ACTION rather than mass-line contact, this is the historical case that would justify it, and should be flagged as the exception the design law is built to exclude.

findings.case: Claude McKay's 1922 diagnosis of Black mass receptivity to Communist propaganda in the U.S.
findings.evidence: Only the best and broadest minded Negro leaders who can combine Communist ideas with a deep sympathy for and understanding of the black man's grievances will reach the masses with revolutionary propaganda.
findings.implication: Mass Receptivity's 'ideological-alignment' factor is gated by whether cadre visibly share the mass's specific grievance-frame, not just class content; McKay notes such dual-fluent cadre were scarce ('There are few such leaders in America today') -- cadre-presence should be a hard multiplier on I_c, not a flat bonus, because the RIGHT cadre are rare.

findings.case: Cold War Iron Curtain as a bilateral zero-contact partition, described in 'Crisis of Stalinism' introduction
findings.evidence: the same Iron Curtain left the workers of Eastern Europe and the Soviet Union isolated from the support of workers in the West.
findings.implication: A hard geopolitical partition can act as a total M_r=0 cap independent of doctrine or cadre effort on either side — supporting a spatial/topological ceiling on Intel Confidence (a 'sealed border' modifier) distinct from the mass-receptivity formula's other terms.

findings.case: Comparative intelligence security in Red base areas vs. White (KMT-controlled) areas, Chinese Civil War
findings.evidence: In the White areas the mere leakage of a piece of information may cause defeat in a subsequent engagement, but in the Red areas such leakage is often not a very serious matter.
findings.implication: This is the single clearest primary-source statement of the EH thesis: identical information has wildly different strategic value depending on whether the surrounding population is bound to you by relationship (Water/high M_r) or not (Desert/low M_r) — the same leak is catastrophic in one and trivial in the other, exactly the intended asymmetry of the mechanic.

findings.case: Diminishing returns of 20 years of tsarist emergency law (1902)
findings.evidence: the population is gradually becoming inured to this potent medicine, and is ceasing to react to fresh injections of it
findings.implication: Contemporary evidence that raw repression has a saturation/decay curve rather than a constant suppressive multiplier on mass receptivity over long timescales — argues against modeling Repression as a flat linear penalty on M_r.
findings.parameter: ~20 years of continuous emergency-law application before contemporaries explicitly describe diminishing suppressive returns.

findings.case: Erosion of working-class membership share in the Polish United Workers' Party, 1949-1959
findings.evidence: Working-class membership in the Party fell from a high of 61% in 1949 to 45% in 1955.
findings.implication: A documented multi-year, double-digit-percentage erosion of an organization's proletarian composition (continuing 45%->40% by 1959) provides a real decay-rate anchor for how fast a mass base — and hence Mass Receptivity generation capacity — erodes absent active mass-line work, even without acute crisis.
findings.parameter: 61%->45% over 6 years (1949-1955), then 45%->40% by 1959 — roughly 2.7 points/year decline; candidate baseline decay-rate for Mass Receptivity/mass-base erosion absent renewed mass-line investment.

findings.case: Ex-PRRWO cadre account of Brooklyn College mass work, built then destroyed by internal purge, mid-1970s
findings.evidence: After years of work, the cadres and supporters of the organization won the respect of the masses of students and were seen by them as revolutionary leadership.
findings.implication: Real Water-state relationships (won leadership, hundreds of students mobilized) were destroyed in a single leadership decision ('bowing to spontaneity' purge) — models a sharp asymmetry: M_r accretes over years but can be annihilated in one tick by an internal doctrinal/organizational shock, not just external repression.
findings.parameter: Multi-year relationship-build (years to 'win the respect of the masses') destroyed by a single purge event — supports a fast collapse-rate parameter distinct from the slow build-rate for M_r.

findings.case: Ex-PRRWO cadre account of City College vs. Brooklyn College mass work, NY, mid-1970s
findings.evidence: At City College in New York, for instance, after six years of work on that campus, the PRRWO was isolated, with few contacts and little influence among the masses.
findings.implication: Two branches of the same org, same doctrine, produced opposite M_r outcomes purely from local practice (sectarian dogmatism vs. genuine mass-line work) — supports per-territory/per-cadre M_r rather than an org-wide scalar, and shows years of investment can still yield zero relationship if the mode of work is wrong.
findings.parameter: 6 years of dogmatic/sectarian work at City College yielded near-zero mass ties, contrasted with Brooklyn College (below) — same org, same time horizon, opposite receptivity outcome.

findings.case: FLN radio broadcasts and mass 're-invention' of news, Algerian War of Independence
findings.evidence: when they cannot hear what it says, why they invent what it says—to the greater glory of the revolution
findings.implication: Shows Mass Receptivity actively distorting incoming signal in the revolution's favor once trust is high enough - the inverse of state-side falsification, modelable as an M_r-scaled confidence bonus (or hallucination risk) on ambiguous/jammed information.

findings.case: FSLN compartmentalization discipline within the Frente, Leon, Nicaragua, early 1970s
findings.evidence: with our practice of compartmentalization, which meant that none of the companeros knew the details of the organization as a whole
findings.implication: Compartmentalization protects the network from rollup but caps any single cadre's true Intel Confidence - each only sees a fragment - so I_c should be bounded by an individual's compartment rather than the network's total reach.

findings.case: FSLN/FER mass-base growth in Leon and the Subtiava barrio, Nicaragua, 1969-1973
findings.evidence: FER had about one hundred members, most of them first-year students.
findings.implication: Anchors a multi-year Mass Receptivity ramp: a 4-5 person cell (1969) grew to ~100 organized members within a year (1970 CUUN win), then reached cross-class mass demonstrations by 1972-73 - roughly 3-4 years from Mud to Water via repeated small-scale contact work, not a single decisive event.
findings.parameter: Cadre base grew ~20-25x (4-5 -> ~100) within about a year; mass-demonstration capability reached after ~3-4 years total.

findings.case: Failed frontal agitation contrasted with successful patient agitation (Tasks of the Proletariat, Ch.5)
findings.evidence: the inexperienced agitator of the 'good old days' went simply and directly to the countryside -- and got a beating for his pains
findings.implication: Direct, unearned agitation (a SCOUT-style probe) actively backfires against un-primed masses -- literal historical grounding for the design law 'you cannot SCOUT your way to intelligence': blunt exposure without prior relationship-building provokes rejection, not receptivity gain.

findings.case: French colonial 'unveiling' campaign targeting Algerian women as the vector into society, 1930s onward
findings.evidence: If we want to destroy the structure of Algerian society, its capacity for resistance, we must first of all conquer the women
findings.implication: A real doctrine of targeted relationship-infiltration by the state: rather than blanket surveillance, the occupier explicitly picked the social vector it believed carried the most latent solidarity and spent decades contesting it - the state-side mirror of ESTABLISH_CONTACT_NETWORK.

findings.case: German November Revolution 1918 — nationwide spread from the Kiel mutiny
findings.evidence: Forty thousand sailors and dockers surged through the streets and a workers' and sailors' council took control of the town
findings.implication: A documented week-scale mass-receptivity phase transition: from a single naval mutiny to workers'/soldiers' councils controlling ten-plus major cities including the capital in under a week — this is the historical anchor for how fast M_r can spike from near-zero to Water-state under an acute crisis trigger.
findings.parameter: Revolution spread Kiel (3 Nov) -> Hamburg/Bremen/Luebeck (6 Nov) -> Dresden/Leipzig/Chemnitz/Magdeburg/Brunswick/Frankfurt/Cologne/Stuttgart/Nuremberg/Munich (7-8 Nov) -> Berlin (9 Nov): full-country receptivity spike in 6 days.

findings.case: German mass strikes 1916-1918, driven by war exhaustion and SPD betrayal
findings.evidence: over one million armament workers downed tools against the treaty of Brest-Litovsk, which the German regime had forced upon the Bolshevik government
findings.implication: Mass Receptivity did not rise smoothly — it jumped in discrete strike waves tied to specific triggers (Liebknecht's 1916 arrest → 50,000 strikers; Jan 1918 Brest-Litovsk anger → 1,000,000+ strikers), each larger than the last as desperation compounded. Model M_r rise as event-triggered step jumps layered on a slow baseline climb, not a smooth function of ticks.
findings.parameter: strike-wave escalation 1916→1918: ~50,000 (May 1916) → ~250,000 (Apr 1917, Berlin) → 1,000,000+ (Jan 1918, nationwide) workers

findings.case: Harriet Tubman's Combahee River spy network, 1863
findings.evidence: She became commander of her spy ring and developed a plan that led to the freedom of nearly eight hundred people.
findings.source: /media/user/data/old-hdd/old-hdd/prolewiki/Exports/Library/Library_A Black Women's History of the United States.txt
findings.implication: The clean positive mirror of the EH thesis: a built relationship-network, not battlefield presence, converts directly into a liberation outcome at a specific numeric scale — a strong benchmark for the payoff of a high-M_r/Water-state ESTABLISH_CONTACT_NETWORK chain.
findings.parameter: ~800 people liberated in a single relationship-built intelligence operation (1863)

findings.case: House Committee on Internal Security report naming Venceremos/Revolutionary Union leadership, 1972
findings.evidence: any organization that reaches a certain level of really serving the people gets smashed by the state
findings.implication: State surveillance visibility scaled WITH the depth of the org's mass ties, not in spite of them — the state 'sees' an org precisely because it is effective with the masses, while still failing to understand or predict it. Repression intensity could be modeled as M_r-linked rather than purely presence-linked.

findings.case: Hunan Peasant Movement class survey, Changsha County, 1927
findings.evidence: the poor peasants comprise 70 per cent, the middle peasants 20 per cent, and the landlords and the rich peasants 10 per cent of the population in the rural areas
findings.implication: M_r's class-factor term should be a discrete multi-class distribution rather than one scalar: poor peasants organized instantly and led, middle peasants waited, rich peasants resisted until coerced — a natural 3-tier class_factor lookup.
findings.parameter: Class population split 70% poor / 20% middle / 10% landlord-rich (Changsha County survey, 1927) as candidate default weights for class_factor tiers.

findings.case: Hunan peasant-association membership surge, Oct 1926-Jan 1927
findings.evidence: The membership of the associations jumped to two million and the masses directly under their leadership increased to ten million.
findings.implication: Mass receptivity stayed flat for 9 months of organizing then quintupled in ~4 months once a visible material condition (Northern Expedition victory removing fear of reprisal) was met — M_r growth should be regime-switching/threshold-triggered, not smooth.
findings.parameter: ~5x membership growth (300-400k to 2,000,000) in roughly 4 months once a threshold condition was crossed — candidate phase-change growth multiplier for M_r.

findings.case: IWW Agricultural Workers Organization No. 400, founding April 1915 to Fall 1916
findings.evidence: official reports made to the fall, 1916, general meeting of the A. W. O. No. 400 showed that the A. W. O. had initiated more than 8,000 members
findings.implication: From a single Kansas City branch with under $900 in quarterly receipts to over 8,000 initiated members in about 18 months, driven purely by field-delegate mass-line organizing with no repression yet in play — a clean baseline growth curve for Mass Receptivity from AGITATE_LOCALLY/SOCIAL_INVESTIGATION-type actions absent state suppression.
findings.parameter: 0 to 8,000+ members in ~18 months (April 1915-Fall 1916); first-quarter (Apr-Jul 1915) receipts $878 vs. expenses $789 from a single branch — candidate early mass-line growth-rate coefficient.

findings.case: July Days crackdown and its reversal (5 July - 1 September 1917)
findings.evidence: the mere mention of Lenin or the Bolsheviks is cause for arrest
findings.implication: A hard state crackdown (press destroyed, HQ ransacked, leader in hiding) can crush an organization's Intel Confidence to near-zero (Desert) in days, yet mass-line relationships rebuilt from below recovered majority support in roughly two months -- a concrete decay-then-recovery timescale pair.
findings.parameter: Desert-level suppression (July 5) to Soviet majority vote 279-115 with 126 local Soviets in support (Sept 1) ~= 8 weeks

findings.case: Kornilov affair -- agitators dissolve an elite loyalist division from within, 27-30 August 1917
findings.evidence: Revolutionary fevour spreads through the masses like a contagion.
findings.implication: Formal chain-of-command 'vision' (the state ordering troop movements) was blind to the actual relationship-built receptivity of the rank and file, which flipped an entire loyalist corps -- the State-sees-everywhere-but-understands-nothing thesis almost literally staged.
findings.parameter: elite loyalist corps (including the 'Savage Division') flips in ~3 days (27-30 August) once mass-line agitators reach it

findings.case: Kronstadt rebellion, garrison no-confidence resolution (Mar 1 1921) to requiem for battle dead reported in Izvestia #12 (Mar 14 1921)
findings.evidence: REQUIEM SERVICES WILL BE PERFORMED FOR THE FALLEN DEFENDERS OF THE APPROACHES TO KRONSTADT.
findings.implication: From the base's open declaration of lost faith in the party to armed clashes with fatalities is documented at 13 days across these two bulletins (Mar 1 -> Mar 14); the historical rebellion was crushed by the Red Army days after. This bounds how fast a Water-state relationship can invert to open Dissociation once the triggering grievance (hunger, repression) is acute.
findings.parameter: Dissociation-collapse window >= 13 days (Kronstadt, Mar 1-14 1921, from no-confidence resolution to documented casualties) — candidate floor for how fast M_r can crash from high to zero under acute material stress

findings.case: Lenin's Letter to Central Committee Members, October 24 (Nov 6) 1917 — insurrection timing decision
findings.evidence: everything now hangs by a thread; that we are confronted by problems which are not to be solved by conferences or congresses (even congresses of Soviets), but exclusively by peoples, by the masses
findings.implication: A textbook Water-state decision point: Lenin explicitly rejects deliberative/institutional confirmation (congresses) in favor of acting directly on a read of mass and garrison mood he already trusts completely. Models I_c crossing a decisive-action threshold where further verification is treated as pure cost, not information gain.

findings.case: Lenin's Letter to Central Committee Members, October 24 1917 — decay-of-opportunity argument
findings.evidence: History will not forgive revolutionaries for procrastinating when they could be victorious today (and they certainly will be victorious today), while they risk losing much tomorrow
findings.implication: High-confidence intelligence is explicitly treated as perishable — Lenin's whole argument is that the Water-state condition (mass/garrison readiness) will decay if not acted on within hours ("this very evening, this very night"). Supports a steep per-tick decay curve on I_c once it peaks, not a slow drift.
findings.parameter: Zero-tolerance decay: Lenin frames the window as hours ("this very evening, this very night") not days — candidate for a near-instant I_c decay constant once a peak Water-state is reached and not acted on

findings.case: Lenin's account of 'revolutionary defencism' as slow-changing mass ideology needing patient work (Tasks of the Proletariat, Ch.5, 1917)
findings.evidence: not a very rapid success, but one that will be real and enduring
findings.implication: Contrasts with the 24-hour garrison flips above: shifting a deeply-held ideological-alignment factor (patriotic defencism among the 'honest' masses) is explicitly slow -- supports distinct fast (crisis-triggered) and slow (ideology-baseline) components inside M_r rather than one uniform rate.

findings.case: Leuna works, March 1921 -- local cadre's accurate low-M_r read overridden by an isolated central directive
findings.evidence: isolated from their Berlin central committee, were unaware that the latter had supported the KPD's insurrectionary directives.
findings.implication: Local cadre held a more accurate receptivity read than the leadership; models a failure mode where cadre-presence intel exists at the node but is severed from command -- I_c should be tied to local cadre, not assumed to propagate instantly upward.

findings.case: Mao's 1957 call to 'have firm faith in the majority of the people' during the Hundred Flowers/Anti-Rightist transition
findings.evidence: We must have faith in the majority, and by that do we mean 51 per cent? No, we do not, we mean 90 to 98 per cent.
findings.implication: A quantified ceiling/floor for Mass Receptivity: 90-98% of a population is treated as reachable/recoverable mass base, leaving only a 2-10% irreducibly hostile fraction (named cases: Ting Ling, Feng Hsueh-feng) — usable as the design ceiling for M_r in a well-organized area and the floor for permanent informant/hostile fraction.
findings.parameter: 90-98% treated as reachable mass base; ~2-10% irreducibly hostile fraction, 1957

findings.case: Mao's April 1959 Seventh Plenum talk enumerating the disincentives that suppress upward dissent, months before the Lushan purge of the one man who spoke out anyway
findings.evidence: When people don't dare speak out it is for one of six reasons: fear of admonition, fear of demotion, fear of loss of prestige, fear of dismissal from the party, fear of execution, fear of divorce.
findings.implication: A concrete enumerated list of disincentives suppressing upward-flowing true information inside a hierarchy. Usable as named sub-penalties dragging down Intel Confidence when the informant is a Party/state insider rather than a mass-line contact -- I_c should discount insider testimony by a 'cost of truth-telling' term separate from raw cadre-presence.

findings.case: Mao's Two Talks on Mutual Aid, mass-sentiment stock-take, 1953
findings.evidence: the life of the peasants in general is good and is getting better; that is why 80 to 90 per cent of them feel happy and support the government
findings.implication: A rare instance of Mao giving an explicit population-level receptivity distribution rather than a qualitative claim, with a named dissenting minority (landlords/rich peasants). Usable as a real baseline for M_r's distribution shape across a class-differentiated population in a stable, successful base area.
findings.parameter: ~80-90% of population supportive, ~7% opposed (landlord/rich-peasant stratum), ~10% grain-deficient/needy households (1953 rural baseline)

findings.case: Mao's three-part stratification of the masses, quoted in The Call, 1976
findings.evidence: The masses in any given place are generally composed of three parts, the relatively active, the intermediate and the relatively backward.
findings.implication: Historical precedent for modeling Mass Receptivity per-territory as a 3-tier distribution (active/intermediate/backward) rather than a single scalar — cadre work should be understood as shifting population mass between tiers (backward→intermediate→active) in sequence.
findings.parameter: 3-tier receptivity population split (active/intermediate/backward) as a structural basis for M_r, instead of a uniform per-territory scalar.

findings.case: Mao, staged land-reform escalation in newly-liberated territory, May 1948
findings.evidence: After one, two or even three years... when conditions have become stable... we can enter the stage of land reform
findings.implication: Mao prescribes an explicit staged throttle keyed to territory-age/mass-awakening: cautious rent-reduction only, THEN full land redistribution once masses are organized and the war has moved away. This is near-literal tick-by-tick M_r calibration: gate the aggressive-action tier behind a multi-year receptivity ramp, not an instant unlock.
findings.parameter: 1-3 years from initial cautious mass-line policy to full land-redistribution unlock in newly-liberated territory

findings.case: Mass campaigns explicitly described (in the 'Sweep Away All Monsters' editorial) as able to compress the normally slow process of changing mass customs/habits
findings.evidence: the transformation of customs and habits can be accelerated if the masses are fully mobilized, the mass line is implemented and the transformation is made into a genuine mass movement.
findings.implication: Direct textual support for Mass Receptivity being a variable-rate quantity, not a fixed decay/growth constant -- mass-line ACTIONS (not passive presence) are named as the specific accelerant, matching the 'you cannot SCOUT your way to intelligence' design law.

findings.case: Mass defection from the Communist Party during the Kronstadt uprising, March 1921
findings.evidence: The Provisional Revolutionary Committe and the editors of Izvestiia are swamped by Communists' declarations of departure from the party.
findings.implication: Shows the governing party's own internal mass receptivity collapsing within days (uprising began March 1, this issue dated March 11) once legitimacy cracked — a real-world ceiling on how fast M_r/I_c can crash, useful for calibrating decay-rate maxima, not just growth-rate floors.
findings.parameter: Mass party-defection wave measured in ~10 days (Mar 1-11, 1921) — candidate upper bound for receptivity-collapse rate.

findings.case: Mass defection from the R.C.P. inside Kronstadt during the siege, March 1-10 1921
findings.evidence: Declarations of departure from the R.C.P. arrive unceasingly at the editorial offices, but in view of their great quantity and the insufficiency of space
findings.implication: Mass Receptivity toward an incumbent party can collapse in days, not seasons, once the relationship (not just the propaganda) is seen to break -- decay of I_c/M_r for a discredited actor should be steep, not gradual.
findings.parameter: Issue #8 alone lists >130 individually-named party-departure declarations accumulated within roughly 9 days of the uprising's start (March 1 to March 10, 1921) -- candidate upper bound for a 'receptivity collapse' decay-rate coefficient under acute legitimacy crisis.

findings.case: Municipal co-optation of the Panthers' Breakfast for Children Program, early 1970s
findings.evidence: Many municipalities, including Chicago, began to implement their own free breakfast programs, taking away one of the Panthers' primary sources for community outreach and support.
findings.implication: Mass Receptivity can decay through a rival actor substituting the same material benefit, not only through repression or fear — a distinct co-optation decay channel separate from per-tick decay or paranoia.

findings.case: Nat Turner's six-week concealment after the failed 1831 rebellion
findings.evidence: I concealed myself for six weeks, never leaving my hiding place but for a few minutes in the dead of night to get water which was very near
findings.implication: Demonstrates that under maximal state repression, mass receptivity collapses to near-zero not because the masses turned hostile but because contact itself becomes lethally risky — an 'M_r decay accelerant' that should scale with repression_intensity past a threshold, independent of underlying sympathy.
findings.parameter: 6 weeks of zero-contact concealment; captured roughly 2 months after the uprising began (Aug 21-Oct 30, 1831)

findings.case: PSL's Kensington (Philadelphia) tenant-organizing block party as a mass-line entry point
findings.evidence: The practical commitment to the well-being of people in the neighborhood demonstrated by the PSL's cadre earned the trust of the people, who invited us to intervene in this local issue.
findings.source: /media/user/data/old-hdd/old-hdd/prolewiki/Exports/Library/Library_Revolutionary Education_ Theory and Practice for Socialist Organizers.txt
findings.implication: Trust (and thus invited access/intelligence) is earned strictly through sustained, visible material service before any political ask is made — validating that AGITATE_LOCALLY/SOCIAL_INVESTIGATION-type actions should have a delay/accumulation curve (service now, receptivity payoff later) rather than an instant M_r bump.

findings.case: Polish United Workers' Party's Seventh Plenum admission after the June 1956 Poznan strikes
findings.evidence: our awareness of the actual situation, of actual moods in the country was insufficient and superficial.
findings.implication: A ruling party with total administrative and police presence explicitly diagnosed its own intelligence failure as a relationship deficit, not a coverage deficit — a primary-source articulation of the EH thesis from the state's own side.

findings.case: Polish government price-rise handling, Dec 1970 vs Jul 1980
findings.evidence: When the government introduced a new price increase on 1 July 1980 they did so extremely cautiously.
findings.implication: The state visibly modeled mass receptivity from a decade-old memory (the 1970 8-17% shock triggered immediate riots) and throttled its own action to stay under the eruption threshold — a real precedent for a state-side 'desperation gauge' that reacts to remembered M_r spikes rather than true relational intelligence.
findings.parameter: 1970: ~8% average / up to 17% (meat) price rise → immediate riot within 3 days. 1980: cautious phased rise → strikes still spread nationwide over ~1 month, wage demands escalating to 20% by early August.

findings.case: Profintern strike-timing doctrine -- Lodz as the model of correctly-timed action vs. premature declarations
findings.evidence: the experiences in Lodz, where the declaration was timely, must carefully be studied. The selection of an untimely moment may predetermine an unfavorable end to the conflict.
findings.implication: Direct historical analog to an M_r threshold gate: acting while receptivity is low (Desert/Mud) predetermines defeat regardless of decree; action-timing should be gated on measured receptivity, not calendar or leadership will.

findings.case: Rank-and-file R.C.P. members' resignation letter, Kronstadt, March 1921
findings.evidence: finding the party's tactics to be fundamentally incorrect, and that it is completely bureaucratized and absolutely separated from the masses, we are leaving its ranks
findings.implication: The stated cause of defection is explicitly relational ('separated from the masses'), not merely material -- confirms M_r should be driven by a relationship/contact term (cadre-presence proxy) independent of raw material conditions.

findings.case: Revolutionary defencism as manufactured mass consent, draft platform 'Tasks of the Proletariat,' 1917
findings.evidence: the masses yield in a different way from the leaders, and they extricate themselves differently, by a different course of development, by different means
findings.implication: Mass Receptivity should not simply mirror cadre/leadership ideological state — the masses' M_r curve follows its own slower, materially-driven trajectory, decoupled from what vanguard cadre already believe.

findings.case: SPD membership collapse during WWI, 1914-1917
findings.evidence: of its 1,000,000 members in 1914, only 200,000 remained in the SPD at the time of its September 1917 Congress
findings.implication: An 80% membership defection over roughly 3 years of war betrayal is a concrete historical ceiling on how fast a mass base can flip loyalty (and thus how fast M_r can migrate from one organization's relationship-network to another's). Useful as an upper bound for per-tick receptivity transfer rate under maximal desperation + betrayal conditions.
findings.parameter: defection fraction: 80% of SPD membership lost 1914→Sept 1917 (1,000,000 → 200,000 over ~3 years)

findings.case: Same RU informant episode — the RU's own social network (via Prof. H. Bruce Franklin) detected and neutralized the informant
findings.evidence: Duncan muttered, 'strange . . . very strange. Did he indicate how he found this out?'
findings.implication: The organization's real mass-line relationship (professor-student trust) served as counter-intelligence, catching the leak faster than the state's dossier-driven approach could adapt — real relationships decay slower and detect faster than surveillance apparatus.
findings.parameter: Informant recruitment-to-blown-cover cycle: about one week ('Student Retires After Week's Stint') — candidate upper bound for how fast a low-M_r infiltration attempt gets rolled up once it touches an actual cadre relationship.

findings.case: Spontaneous formation of workers'/soldiers' councils across Germany, November 1918
findings.evidence: Approximately 10,000 councils were established, electing leaders who were in their great majority members of the SPD
findings.implication: The receptivity spike was massive in absolute numbers (10,000 organs) but organizationally undirected — it defaulted to the pre-existing party (SPD) rather than the revolutionary vanguard, matching the design's warning that a Water-state reading does not equal revolutionary conversion without prior relationship-building.
findings.parameter: ~10,000 spontaneous councils formed within days of the trigger event — order-of-magnitude ceiling for an undirected, maximal M_r spike.

findings.case: The 'August Defeat' of 1928: an adventurist advance launched by misreading a temporarily stabilized White regime
findings.evidence: failing to realize that the period was one of temporary stability for the ruling classes, some comrades adopted a strategy suited to a period of political splits
findings.implication: A concrete case of losing legibility about the ENEMY's internal cohesion (not the masses) causing catastrophic defeat — suggests Intel Confidence should also track a second axis: assessed enemy-regime stability, whose misjudgment is as costly as low Mass Receptivity.

findings.case: The February Revolution garrison mutiny cascade, Petrograd, 27 Feb 1917
findings.evidence: By nightfall, 66,000 men of the Petrograd garrison -- a day ago ordered to fire on striking workers -- have now joined the striking workers, fully armed!
findings.implication: Mass Receptivity can flip an entire armed institution from state-loyal to insurgent-aligned inside about 24 hours once agitation and shared material grievance cross a threshold -- an upper bound for how fast M_r can spike, useful for calibrating the Water-state transition ceiling.
findings.parameter: 66,000 troops flip allegiance in under 24 hours (one day after firing on strikers)

findings.case: The premature agitator, 'Down with the Tsar!'
findings.evidence: the slogan "Down with the Tsar!" with which the inexperienced agitator of the "good old days" went simply and directly to the countryside—and got a beating for his pains
findings.implication: Concrete historical case of agitating an unverified/low-receptivity population — punished, not merely wasted. Supports a Desert-state action penalty (active backfire) beyond a simple wasted-resource cost when acting without a built relationship.

findings.case: The same editorial dating the ideological class struggle's slow-burn intensification from the 1949 liberation to its 1966 eruption
findings.evidence: the class struggle in the ideological field between the proletariat and the bourgeoisie has been very acute right through the 16 years since China's liberation
findings.implication: Gives a 16-year slow-accumulation timescale before a receptivity/tension variable reaches a threshold and erupts into an overt mass campaign -- a candidate long-horizon accumulation constant distinct from the much faster local M_r dynamics of routine criticism sessions.
findings.parameter: 16 years (1949-1966) as an accumulation timescale before eruption into overt struggle.

findings.case: Threshold handoff from open FER student organizing to the underground Frente network, Subtiava barrio, Leon
findings.evidence: The underground network of the Frente will take over from here.
findings.implication: A concrete Mass Receptivity threshold event: once open agitation raises a barrio's receptivity past a point, the organization graduates it to the protected underground network - matching the Mud-to-Water transition where cadre presence becomes durable rather than exposed.

findings.case: Trotsky's contemporaneous critique of Comintern over-optimism about the 1930 Reichstag election results
findings.evidence: The bureaucratic optimists do not want to reflect upon the meaning of the relationship of forces which is disclosed by the election statistics
findings.implication: A direct period statement of the mechanic's own thesis: identical public data (raw vote presence) must be read relationally (proportion of the class, comparative momentum) rather than at face value to get true intelligence — vindicates 'intelligence is not about presence but about relationship' almost verbatim.
findings.parameter: KPD vote 3.0M (1928) -> 4.6M (1930), +53%, versus Nazi vote 800K -> 6.4M (1930), +700%, under the same crisis conditions — comparative receptivity growth-rate benchmark for two movements sharing identical material conditions.

findings.case: UCP membership recovery to comparable scale within ~11 months of the Palmer Raids' 5,000-6,000 arrests
findings.implication: The underground party's mass base rebuilt to a scale comparable to the Jan 1920 arrest wave within under a year, despite the state having just rolled up 5,000-6,000 members nationally -- suggesting mass relationships/receptivity recover on a roughly year-scale timescale even after a severe infiltration-driven rollup, not a multi-year one.
findings.parameter: 5,801 dues-paying members across 12 districts reported to the Dec 1920 Credentials Committee, vs. 5,000-6,000 arrested nationally 11 months earlier (Jan 1920).

findings.case: WITBD's spontaneity/consciousness continuum (1901)
findings.evidence: the "spontaneous element", in essence, represents nothing more nor less than. consciousness in an embryonic form
findings.implication: Grounds the M_r continuum theoretically: mass receptivity is not a binary switch but an embryonic-to-mature consciousness gradient, directly mapping onto the Desert/Mud/Water three-state model rather than a simple threshold.

findings.case: Four Clean-ups Movement, Mao's talk on work-team method, Jan 3 1965
findings.evidence: It took 40 days to study the documents, and why should they be studied so long? I think this amounts to some sort of scholasticism
findings.implication: Mao repeatedly attacks over-investing Theoretical Labor in document study at the expense of field mass-contact, arguing I_c rises from being IN the village, not from TL cycles. A direct opportunity-cost tension for the TL formula: excess TL allocation without cadre-presence time yields zero receptivity gain.
findings.parameter: 40 days actual doc-study observed vs Mao's proposed ~1 day study + up to 1 week discussion before going to the countryside (~5-6x overinvestment)

findings.case: PAIGC liberated-zone cadre-training pipeline (Guinea-Bissau, Cabral, 1965)
findings.evidence: Create schools and spread education in all liberated areas. Select young people between 14 and 20, those who have at least completed their fourth year, for further training.
findings.source: /media/user/data/old-hdd/old-hdd/prolewiki/Exports/Library/Library_Revolutionary Education_ Theory and Practice for Socialist Organizers.txt
findings.implication: A liberated/base area isn't just a military safe zone — Cabral mandated a concrete recruitment/education pipeline with an age band and a minimum prior-education gate. This supports giving base areas a cadre-generation function with tunable intake criteria, feeding both the Theoretical Labor pool and mass receptivity.
findings.parameter: Cadre-track recruitment age band: 14-20 years old; minimum prior schooling: 4th grade completed.

findings.case: Chicago Tribune's fabricated 'Michigan manhunt' story for William Z. Foster, Aug. 1922
findings.evidence: At the moment that the raiders were stated by the Tribune to have been closing in on an alleged Communist convention in Berrien County, Mich., Foster was in the Cook County courthouse in conference with Assistant State's Attorney Jonas.
findings.implication: A press outlet manufactured an entire pursuit narrative in coordination with police access (exclusive photographer, unnecessary 12-officer escort for an empty office) — a documented case of invented, not merely withheld, state-adjacent intelligence, matching Desert's 'masked data looks normal' design exactly.

findings.case: Erosion of the 'old' St. Petersburg Social-Democrats' influence by police arrests, 1895-97
findings.evidence: an increasing number of "old" revolutionaries were "torn away" by the gendarmes and increasing numbers of "young"
findings.implication: Lenin insists the trade-unionist ('Economist') drift inside Russian Social-Democracy wasn't won by better argument -- it happened because repression physically removed the theoretically-trained cadre. Cadre count should be a load-bearing input to both mass receptivity decay (EH) and to which doctrine drifts become available (DT), not a cosmetic stat.

findings.case: Hu Yaobang's 1981 speech on careerists and opportunists entering the CCP during its high-tide growth periods
findings.evidence: a few careerists and opportunists, too, wormed their way into the Party. This could hardly be avoided.
findings.implication: States plainly that rapid Party growth is itself the vulnerability window for infiltration — a growth-rate-linked infiltration risk (not a constant background rate) is the historically honest model for how agent-provocateur-style corruption should scale with recruitment speed in the engine.

findings.case: KAPD delegation, Third Comintern Congress, 1921 -- Comintern press falsifies a minority faction's public image to the rest of the movement
findings.evidence: For several weeks, the Congress participants have been given a completely false image of the KAPD, through articles which misrepresent our party.
findings.implication: Falsification of intelligence is bidirectional in real class struggle -- a revolutionary institution can Desert-mask a rival faction's true positions from its own membership, not only the bourgeois State; EH's masking logic and DT's minority-isolation dynamic are the same mechanism seen from two angles.

findings.case: O'Neal as agent provocateur inside BPP security apparatus
findings.evidence: he admitted he was aware of O'Neal's construction of an electric chair, his advocacy of violent and illegal acts, and his obtaining of weapons for the Panthers
findings.implication: The informant didn't just report — he became chief of security and actively pushed the most militaristic line, i.e. he nudged the organization's doctrine toward the Insurrectionist/Adventurism trap while simultaneously feeding the state a floor plan. Infiltration and doctrine-drift should be mechanically coupled: a hidden low-M_r cell is also a vector for false DT tag-vector pressure toward violence.

findings.case: PRRWO's internal 'Menshevik'-hunting purge, invoking CIA Operation Chaos as justification, NY, 1975-76
findings.evidence: they had been targetted for special attack by the bourgeoisie under Operation Chaos – a CIA program designed to disrupt the revolutionary movement in the U.S.
findings.implication: Real infiltration-paranoia was weaponized to justify a self-inflicted purge that did more damage to mass ties than any external agent could — models a failure mode where State-surveillance FEAR (not State surveillance itself) triggers the Revolution's own Desert-state collapse via internal witch-hunt.
findings.parameter: 'More than one-half of PRRWO's NY cadre were purged' during the campaign — a candidate ceiling for maximum single-episode network/I_c collapse fraction driven by an internal purge cascade.

findings.case: Post-hoc suspicion that PRRWO's own general secretary, Gloria Fontañez, was herself a state agent-provocateur, per ex-cadre pamphlet 'The Degeneration of PRRWO'
findings.evidence: Gloria Fontañez, the mastermind of the destruction of PRRWO, is clearly an agent-provocateur, as her work of sabotage has been at too high a level to have been planned by any one person
findings.implication: Victims of an internal collapse could never conclusively determine whether it was organic sectarian error or engineered infiltration -- the Epistemic Horizon design should preserve this irreducible ambiguity (a Trap's ultimate cause stays partly unknowable even after the fact) rather than always exposing a clean 'was infiltrated: yes/no' flag.

findings.case: The Minsaengdan Incident, Jiandao/Manchuria guerrilla bases, 1932-1936
findings.evidence: if the purge was stepped up, the guerrilla zone would collapse in a year or two
findings.source: /media/user/data/old-hdd/old-hdd/prolewiki/Exports/Library/Library_Reminscences With the Century_ The Anti-Japanese Revolution Volume 3.txt
findings.implication: The strongest EH/DT crossover case: Japanese intelligence manufactured a fifth-column label that the revolutionary organization then weaponized against its own core cadres via ultra-Leftist paranoia — a self-inflicted Desert-state collapse where falsification propagates INTO the movement, and low internal I_c confidence causes it to purge its own high-M_r nodes as an adventurist/sectarian trap.
findings.parameter: internal purge risked total guerrilla-base collapse within ~1-2 years if unchecked

findings.case: The first Rabocheye Dyelo issue seized before publication, St. Petersburg, 8 December 1895
findings.evidence: This issue was ready to go to press when it was seized by the gendarmes, on the night of December 8, 1895, in a raid on the house of one of the members
findings.implication: A named, dated case of a party's first major propaganda/theoretical-labor output being destroyed by a police raid before it ever reached the masses -- concrete precedent for a 'rollup' event that can zero out both accumulated Theoretical Labor investment and local mass-line intelligence in a single tick.
findings.parameter: Raid occurred within roughly weeks of the newspaper's completion (League of Struggle founded late 1895, seized night of Dec 8 1895) -- illustrates that infiltration-to-rollup latency for an early, undisciplined underground cell can be a matter of weeks, not years.

findings.case: U.S.-backed KMT scheme (per Mao, Dec 1948) to plant sympathetic-seeming persons inside the revolutionary camp to manufacture an 'opposition faction'
findings.evidence: scheming to use certain persons who have connections both with them and with the revolutionary camp, inciting and instigating these persons to work artfully, strive to infiltrate the revolutionary camp and form a so-called opposition faction within it.
findings.implication: Infiltration need not steal secrets -- an agent provocateur can manufacture an entire internal faction to pull the doctrine tree toward capitulation, modeling how a State actor could seed a false Reformist/Liquidationist drift by planting doctrine-adjacent 'moderates' rather than spies.

findings.case: US 'mediation' (Marshall Mission) as cover for KMT rearmament, 1945-46
findings.evidence: Chiang Kai-shek had massed 80 per cent of his regular forces (which were about two million men) at the front for attacks on the Liberated Areas
findings.implication: A textbook Desert-state falsification: a public truce/mediation narrative concealed a real, quantifiable troop buildup — exactly the 'masked data looks normal' case the M_r<0.2 Desert state models.
findings.parameter: 80% of ~2,000,000 regular troops massed for attack, 540,000+ transported directly by US warships/planes — candidate magnitude for the falsification gap between public narrative and ground truth.

findings.case: CPUSA Language Bureau fraction coverage report, 1930 (16 language bureaus surveyed)
findings.evidence: The factions, directed by 16 bureaus and numbering almost 5,000 Party members, control organizations having about 50,000 members. About 800 Party members work among 140,000 workers in organizations in which we have influence.
findings.implication: Real, tabulated cadre-to-mass leverage ratios: about 1 cadre per 10 masses in fully controlled orgs vs. 1 per 175 in merely influenced ones -- concrete candidate coefficients for how cadre-presence should scale Intel Confidence at different levels of organizational control.
findings.parameter: Cadre:mass ratio, controlled orgs ~= 1:10 (5,000 cadre / 50,000 members); influenced orgs ~= 1:175 (800 cadre / 140,000 workers)

findings.case: Dimitrov's Comintern doctrine of mass work vs. adventurism (Reichstag Fire Trial defense, 1933)
findings.evidence: Mass work, mass activity, mass opposition and the united front—no adventurism—these are the elements of Communist tactics.
findings.implication: A primary-source formula tying EH's mass-line-intelligence thesis and DT's anti-adventurism trap together: legitimate intelligence and legitimate tactics both derive from sustained mass work, never from isolated conspiratorial action.

findings.case: IWW General Defense Committee 'Amnesty by Christmas' fundraising escalation, 1918-1923
findings.evidence: While the Wichita and Sacramento cases were going on another famous document, 'With Drops of Blood,' was published. The first month's returns from this were over $22,000.
findings.implication: A single well-targeted mass-appeal pamphlet more than doubled the prior campaign's monthly defense-fund receipts — mass receptivity/solidarity can spike sharply off one narrative/agitation artifact, giving a quantified multiplier candidate for AGITATE_LOCALLY-type actions' short-term resource or I_c yield.
findings.parameter: First-year baseline ~$7,000 raised total (1918); a single 'In Memoriam' letter -> ~$9,000 in one month; a single 'With Drops of Blood' pamphlet -> $22,000+ in its first month (~2.4x jump over the prior document) — candidate agitation-yield multipliers.

findings.case: Lenin's illegal-nuclei/legal-strongpoints formula, 1912 Pravda article (cited 1980)
findings.evidence: To build the party means strengthening and increasing the number of illegal nuclei, surrounding them by a network of legal strong-points.
findings.implication: The historical origin-formula for the Water-state architecture: a hidden, cadre-protected core surrounded by a permeable, visible mass-contact surface. Also the organizational template the Doctrine Tree's 'Insurrectionist' trunk should validate structurally (illegality as security, legality as reach) rather than treating them as mutually exclusive.

findings.case: Mao's 1942 Yan'an Rectification Movement resolved by the Seventh CCP Congress, China, 1942-1945
findings.evidence: starting from the desire for unity, resolving contradictions through criticism or struggle, and arriving at a new unity on a new basis
findings.source: /media/user/data/old-hdd/old-hdd/prolewiki/Exports/Library/Library_On the Correct Handling of Contradictions Among the People.txt
findings.implication: Models the Party Congress correction mechanism (rectification/self-criticism) as a multi-year process that concludes at a named congress, not an instant fix; also grounds mass-line intelligence as trust rebuilt through persuasion rather than surveillance.
findings.parameter: ~3-year correction cycle: 1942 rectification campaign begins -> unity ratified at the Seventh National Congress, 1945.

findings.case: The Call's 1973 polemic against the Communist League's self-declared founding Congress
findings.evidence: In its 'Call,' C.L. presents a neat formula for party-building — call a congress and proclaim yourself the party!
findings.implication: A Congress mechanic invoked without corresponding mass-line investment (no prior AGITATE_LOCALLY/SOCIAL_INVESTIGATION base) was itself flagged historically as an illegitimate 'sham' -- the game could penalize Congress actions taken with low accumulated Mass Receptivity as low-legitimacy/high-backfire-risk rather than freely available.

findings.case: Workers Viewpoint Organization's stated rationale for working inside the African Liberation Support Committee (1976)
findings.evidence: Obviously, Workers Viewpoint sees ALSC as little more than a game preserve where 'advanced workers and elements' can be lured, trapped and 'trained.'
findings.implication: This is the historical anti-pattern the Epistemic Horizon thesis warns against: entering a mass space purely to extract cadre, not to build reciprocal relationship. Design confirmation that ESTABLISH_CONTACT_NETWORK-type actions should only raise Intel Confidence when they build durable ties, not when they merely harvest 'advanced' individuals.

findings.case: Browder's 'national unity' framing as the rhetorical vehicle for the 1944 Liquidationism dissolution
findings.evidence: in the interests of national unity and to enable the Communists to function most effectively in the changed political conditions and to make still greater contributions toward winning the war.
findings.implication: A nationalist/patriotic-unity framing (not overtly fascist, but the same cross-class-unity logic) was the exact vehicle that dissolved an independent class organization -- a mild real ancestor-pattern for how nationalist drift erodes CLASS_ANALYSIS, useful for calibrating the PatSoc pipeline's earliest, least-alarming nodes.

findings.case: Sino-Soviet split, CPC reply letter, August 1964
