findings.evidence: you have long since thrown these things in common overboard and are proceeding further and further down the road of revisionism
findings.implication: A fully documented, multi-year international two-line struggle (1957 Declaration to 1960 Statement to 1963-64 open polemics) with named factional headcounts, showing the accusation structure (revisionism/nationalism drift) the DT PatSoc pipeline models domestically also operates at inter-Party scale.
findings.parameter: 26 Parties designated for the 1964 drafting committee vs. 81 Parties present at the 1960 meeting — candidate scale for fractional defection from an international doctrine bloc.

findings.case: 1938 dissolution of the Communist Party of Poland on the Comintern's unproven infiltration accusation
findings.evidence: The claim was made, but never substantiated, that the Party leadership had been infiltrated by police agents.
findings.implication: An unverified infiltration panic triggered total organizational self-destruction. For the EH/DT design this is the ultimate catastrophic failure mode: a false-positive belief in infiltration can be as lethal as real infiltration, so a paranoia/witch-hunt cost function belongs alongside the Intel Confidence and trap mechanics.
findings.parameter: 30 of 37 members/candidates of the 1932 Central Committee were shot or imprisoned following the false-infiltration panic — magnitude anchor for a catastrophic paranoia-driven purge event.

findings.case: April Theses ratified as Party doctrine within about two weeks of Lenin's return (April 1917)
findings.evidence: The pamphlet was typed in several copies and handed out to Party members before and during the Conference so that it did its job in part.
findings.implication: A doctrine document written in direct response to a just-measured spike in mass receptivity (February's upheaval, read by returning emigrants) was drafted, circulated, and substantially adopted as official Party resolutions within about two weeks -- the canonical case of the doctrine tree responding near-instantly to a receptivity spike.
findings.parameter: draft-to-ratified-resolution turnaround ~= 2 weeks (pamphlet written April 10; Party Conference April 24-29, 1917)

findings.case: Mutual membership-inflation between the Communist Party of America and Communist Labor Party reporting to Moscow, 1920-21
findings.evidence: Both parties freely indulged in "padding" membership and falsifying records, particularly when reports were made to Moscow or conferences were held to effect unity.
findings.implication: Falsified data that 'looks normal' (the Desert state) is not only a State weapon against the Revolution -- factions falsify their OWN numbers upward to their patron (Moscow/Theoretical Labor coherence). The Desert mechanic should also apply to a faction's self-report to its own doctrine/TL ledger, not only to enemy surveillance of the masses.

findings.case: Nkrumah's three-zone doctrine for the armed phase of the African revolution
findings.evidence: 1. Liberated areas 2. Zones under enemy control 3. Contested zones (i.e. hot points).
findings.source: /media/user/data/old-hdd/old-hdd/prolewiki/Exports/Library/Library_Handbook of Revolutionary Warfare_ A Guide to the Armed Phase of the African Revolution.txt
findings.implication: A real doctrinal precedent for exactly three graded zones of revolutionary control, keyed explicitly to "the level of the people's awareness and participation" rather than force ratio — directly validates a discrete three-state Desert/Mud/Water design over a continuous-only model.
findings.parameter: CONCP (1961 united front of Portuguese-colony liberation movements) linked "12,400,000 inhabitants over an area of some 2 million square kilometres"; Algeria's armed liberation struggle lasted seven years — candidate base-area scale/duration references.

findings.case: 1907 St. Petersburg SD organisation referendum on Cadet electoral agreements
findings.evidence: the success of mass actions requires the conscious and voluntary participation of every individual worker
findings.implication: Documents a real proto-'social investigation' mechanic: a democratic party of ~6,000 members required direct canvassing of every member (not just delegate voting) before any mass action — usable to calibrate the cadre-count-vs-party-size ratio and the delegate-density constant in the Theoretical Labor formula.
findings.parameter: ~6,000 members; 1 delegate per 50 members (raised to 1 per 75 for disputed credentials); 3,881 total members cast referendum votes (2,148 vs 1,733) — candidate base-rate for mass-consultation action cost.

findings.case: CPA (Australia) 1949 Coal Strike collapse — rank-and-file reversal after bureaucratic information suppression
findings.evidence: The recommendation was rejected by 6974 to 2378, and every district voted for a return to work except Wonthaggi
findings.implication: Concrete numbers for how fast mass receptivity/solidarity can invert once cadre are seen to be withholding information from the base — the vote flipped from overwhelming support to overwhelming rejection within about two months, a real-world decay-rate for I_c/M_r once cadre-mass trust is broken.
findings.parameter: Strike authorized 16 June 1949 by 7,995 to 882 (90% for); by ~August the continuation vote reversed to 6,974 against to 2,378 for — a ~2-month collapse window and a measurable defection fraction (roughly 75% turning against continuation).

findings.case: CPUSA Organizer's Manual (1935) on neighborhood-paper mass reach as cadre-labor output
findings.evidence: If each neighborhood paper would be read by only 200 or 300 people we would have close to a million workers closely connected with the Party.
findings.implication: Explicit theoretical-labor ROI math from the source material itself: a modest 200-300 reader circulation per local paper, multiplied across many units, was calculated to yield near-million-worker relationship coverage -- a concrete circulation-to-M_r conversion figure to calibrate the AGITATE_LOCALLY payoff curve.
findings.parameter: Circulation-to-reach multiplier: 200-300 readers per local paper unit -> ~1,000,000 aggregate mass contact

findings.case: James P. Cannon's 1924 call for CPUSA-wide 'industrial registration' of the membership
findings.evidence: the Party must know who its members are, where they work, what organizations they belong to, etc. Without this information, the Party is stumbling in the dark.
findings.implication: A real Comintern-era articulation of 'no investigation, no right to speak' applied inward: the party needed its own Intel-Confidence-like self-knowledge before it could correctly direct Theoretical Labor or tactics. Model an internal-registration Theoretical Labor draw as a prerequisite unlock for other DT nodes, not just external mass receptivity.
findings.parameter: Registration ordered to begin Aug. 1, 1924 and be completed "before the end of the month" -- a 1-month nationwide cadre-time cost for a single data-gathering campaign.

findings.case: LPR's newspaper Resistencia vs. the Iskra agitation model, per ATM's 1977 polemic
findings.evidence: we have to look at the content of it and in this case what we have is a periodical which in the last year averaged ONE PROPAGANDA ARTICLE PER WEEK
findings.implication: A press cadence built around theory-to-the-advanced (propaganda) rather than agitation-to-the-masses produced a documented 7-year sectarian plateau — supports a coefficient where AGITATE_LOCALLY-type output must clear a cadence floor to move Mass Receptivity, mere propaganda volume does not.
findings.parameter: Historical Iskra-model orgs required frequent (weekly+) agitational output; ~1 propaganda article/week correlates with a 7-year stagnation-as-small-sect outcome (LPR case) — candidate cadence threshold for M_r growth.

findings.case: Peking Review, 'Put Mao Tse-tung's Thought in the Forefront,' June 1966
findings.evidence: How are we to see through representatives of the bourgeoisie who have wormed their way into the Party? We must rely on Mao Tse-tung's thought
findings.implication: The explicit historical fusion point of both mechanics: doctrinal study (a Theoretical Labor output) is presented as the mechanism for detecting infiltrators (EH counter-intelligence) — a DT node's purity tag should plausibly feed the EH infiltration-detection roll rather than remain siloed.

findings.case: Same BWC two-line struggle, documented output over its active period, Feb-Nov 1974
findings.evidence: During this eight month period approximately 15 internal documents came out that speak to the line of the organization
findings.implication: A concrete TL-output figure for an active internal line-struggle: ~15 internal documents plus 3 pamphlets and 4 newspapers in 8 months — usable to calibrate how much 'Theoretical Labor' a live two-line struggle consumes per unit time.
findings.parameter: 8-month two-line struggle produced ~15 internal docs + 3 pamphlets + 4 newspaper issues.

findings.case: Solidarity cell circulating Lenin's What Is to Be Done?, late 1980
findings.evidence: one of the Solidarity centres circulated copies if Lenin's What Is to Be Done?, with the aim of emphasising the need to transform the trade union consciousness of the movement to political consciousness
findings.implication: A canonical theoretical-labor text (~80 years old) was deployed as a live tool to convert a purely economist mass movement into a politically-conscious one — grounds Theoretical Labor as literally raising the CLASS_ANALYSIS component of Mass Receptivity's ideological-alignment factor, not an abstract stat.

findings.case: 'Economism' counter-attack against Shanghai rebels, Jan 1967
findings.evidence: They are attempting vainly to use material incentives to corrupt the revolutionary will of the masses
findings.implication: Named 'economism' is a deliberate deception tactic by entrenched cadres to redirect mass struggle into economic demands, functioning simultaneously as (a) a state-side disinformation play degrading the Revolution's read on genuine mass sentiment (EH), and (b) a documented deviation/trap trigger — material-incentive capture — that a doctrine tree could gate as a corrupting node.

findings.case: 1978 CPUSA/ML split by an 'infiltrator faction' (Thrasher/Suber), nine months after founding
findings.evidence: Only nine months after the founding of the CPUSA/ML in December 1978, a split was engineered in our Party by a faction of infiltrators.
findings.implication: A concrete fragility window: a newly-founded party/org node was destabilized by an internal ideological-sabotage faction within 9 months. Candidate minimum 'stability period' before a young org becomes vulnerable to infiltration-driven trunk splits.
findings.parameter: 9 months (Dec 1978 founding to the engineered split).

findings.case: C.E. Ruthenberg's cumulative prison record for founding/defending the Left Wing (obituary by Foster, 1927)
findings.evidence: He served 10 months in Canton, Ohio jail for anti-war activities, and two years in Sing Sing for his activities in the formation of the Left Wing in 1919.
findings.implication: A single trunk-founding leader accumulated concrete, escalating state-imposed prison costs purely for organizing/defending doctrine in court — grounds an external 'state tax' parameter on trunk-founding actions, distinct from and additive to internal trap-triggered costs.
findings.parameter: 10 months (Canton OH, anti-war) + 2 years (Sing Sing, Left Wing formation 1919) + a pending 5-10 year sentence (Bridgman/Michigan conviction) at time of death in 1927 — roughly 3+ years served and 5-10 years pending across an 8-year span (1919-1927) for one cadre.

findings.case: Contradictory Cultural Revolution field directives to Lin Piao/the PLA -- an explicit 'peaceful, not violent' struggle mandate (June 1966) followed within seven months by an order cancelling the PLA's declared 'non-interference' policy
findings.evidence: The so called 'non-interference' is untrue. [The PLA] has been involved for some time. I think on this matter you should issue new orders; the old ones should be cancelled.
findings.implication: Primary-source proof that 'no optimal path' is lived history: the top of the chain of command reversed standing orders within months, leaving field-level actors no stable doctrine to execute -- an argument for making doctrine directives genuinely stale/reversible mid-campaign rather than fixed once chosen.
findings.parameter: ~7-month window (June 1966 'peaceful struggle' directive to Jan 1967 order-cancellation) for a full policy-reversal cycle.

findings.case: József Pogány ('John Pepper') shot as an alleged 'Hungarian spy' in the 1937-38 Great Purge
findings.evidence: sucked into the whirlpool of official paranoia, anonymous denunciations, early morning arrests, and coercive interrogations remembered as the Ezhovshchina, or Great Purge of 1937-38.
findings.implication: A Desert-state failure mode generated from inside the movement itself: falsified, coerced 'intelligence' (forced confessions) executing a real senior cadre on a fabricated charge -- directly informs the Bureaucratic-degeneration trap's mechanically-triggered ending.

findings.case: KPD 'social fascism' doctrine blinds it to the NSDAP threat, 1929-1930
findings.evidence: the KPD paid scarcely any attention to the monster that was daily growing stronger and more bold within their midst
findings.implication: Doctrine doesn't just constrain action, it degrades perception itself: the KPD's own ideology (SPD as 'main enemy') functioned as a Desert-state mask over a real, growing threat, right up to the Nazi's 1930 election breakthrough. Model doctrine tags as able to actively corrupt I_c/vision-state, not just gate available actions — a doctrinal trap that also blinds is worse than one that merely restricts.

findings.case: KPD's 'Red Trade Union Opposition' (RGO) splitting policy vs actual mass base, Germany 1930-1931
findings.evidence: KPD membership had doubled to around 240 000 but the RGO's had meanwhile... managed to increase by precisely 914.
findings.implication: A textbook Adventurism-trap number: doctrinal purity (breaking from 'social-fascist' unions to found parallel 'red' ones) grew the vanguard party while its supposed mass organ stagnated almost completely — proof that Theoretical Labor invested in doctrinal purity does not automatically convert to Mass Receptivity, and can actively cap it.
findings.parameter: RGO net growth of 914 members over ~2 years (1930-1931) vs KPD near-doubling to 240,000 in the same window — a stark vanguard-growth-vs-mass-receptivity divergence ratio for tuning the Adventurism trunk's mechanical penalty.

findings.case: Kronstadt Provisional Revolutionary Committee's platform: 'Soviets without Communists' vs party dictatorship, March 1921
findings.evidence: In view of the fact that the present Soviets do not express the will of the workers and peasants, to immediately hold new elections to the Soviets by secret ballot
findings.implication: Kronstadt is a real historical instance of the Autonomist-trunk Dissociation trap: a base built genuine council organs, then broke entirely from the vanguard party while still claiming socialist legitimacy (new soviet elections, not counter-revolution). It is also the mechanically-triggered outcome of an EH rupture (M_r->0 with the party) that the party read as pure counter-revolution rather than lost relationship.

findings.case: Mao's antagonistic vs. non-antagonistic contradiction typology, 'On the Correct Handling of Contradictions Among the People' (1957)
findings.evidence: The contradictions between ourselves and the enemy are antagonistic contradictions. Within the ranks of the people, the contradictions among the working people are non-antagonistic
findings.implication: This is the direct theoretical ancestor of a binary trap-trigger vs. trap-defusable distinction: contradictions among the people (non-antagonistic) are meant to be resolved by 'unity-criticism-unity' (rectification, not collapse), while contradictions with the enemy (antagonistic) demand dictatorship/rupture — the engine's ContradictionField should gate which resolution path (Congress-fix vs. terminal trap) is even available by this same test.

findings.case: Mass defection cascade from the R.C.P. during the Kronstadt rebellion (March 1921)
findings.evidence: Declarations of departure from the R.C.P. arrive unceasingly at the editorial offices, but in view of their great quantity and the insufficiency of space, the editors are unable to publish them immediately
findings.implication: During an acute rupture/trap event defection is a flood large enough to overwhelm a newspaper's column space, not a trickle — supports modeling defection fraction as a fast self-reinforcing cascade once a trap ending triggers, rather than slow linear decay.
findings.parameter: single bulletin issue lists 22 named individual defections (nos. 210-231) as only a partial backlog of a 'great quantity'

findings.case: PRRWO's internal 'bourgeois plot' purge, 1976 -- torture-extracted false confessions used to justify destroying the organization
findings.evidence: Both psychological and physical torture were used on these cadres, and under these conditions, some of them broke and admitted to these phony charges
findings.implication: Fabricated 'intelligence' (coerced confessions) was indistinguishable from real intelligence to the cadres experiencing it in real time -- models the Desert vision-state where masked data looks normal, and shows how a Trap (here, Bureaucratic degeneration/ultra-leftism) can be triggered and legitimated by manufactured security threats rather than real ones.
findings.parameter: purge eliminated at least 3/4 of PRRWO's membership -- candidate severity coefficient for a fully-triggered organizational-collapse trap

findings.case: Rabochaya Mysl and 'Economism' as the historical Liquidationism/Reformist archetype (1897-1901)
findings.evidence: the spontaneous working-class movement is trade-unionism...and trade unionism means the ideological enslavement of the workers by the bourgeoisie
findings.implication: Establishes that absent active Theoretical Labor investment, a workers' movement does not stay neutral — it defaults/drifts toward the Reformist trunk's trap. This grounds the design law 'no optimal path': zero TL spend is itself a losing move, not a safe baseline.

findings.case: 'Sweep Away All Monsters' (Renmin Ribao editorial, June 1, 1966) -- the exact-dated editorial publicly launching the GPCR's mass mobilization, framing loss of focus on state power as the mechanism of ideological drift
findings.evidence: Forgetting about state power means forgetting about politics, forgetting about the basic theses of Marxism and switching to economism, anarchism and utopianism and becoming muddle-headed.
findings.implication: An exact calendar trigger (June 1, 1966) for a mass trap-cascade event, and a causal chain for the Autonomist trunk's Dissociation trap: losing sight of the material anchor (state power) is named as the mechanism producing drift into 'economism, anarchism, utopianism' -- usable almost verbatim as the trap's diagnostic description.

findings.case: Palmer Raids interrupting the CLP-CP unity process, Jan. 2/3, 1920
findings.evidence: Both parties were disrupted by the coordinated mass raids launched against them and forced to immediately move to a regime of secrecy to survive.
findings.implication: External state repression directly interrupted an in-progress doctrine-tree correction — repression events (EH mechanic) should be able to freeze or derail pending Party Congress resolutions (DT mechanic), a genuine cross-mechanic coupling.
findings.parameter: ~3 weeks between the Jan. 2/3, 1920 Palmer Raids and the next scheduled unity conference (Jan. 24, 1920) — candidate shock-to-resume timescale for interrupted Congress events.

findings.case: The Sixth RSDLP(b) Congress (July 26-Aug 3 1917) held while Lenin was underground
findings.evidence: Sixth Congress of the R.S.D.L.P.(b) occurs, representing 240,000 party members.
findings.implication: A full Party Congress ran and ratified strategic doctrine (peaceful revolution abandoned, democratic centralism adopted) even with the top leader in hiding — congress cadence should be decoupled from any single cadre's availability.
findings.parameter: party membership at 6th Congress = 240,000, up from ~24,000 on Jan 9 1917 (~10x growth in ~7 months)

findings.case: The Zunyi Conference, January 1935 (mid-Long March emergency Politburo meeting)
findings.evidence: It was a vital turning point in the history of the Party.
findings.implication: The correction mechanism was not a scheduled Congress but an emergency mid-retreat meeting triggered by near-destruction. Congress-as-correction should include an emergency/crisis-triggered variant distinct from cadence-based Party Congresses.

findings.case: Zinoviev/Stalin/Kamenev, 'Leninism or Trotskyism?' (1925) on permanent revolution vs. the peasant question
findings.evidence: if there had been no imperialist war and if the peasantry had not predominated in our country, then Trotskyism would have been right as opposed to Leninism
findings.implication: The CPUSA's own core anti-Trotsky doctrinal tract concedes a rival line's correctness is CONTINGENT on material/class composition, not universal — directly grounds CLASS_ANALYSIS as material-conditions-dependent: which trunk is 'correct' should depend on the graph's actual class distribution at the moment of choice, satisfying the no-optimal-path law by design.
